revision.comment: [[野見神社]] を [[野見神社 (高槻市)]] へ移動: 他にもあるため
revision.text: #REDIRECT [[野見神社 (高槻市)]]

'''野見神社'''（のみじんじゃ）という名前の[[神社]]は日本各地に存在する。そのほとんどは[[野見宿禰]]を祭神とするものである。
*[[野見神社 (豊田市野見山町)]]（愛知県豊田市野見山町） *[[野見神社 (豊田市榊野)]]（愛知県豊田市（旧東加茂郡旭町）榊野。野見宿禰ではなく[[天夷鳥命]]を祀る） *[[野見神社 (高槻市)]]（大阪府高槻市野見町） *[[上宮天満宮]]摂社 野身神社（大阪府高槻市天神町）
==関連項目== *[[野見宿禰神社]] *[[土師神社]] *[[相撲神社]]
[[Category:神社 (祭神・信仰別)|のみしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

==関連項目== *[[野見宿禰神社]] *[[土師神社]] *[[相撲神社]]

revision.text:
{{Shinto shrine |Name = '''Nomijinja''' (野見神社) |Image = [[File:Nomijinja01.jpg|300px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=18|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|frame-height=300|coord={{coord|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Takatsuki City|ja|高槻市}} Nomimachi 6-6 |Coordinates = {{wikicoordinates|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命}}
{{ill|Nomitatsune|ja|野見宿禰|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=野見宿禰命}} |Founding = Late 9th century |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small), former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Annual Festival = Second Monday of October }} '''Nomijinja''' (のみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Nomimachi, {{ill|Takatsuki City|ja|高槻市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} shrine, and its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == History == In the late 9th century, during the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} of {{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}} ({{ill|887|ja|887年}} – {{ill|897|ja|897年}}), an epidemic spread throughout the region. Following a divine oracle, {{ill|Gozutennō|ja|牛頭天王|lt=Gozutennō|lt_ja=ごずてんのう}} (identified with {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命}}) was enshrined, and as the epidemic subsided, a shrine building was constructed, establishing the Gozutennō Shrine. In the late 10th century, when {{ill|Takatsuki Castle|ja|高槻城}} was constructed, the shrine was venerated as a guardian of the castle.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/ |title=高槻城内守護 野見神社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} However, in the 16th century when {{ill|Takayama Ukon|ja|高山右近}} became the lord of Takatsuki Castle, his adherence to {{ill|Christianity|ja|キリスト教}} led him to destroy the shrine building within the castle and confiscate its estate. Later, in the 5th year of the {{ill|Genna|ja|元和}} era ({{ill|1619|ja|1619年}}), the lord of Takatsuki Castle, {{ill|Matsudaira Ienobu|ja|松平家信}}, rebuilt the shrine building.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e5%be%a1%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92-%e8%a1%8c%e4%ba%8b.html |title=野見神社 御由緒・年間行事 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} In {{ill|1868|ja|1868年}} (the first year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}}), due to the {{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}, the name of the enshrined deity was changed to {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命}}, and furthermore, {{ill|Nomitatsune|ja|野見宿禰|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=野見宿禰命}} was enshrined alongside, prompting the shrine to be renamed “Nomijinja”. Consequently, although it is counted as one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} shrines listed in the {{ill|Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, the publication “Historic Sites, Famous Landscapes, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture” asserts that the renaming to “Nomijinja”—by enshrining Nomitatsune to emulate a Shikinaisha—meant that its descendant shrine is the auxiliary shrine {{ill|Nomigami Shrine|ja|野身神社}} of {{ill|Kamigu Tenmangu|ja|上宮天満宮}} in Takatsuki City. In {{ill|1871|ja|1871年}} (the 4th year of Meiji), it was classified as a prefectural shrine ({{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}) as a Shikinaisha; however, in November of the following year, {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} (the 5th year of Meiji), it was demoted to a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}) for the reasons mentioned above. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities – {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命}}, {{ill|Nomitatsune|ja|野見宿禰|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=野見宿禰命}} == Shrine Grounds == === Shrine Buildings === * Main Hall – Rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Tenwa|ja|天和 (日本)|lt=Tenwa}} ({{ill|1682|ja|1682年}}). * Haiden – Renovated in {{ill|2003|ja|2003年}} (the 15th year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}). * Noh Stage * Shrine Office === Auxiliary Shrines === * {{ill|Nagai Shrine|ja|永井神社}} – Enshrined deity: the first lord of {{ill|Takatsuki Domain|ja|高槻藩}}, {{ill|Nagai Naotoki|ja|永井直清}}. Founded in the 5th year of the {{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政}} era ({{ill|1793|ja|1793年}}) by the 9th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進}}. Initially called “Naotoki Shrine”, it was referred to as “Naotoki Gongen” during the late Edo period. In the first year of the {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} era ({{ill|1848|ja|1848年}}), during the time of the 11th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naoteru|ja|永井直輝}}, the main hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Takatsuki City) was restored, and the haiden (also a designated tangible cultural property) along with the karamon (quadrangular gate, designated tangible cultural property) were established. The karamon was repaired in {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}} (the 22nd year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}). : Annual Festival: April 24{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e6%91%82%e6%9c%ab%e7%a4%be.html |title=野見神社 - 摂末社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} * {{ill|Kojima Shrine|ja|小島神社}} – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いちきしまひめのみこと}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=おほなむちのみこと}}, {{ill|Iwanagahime|ja|磐長姫命|lt=Iwanagahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いわながひめのみこと}}, and {{ill|Ameno Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=あめのこやねのみこと}}. Its founding date is unknown; the shrine building was newly constructed on October 1, {{ill|2021|ja|2021年}} (the 3rd year of {{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和}}). : Annual Festival: Second Sunday of October (Nomijinja Autumn Evening Festival) * Shishamyojin – In the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正}} the separate shrines of Fukujinja, Inari Shrine, Iwajinsha, and Sorei Shrine were merged into one. : Annual Festival: April 11 :: Fukujinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Shinatsuhikokami|ja|志那都比古神|lt=Shinatsuhikokami|lt_ja=しなつひこのかみ}}, {{ill|Shinatsuhime|ja|志那都姫神|lt=Shinatsuhime|lt_ja=しなつひめのかみ}}, {{ill|Homusubi|ja|火産霊|lt=Homusubi|lt_ja=ほむすび}}, {{ill|Kanayamahikokami|ja|金山比古神|lt=Kanayamahikokami|lt_ja=かなやまひこのかみ}}, {{ill|Kanayamahime|ja|金山姫神|lt=Kanayamahime|lt_ja=かなやまひめのかみ}}, {{ill|Haniyamahikokami|ja|埴山比古神|lt=Haniyamahikokami|lt_ja=はにやまひこのかみ}} (linked as {{ill|Haniyamas|ja|ハニヤス}} with display 埴山比古神), and {{ill|Mizuhanomenokami|ja|弥都波能売神|lt=Mizuhanomenokami|lt_ja=みづはのめのかみ}}. Founded in August of the 9th year of {{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政}} ({{ill|1797|ja|1797年}}) by {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進}}. :: Inari Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|宇迦之御魂神|lt=Ukano Mitama-no-Kami|lt_ja=うかのみたまのかみ}}. Founded in February of the 11th year of Kansei ({{ill|1799|ja|1799年}}) by Nagai Naotoshi. :: Iwajinsha – Enshrined deities: (i) Yachimata Hikonami, (ii) Yachimata Hime, and (iii) Kunado-no-Kami. Founded in May of the 4th year of {{ill|Genbun|ja|元文}} ({{ill|1739|ja|1739年}}) by {{ill|Nagai Ryunosuke|ja|永井流之助}}. :: Sorei Shrine – Enshrined are the successive chief priests, shrine priests, and parishioners who have devoted themselves to the shrine. Founded in {{ill|1878|ja|1878年}} (the 11th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}}). * {{ill|Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine|ja|高槻えびす神社}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu|ja|蛭子大神}}. It was established in {{ill|1956|ja|1956年}} ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 31) as a branch from {{ill|Nishinomiya Shrine|ja|西宮神社}}, and the current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1966|ja|1966年}} ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 41). : Takatsuki Ebisu Festival – January 10 (subdivided into the 9th: Evening Ebisu, 10th: Main Ebisu, 11th: Remaining Fortune) * {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}} – Enshrined are the war dead of Takatsuki who perished in the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|ja|西南戦争}}, {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, {{ill|Second Sino-Japanese War|ja|日中戦争}}, and the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争}}. : Annual Festival: Third Sunday of May Nomijinja02.jpg|Auxiliary Shrine Nagai Shrine Karamon Tessen.png|Nagai Shrine {{ill|Family crest|ja|家紋}} ({{ill|Takatsuki Domain|ja|高槻藩}} lord, {{ill|Nagai family|ja|永井氏}}, {{ill|Family crest|ja|家紋}}) == Festivals == * New Year’s Festival – January 1 * {{ill|Tondo Festival|ja|とんど}} – January 15 * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭}} – February 3 * Dedication {{ill|Crying Sumo|ja|泣き相撲}} – April 29 * Great Purification of Summer Crossing (Chinowa Ritual) – June 30 * Autumn Grand Festival – Second Monday of October * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} – November 23 * Year-End Great Purification – December 31 == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Takatsuki City === * Nagai Shrine buildings (main hall, haiden) with attached items: 4 ridge plaques and 1 Takatsuki Castle ema – Designated on June 14, {{ill|2005|ja|2005年}} (Heisei 17). * Nagai Shrine Karamon – Designated on June 14, 2005 (Heisei 17). * Seventeen items of materials related to the traditional Nagai Naotoki of Nagai Shrine – Deposited at {{ill|Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|ja|高槻市立しろあと歴史館}}. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Catholic Takatsuki Church|ja|カトリック高槻教会}} (adjacent) * Japan Kurarechian Association Headquarters Monastery * Maria Inmaculada Kindergarten * {{ill|Takatsuki Modern Theater|ja|高槻現代劇場}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|ja|高槻市立しろあと歴史館}} * The ruins of {{ill|Takatsuki Castle|ja|高槻城}}, {{ill|Castle Ruins Park|ja|城跡公園 (高槻市)}}, and the {{ill|Takatsuki City Historical Folk Museum|ja|高槻市立歴史民俗資料館}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.nomijinja.jp/ Nomijinja] (Official website) * {{Cite web|和書|year=2012 |url=http://www.city.takatsuki.osaka.jp/rekishi_kanko/rekishi/rekishikan/jidai/shaji/1327659158743.html |title=Nomijinja |work=インターネット歴史館 |publisher=Takatsuki City|accessdate=2014-12-13}} * {{Cite web|和書|year=2012 |url=http://www.city.takatsuki.osaka.jp/rekishi_kanko/rekishi/rekishikan/jidai/shaji/1327671298593.html |title=Revived "Nagai Shrine Karamon" |work=インターネット歴史館 |publisher=Takatsuki City|accessdate=2014-12-13}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Takatsuki City]] [[Category:History of Takatsuki City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha of Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Gion Shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Settsu Province]] {{shinto-stub}} [[category:completion tier 1]][[ja:野見神社]]

revision.text:
{{Shinto shrine |Name = '''Nomijinja''' (野見神社) |Image = [[File:Nomijinja01.jpg|300px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=18|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|frame-height=300|coord={{coord|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Takatsuki City|ja|高槻市}} Nomimachi 6-6 |Coordinates = {{wikicoordinates|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命}}
{{ill|Nomitatsune|ja|野見宿禰|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=野見宿禰命}} |Founding = Late 9th century |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small), former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Annual Festival = Second Monday of October }} '''Nomijinja''' (のみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Nomimachi, {{ill|Takatsuki City|ja|高槻市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} shrine, and its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == History == In the late 9th century, during the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} of {{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}} ({{ill|887|ja|887年}} – {{ill|897|ja|897年}}), an epidemic spread throughout the region. Following a divine oracle, {{ill|Gozutennō|ja|牛頭天王|lt=Gozutennō|lt_ja=ごずてんのう}} (identified with {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命}}) was enshrined, and as the epidemic subsided, a shrine building was constructed, establishing the Gozutennō Shrine. In the late 10th century, when {{ill|Takatsuki Castle|ja|高槻城}} was constructed, the shrine was venerated as a guardian of the castle.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/ |title=高槻城内守護 野見神社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} However, in the 16th century when {{ill|Takayama Ukon|ja|高山右近}} became the lord of Takatsuki Castle, his adherence to {{ill|Christianity|ja|キリスト教}} led him to destroy the shrine building within the castle and confiscate its estate. Later, in the 5th year of the {{ill|Genna|ja|元和}} era ({{ill|1619|ja|1619年}}), the lord of Takatsuki Castle, {{ill|Matsudaira Ienobu|ja|松平家信}}, rebuilt the shrine building.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e5%be%a1%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92-%e8%a1%8c%e4%ba%8b.html |title=野見神社 御由緒・年間行事 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} In {{ill|1868|ja|1868年}} (the first year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}}), due to the {{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}, the name of the enshrined deity was changed to {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命}}, and furthermore, {{ill|Nomitatsune|ja|野見宿禰|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=野見宿禰命}} was enshrined alongside, prompting the shrine to be renamed “Nomijinja”. Consequently, although it is counted as one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} shrines listed in the {{ill|Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, the publication “Historic Sites, Famous Landscapes, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture” asserts that the renaming to “Nomijinja”—by enshrining Nomitatsune to emulate a Shikinaisha—meant that its descendant shrine is the auxiliary shrine {{ill|Nomigami Shrine|ja|野身神社}} of {{ill|Kamigu Tenmangu|ja|上宮天満宮}} in Takatsuki City. In {{ill|1871|ja|1871年}} (the 4th year of Meiji), it was classified as a prefectural shrine ({{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}) as a Shikinaisha; however, in November of the following year, {{ill|1872|ja|1872年}} (the 5th year of Meiji), it was demoted to a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}) for the reasons mentioned above. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities – {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命}}, {{ill|Nomitatsune|ja|野見宿禰|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=野見宿禰命}} == Shrine Grounds == === Shrine Buildings === * Main Hall – Rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Tenwa|ja|天和 (日本)|lt=Tenwa}} ({{ill|1682|ja|1682年}}). * Haiden – Renovated in {{ill|2003|ja|2003年}} (the 15th year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}). * Noh Stage * Shrine Office === Auxiliary Shrines === * {{ill|Nagai Shrine|ja|永井神社}} – Enshrined deity: the first lord of {{ill|Takatsuki Domain|ja|高槻藩}}, {{ill|Nagai Naotoki|ja|永井直清}}. Founded in the 5th year of the {{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政}} era ({{ill|1793|ja|1793年}}) by the 9th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進}}. Initially called “Naotoki Shrine”, it was referred to as “Naotoki Gongen” during the late Edo period. In the first year of the {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} era ({{ill|1848|ja|1848年}}), during the time of the 11th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naoteru|ja|永井直輝}}, the main hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Takatsuki City) was restored, and the haiden (also a designated tangible cultural property) along with the karamon (quadrangular gate, designated tangible cultural property) were established. The karamon was repaired in {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}} (the 22nd year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}). : Annual Festival: April 24{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e6%91%82%e6%9c%ab%e7%a4%be.html |title=野見神社 - 摂末社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} * {{ill|Kojima Shrine|ja|小島神社}} – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いちきしまひめのみこと}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=おほなむちのみこと}}, {{ill|Iwanagahime|ja|磐長姫命|lt=Iwanagahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いわながひめのみこと}}, and {{ill|Ameno Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=あめのこやねのみこと}}. Its founding date is unknown; the shrine building was newly constructed on October 1, {{ill|2021|ja|2021年}} (the 3rd year of {{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和}}). : Annual Festival: Second Sunday of October (Nomijinja Autumn Evening Festival) * Shishamyojin – In the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正}} the separate shrines of Fukujinja, Inari Shrine, Iwajinsha, and Sorei Shrine were merged into one. : Annual Festival: April 11 :: Fukujinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Shinatsuhikokami|ja|志那都比古神|lt=Shinatsuhikokami|lt_ja=しなつひこのかみ}}, {{ill|Shinatsuhime|ja|志那都姫神|lt=Shinatsuhime|lt_ja=しなつひめのかみ}}, {{ill|Homusubi|ja|火産霊|lt=Homusubi|lt_ja=ほむすび}}, {{ill|Kanayamahikokami|ja|金山比古神|lt=Kanayamahikokami|lt_ja=かなやまひこのかみ}}, {{ill|Kanayamahime|ja|金山姫神|lt=Kanayamahime|lt_ja=かなやまひめのかみ}}, {{ill|Haniyamahikokami|ja|埴山比古神|lt=Haniyamahikokami|lt_ja=はにやまひこのかみ}} (linked as {{ill|Haniyamas|ja|ハニヤス}} with display 埴山比古神), and {{ill|Mizuhanomenokami|ja|弥都波能売神|lt=Mizuhanomenokami|lt_ja=みづはのめのかみ}}. Founded in August of the 9th year of {{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政}} ({{ill|1797|ja|1797年}}) by {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進}}. :: Inari Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|宇迦之御魂神|lt=Ukano Mitama-no-Kami|lt_ja=うかのみたまのかみ}}. Founded in February of the 11th year of Kansei ({{ill|1799|ja|1799年}}) by Nagai Naotoshi. :: Iwajinsha – Enshrined deities: (i) Yachimata Hikonami, (ii) Yachimata Hime, and (iii) Kunado-no-Kami. Founded in May of the 4th year of {{ill|Genbun|ja|元文}} ({{ill|1739|ja|1739年}}) by {{ill|Nagai Ryunosuke|ja|永井流之助}}. :: Sorei Shrine – Enshrined are the successive chief priests, shrine priests, and parishioners who have devoted themselves to the shrine. Founded in {{ill|1878|ja|1878年}} (the 11th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}}). * {{ill|Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine|ja|高槻えびす神社}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu|ja|蛭子大神}}. It was established in {{ill|1956|ja|1956年}} ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 31) as a branch from {{ill|Nishinomiya Shrine|ja|西宮神社}}, and the current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1966|ja|1966年}} ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 41). : Takatsuki Ebisu Festival – January 10 (subdivided into the 9th: Evening Ebisu, 10th: Main Ebisu, 11th: Remaining Fortune) * {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}} – Enshrined are the war dead of Takatsuki who perished in the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|ja|西南戦争}}, {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, {{ill|Second Sino-Japanese War|ja|日中戦争}}, and the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争}}. : Annual Festival: Third Sunday of May Nomijinja02.jpg|Auxiliary Shrine Nagai Shrine Karamon Tessen.png|Nagai Shrine {{ill|Family crest|ja|家紋}} ({{ill|Takatsuki Domain|ja|高槻藩}} lord, {{ill|Nagai family|ja|永井氏}}, {{ill|Family crest|ja|家紋}}) == Festivals == * New Year’s Festival – January 1 * {{ill|Tondo Festival|ja|とんど}} – January 15 * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭}} – February 3 * Dedication {{ill|Crying Sumo|ja|泣き相撲}} – April 29 * Great Purification of Summer Crossing (Chinowa Ritual) – June 30 * Autumn Grand Festival – Second Monday of October * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} – November 23 * Year-End Great Purification – December 31 == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Takatsuki City === * Nagai Shrine buildings (main hall, haiden) with attached items: 4 ridge plaques and 1 Takatsuki Castle ema – Designated on June 14, {{ill|2005|ja|2005年}} (Heisei 17). * Nagai Shrine Karamon – Designated on June 14, 2005 (Heisei 17). * Seventeen items of materials related to the traditional Nagai Naotoki of Nagai Shrine – Deposited at {{ill|Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|ja|高槻市立しろあと歴史館}}. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Catholic Takatsuki Church|ja|カトリック高槻教会}} (adjacent) * Japan Kurarechian Association Headquarters Monastery * Maria Inmaculada Kindergarten * {{ill|Takatsuki Modern Theater|ja|高槻現代劇場}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|ja|高槻市立しろあと歴史館}} * The ruins of {{ill|Takatsuki Castle|ja|高槻城}}, {{ill|Castle Ruins Park|ja|城跡公園 (高槻市)}}, and the {{ill|Takatsuki City Historical Folk Museum|ja|高槻市立歴史民俗資料館}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.nomijinja.jp/ Nomijinja] (Official website) * {{Cite web|和書|year=2012 |url=http://www.city.takatsuki.osaka.jp/rekishi_kanko/rekishi/rekishikan/jidai/shaji/1327659158743.html |title=Nomijinja |work=インターネット歴史館 |publisher=Takatsuki City|accessdate=2014-12-13}} * {{Cite web|和書|year=2012 |url=http://www.city.takatsuki.osaka.jp/rekishi_kanko/rekishi/rekishikan/jidai/shaji/1327671298593.html |title=Revived "Nagai Shrine Karamon" |work=インターネット歴史館 |publisher=Takatsuki City|accessdate=2014-12-13}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Takatsuki City]] [[Category:History of Takatsuki City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha of Osaka Prefecture]] {{Shinto shrine |Name = '''Nomijinja''' (野見神社) |Image = [[File:Nomijinja01.jpg|300px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=18|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|frame-height=300|coord={{coord|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Takatsuki City|ja|高槻市}} Nomimachi 6-6 |Coordinates = {{wikicoordinates|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命}}
{{ill|Nomitatsune|ja|野見宿禰|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=野見宿禰命}} |Founding = Late 9th century |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small), former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Annual Festival = Second Monday of October }} '''Nomijinja''' (のみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Nomimachi, {{ill|Takatsuki City|ja|高槻市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} shrine, and its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == History == In the late 9th century, during the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} of {{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}} ({{ill|887|ja|887年}} – {{ill|897|ja|897年}}), an epidemic spread throughout the region. Following a divine oracle, {{ill|Gozutennō|ja|牛頭天王|lt=Gozutennō|lt_ja=ごずてんのう}} (identified with {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命}}) was enshrined, and as the epidemic subsided, a shrine building was constructed, establishing the Gozutennō Shrine. In the late 10th century, when {{ill|Takatsuki Castle|ja|高槻城}} was constructed, the shrine was venerated as a guardian of the castle.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/ |title=高槻城内守護 野見神社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} However, in the 16th century when {{ill|Takayama Ukon|ja|高山右近}} became the lord of Takatsuki Castle, his adherence to {{ill|Christianity|ja|キリスト教}} led him to destroy the shrine building within the castle and confiscate its estate. Later, in the 5th year of the {{ill|Genna|ja|元和}} era ({{ill|1619|ja|1619年}}), the lord of Takatsuki Castle, {{ill|Matsudaira Ienobu|ja|松平家信}}, rebuilt the shrine building.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e5%be%a1%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92-%e8%a1%8c%e4%ba%8b.html |title=野見神社 御由緒・年間行事 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} In {{ill|1868|ja|1868年}} (the first year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}}), due to the {{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}, the name of the enshrined deity was changed to {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命}}, and furthermore, {{ill|Nomitatsune|ja|野見宿禰|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=野見宿禰命}} was enshrined alongside, prompting the shrine to be renamed “Nomijinja”. Consequently, although it is counted as one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} shrines listed in the {{ill|Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, the publication “Historic Sites, Famous Landscapes, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture” asserts that the renaming to “Nomijinja”—by enshrining Nomitatsune to emulate a Shikinaisha—meant that its descendant shrine is the auxiliary shrine {{ill|Nomigami Shrine|ja|野身神社}} of {{ill|Kamigu Tenmangu|ja|上宮天満宮}} in Takatsuki City. In {{ill|1871|ja|1871st year }} (the 4th year of Meiji), it was classified as a prefectural shrine ({{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}) as a Shikinaisha; however, in November of the following year, {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year }} (the 5th year of Meiji), it was demoted to a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}) for the reasons mentioned above. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities – {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命}}, {{ill|Nomitatsune|ja|野見宿禰|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=野見宿禰命}} == Shrine Grounds == === Shrine Buildings === * Main Hall – Rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Tenwa|ja|天和 (日本)|lt=Tenwa}} ({{ill|1682|ja|1682nd year }}). * Haiden – Renovated in {{ill|2003|ja|2003年}} (the 15th year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}). * Noh Stage * Shrine Office === Auxiliary Shrines === * {{ill|Nagai Shrine|ja|永井神社}} – Enshrined deity: the first lord of {{ill|Takatsuki Domain|ja|高槻藩}}, {{ill|Nagai Naotoki|ja|永井直清}}. Founded in the 5th year of the {{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政}} era ({{ill|1793|ja|1793年}}) by the 9th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進}}. Initially called “Naotoki Shrine”, it was referred to as “Naotoki Gongen” during the late Edo period. In the first year of the {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} era ({{ill|1848|ja|1848年}}), during the time of the 11th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naoteru|ja|永井直輝}}, the main hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Takatsuki City) was restored, and the haiden (also a designated tangible cultural property) along with the karamon (quadrangular gate, designated tangible cultural property) were established. The karamon was repaired in {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}} (the 22nd year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}). : Annual Festival: April 24{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e6%91%82%e6%9c%ab%e7%a4%be.html |title=野見神社 - 摂末社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} * {{ill|Kojima Shrine|ja|小島神社}} – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いちきしまひめのみこと}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=おほなむちのみこと}}, {{ill|Iwanagahime|ja|磐長姫命|lt=Iwanagahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いわながひめのみこと}}, and {{ill|Ameno Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=あめのこやねのみこと}}. Its founding date is unknown; the shrine building was newly constructed on October 1, {{ill|2021|ja|2021st year }} (the 3rd year of {{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和}}). : Annual Festival: Second Sunday of October (Nomijinja Autumn Evening Festival) * Shishamyojin – In the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正}} the separate shrines of Fukujinja, Inari Shrine, Iwajinsha, and Sorei Shrine were merged into one. : Annual Festival: April 11 :: Fukujinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Shinatsuhikokami|ja|志那都比古神|lt=Shinatsuhikokami|lt_ja=しなつひこのかみ}}, {{ill|Shinatsuhime|ja|志那都姫神|lt=Shinatsuhime|lt_ja=しなつひめのかみ}}, {{ill|Homusubi|ja|火産霊|lt=Homusubi|lt_ja=ほむすび}}, {{ill|Kanayamahikokami|ja|金山比古神|lt=Kanayamahikokami|lt_ja=かなやまひこのかみ}}, {{ill|Kanayamahime|ja|金山姫神|lt=Kanayamahime|lt_ja=かなやまひめのかみ}}, {{ill|Haniyamahikokami|ja|埴山比古神|lt=Haniyamahikokami|lt_ja=はにやまひこのかみ}} (linked as {{ill|Haniyamas|ja|ハニヤス}} with display 埴山比古神), and {{ill|Mizuhanomenokami|ja|弥都波能売神|lt=Mizuhanomenokami|lt_ja=みづはのめのかみ}}. Founded in August of the 9th year of {{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政}} ({{ill|1797|ja|1797年}}) by {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進}}. :: Inari Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|宇迦之御魂神|lt=Ukano Mitama-no-Kami|lt_ja=うかのみたまのかみ}}. Founded in February of the 11th year of Kansei ({{ill|1799|ja|1799年}}) by Nagai Naotoshi. :: Iwajinsha – Enshrined deities: (i) Yachimata Hikonami, (ii) Yachimata Hime, and (iii) Kunado-no-Kami. Founded in May of the 4th year of {{ill|Genbun|ja|元文}} ({{ill|1739|ja|1739年}}) by {{ill|Nagai Ryunosuke|ja|永井流之助}}. :: Sorei Shrine – Enshrined are the successive chief priests, shrine priests, and parishioners who have devoted themselves to the shrine. Founded in {{ill|1878|ja|1878年}} (the 11th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}}). * {{ill|Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine|ja|高槻えびす神社}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu|ja|蛭子大神}}. It was established in {{ill|1956|ja|1956年}} ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 31) as a branch from {{ill|Nishinomiya Shrine|ja|西宮神社}}, and the current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1966|ja|1966年}} ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 41). : Takatsuki Ebisu Festival – January 10 (subdivided into the 9th: Evening Ebisu, 10th: Main Ebisu, 11th: Remaining Fortune) * {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}} – Enshrined are the war dead of Takatsuki who perished in the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|ja|西南戦争}}, {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, {{ill|Second Sino-Japanese War|ja|日中戦争}}, and the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争}}. : Annual Festival: Third Sunday of May Nomijinja02.jpg|Auxiliary Shrine Nagai Shrine Karamon Tessen.png|Nagai Shrine {{ill|Family crest|ja|家紋}} ({{ill|Takatsuki Domain|ja|高槻藩}} lord, {{ill|Nagai family|ja|永井氏}}, {{ill|Family crest|ja|家紋}}) == Festivals == * New Year’s Festival – January 1 * {{ill|Tondo Festival|ja|とんど}} – January 15 * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭}} – February 3 * Dedication {{ill|Crying Sumo|ja|泣き相撲}} – April 29 * Great Purification of Summer Crossing (Chinowa Ritual) – June 30 * Autumn Grand Festival – Second Monday of October * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} – November 23 * Year-End Great Purification – December 31 == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Takatsuki City === * Nagai Shrine {{Shinto shrine |Name = '''Nomijinja''' (野見神社) |Image = [[File:Nomijinja01.jpg|300px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=18|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|frame-height=300|coord={{coord|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Takatsuki City|ja|高槻市}} Nomimachi 6-6 |Coordinates = {{wikicoordinates|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命}}
{{ill|Nomitatsune|ja|野見宿禰|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=野見宿禰命}} |Founding = Late 9th century |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small), former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Annual Festival = Second Monday of October }} '''Nomijinja''' (のみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Nomimachi, {{ill|Takatsuki City|ja|高槻市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} shrine, and its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == History == In the late 9th century, during the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} of {{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}} ({{ill|887|ja|887年}} – {{ill|897|ja|897年}}), an epidemic spread throughout the region. Following a divine oracle, {{ill|Gozutennō|ja|牛頭天王|lt=Gozutennō|lt_ja=ごずてんのう}} (identified with {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命}}) was enshrined, and as the epidemic subsided, a shrine building was constructed, establishing the Gozutennō Shrine. In the late 10th century, when {{ill|Takatsuki Castle|ja|高槻城}} was constructed, the shrine was venerated as a guardian of the castle.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/ |title=高槻城内守護 野見神社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} However, in the 16th century when {{ill|Takayama Ukon|ja|高山右近}} became the lord of Takatsuki Castle, his adherence to {{ill|Christianity|ja|キリスト教}} led him to destroy the shrine building within the castle and confiscate its estate. Later, in the 5th year of the {{ill|Genna|ja|元和}} era ({{ill|1619|ja|1619年}}), the lord of Takatsuki Castle, {{ill|Matsudaira Ienobu|ja|松平家信}}, rebuilt the shrine building.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e5%be%a1%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92-%e8%a1%8c%e4%ba%8b.html |title=野見神社 御由緒・年間行事 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} In {{ill|1868|ja|1868年}} (the first year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}}), due to the {{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}, the name of the enshrined deity was changed to {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命}}, and furthermore, {{ill|Nomitatsune|ja|野見宿禰|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=野見宿禰命}} was enshrined alongside, prompting the shrine to be renamed “Nomijinja”. Consequently, although it is counted as one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} shrines listed in the {{ill|Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, the publication “Historic Sites, Famous Landscapes, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture” asserts that the renaming to “Nomijinja”—by enshrining Nomitatsune to emulate a Shikinaisha—meant that its descendant shrine is the auxiliary shrine {{ill|Nomigami Shrine|ja|野身神社}} of {{ill|Kamigu Tenmangu|ja|上宮天満宮}} in Takatsuki City. In {{ill|1871|ja|1871st year }} (the 4th year of Meiji), it was classified as a prefectural shrine ({{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}) as a Shikinaisha; however, in November of the following year, {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year }} (the 5th year of Meiji), it was demoted to a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}) for the reasons mentioned above. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities – {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命}}, {{ill|Nomitatsune|ja|野見宿禰|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=野見宿禰命}} == Shrine Grounds == === Shrine Buildings === * Main Hall – Rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Tenwa|ja|天和 (日本)|lt=Tenwa}} ({{ill|1682|ja|1682nd year }}). * Haiden – Renovated in {{ill|2003|ja|2003rd year }} (the 15th year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}). * Noh Stage * Shrine Office === Auxiliary Shrines === * {{ill|Nagai Shrine|ja|永井神社}} – Enshrined deity: the first lord of {{ill|Takatsuki Domain|ja|高槻藩}}, {{ill|Nagai Naotoki|ja|永井直清}}. Founded in the 5th year of the {{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政}} era ({{ill|1793|ja|1793rd year }}) by the 9th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進}}. Initially called “Naotoki Shrine”, it was referred to as “Naotoki Gongen” during the late Edo period. In the first year of the {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} era ({{ill|1848|ja|1848年}}), during the time of the 11th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naoteru|ja|永井直輝}}, the main hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Takatsuki City) was restored, and the haiden (also a designated tangible cultural property) along with the karamon (quadrangular gate, designated tangible cultural property) were established. The karamon was repaired in {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}} (the 22nd year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}). : Annual Festival: April 24{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e6%91%82%e6%9c%ab%e7%a4%be.html |title=野見神社 - 摂末社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} * {{ill|Kojima Shrine|ja|小島神社}} – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いちきしまひめのみこと}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=おほなむちのみこと}}, {{ill|Iwanagahime|ja|磐長姫命|lt=Iwanagahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いわながひめのみこと}}, and {{ill|Ameno Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=あめのこやねのみこと}}. Its founding date is unknown; the shrine building was newly constructed on October 1, {{ill|2021|ja|2021st year }} (the 3rd year of {{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和}}). : Annual Festival: Second Sunday of October (Nomijinja Autumn Evening Festival) * Shishamyojin – In the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正}} the separate shrines of Fukujinja, Inari Shrine, Iwajinsha, and Sorei Shrine were merged into one. : Annual Festival: April 11 :: Fukujinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Shinatsuhikokami|ja|志那都比古神|lt=Shinatsuhikokami|lt_ja=しなつひこのかみ}}, {{ill|Shinatsuhime|ja|志那都姫神|lt=Shinatsuhime|lt_ja=しなつひめのかみ}}, {{ill|Homusubi|ja|火産霊|lt=Homusubi|lt_ja=ほむすび}}, {{ill|Kanayamahikokami|ja|金山比古神|lt=Kanayamahikokami|lt_ja=かなやまひこのかみ}}, {{ill|Kanayamahime|ja|金山姫神|lt=Kanayamahime|lt_ja=かなやまひめのかみ}}, {{ill|Haniyamahikokami|ja|埴山比古神|lt=Haniyamahikokami|lt_ja=はにやまひこのかみ}} (linked as {{ill|Haniyamas|ja|ハニヤス}} with display 埴山比古神), and {{ill|Mizuhanomenokami|ja|弥都波能売神|lt=Mizuhanomenokami|lt_ja=みづはのめのかみ}}. Founded in August of the 9th year of {{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政}} ({{ill|1797|ja|1797年}}) by {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進}}. :: Inari Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|宇迦之御魂神|lt=Ukano Mitama-no-Kami|lt_ja=うかのみたまのかみ}}. Founded in February of the 11th year of Kansei ({{ill|1799|ja|1799年}}) by Nagai Naotoshi. :: Iwajinsha – Enshrined deities: (i) Yachimata Hikonami, (ii) Yachimata Hime, and (iii) Kunado-no-Kami. Founded in May of the 4th year of {{ill|Genbun|ja|元文}} ({{ill|1739|ja|1739年}}) by {{ill|Nagai Ryunosuke|ja|永井流之助}}. :: Sorei Shrine – Enshrined are the successive chief priests, shrine priests, and parishioners who have devoted themselves to the shrine. Founded in {{ill|1878|ja|1878年}} (the 11th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}}). * {{ill|Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine|ja|高槻えびす神社}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu|ja|蛭子大神}}. It was established in {{ill|1956|ja|1956年}} ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 31) as a branch from {{ill|Nishinomiya Shrine|ja|西宮神社}}, and the current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1966|ja|1966年}} ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 41). : Takatsuki Ebisu Festival – January 10 (subdivided into the 9th: Evening Ebisu, 10th: Main Ebisu, 11th: Remaining Fortune) * {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}} – Enshrined are the war dead of Takatsuki who perished in the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|ja|西南戦争}}, {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, {{ill|Second Sino-Japanese War|ja|日中戦争}}, and the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争}}. : Annual Festival: Third Sunday of May Nomijinja02.jpg|Auxiliary Shrine Nagai Shrine Karamon Tessen.png|Nagai Shrine {{ill|Family crest|ja|家紋}} ({{ill|Takatsuki {{Shinto shrine |Name = '''Nomijinja''' (野見神社) |Image = [[File:Nomijinja01.jpg|300px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=18|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|frame-height=300|coord={{coord|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Takatsuki City|ja|高槻市}} Nomimachi 6-6 |Coordinates = {{wikicoordinates|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命}}
{{ill|Nomitatsune|ja|野見宿禰|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=野見宿禰命}} |Founding = Late 9th century |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small), former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Annual Festival = Second Monday of October }} '''Nomijinja''' (のみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Nomimachi, {{ill|Takatsuki City|ja|高槻市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} shrine, and its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == History == In the late 9th century, during the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} of {{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}} ({{ill|887|ja|887th year }} – {{ill|897|ja|897th year }}), an epidemic spread throughout the region. Following a divine oracle, {{ill|Gozutennō|ja|牛頭天王|lt=Gozutennō|lt_ja=ごずてんのう}} (identified with {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命}}) was enshrined, and as the epidemic subsided, a shrine building was constructed, establishing the Gozutennō Shrine. In the late 10th century, when {{ill|Takatsuki Castle|ja|高槻城}} was constructed, the shrine was venerated as a guardian of the castle.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/ |title=高槻城内守護 野見神社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} However, in the 16th century when {{ill|Takayama Ukon|ja|高山右近}} became the lord of Takatsuki Castle, his adherence to {{ill|Christianity|ja|キリスト教}} led him to destroy the shrine building within the castle and confiscate its estate. Later, in the 5th year of the {{ill|Genna|ja|元和}} era ({{ill|1619|ja|1619年}}), the lord of Takatsuki Castle, {{ill|Matsudaira Ienobu|ja|松平家信}}, rebuilt the shrine building.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e5%be%a1%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92-%e8%a1%8c%e4%ba%8b.html |title=野見神社 御由緒・年間行事 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} In {{ill|1868|ja|1868th year }} (the first year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}}), due to the {{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}, the name of the enshrined deity was changed to {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命}}, and furthermore, {{ill|Nomitatsune|ja|野見宿禰|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=野見宿禰命}} was enshrined alongside, prompting the shrine to be renamed “Nomijinja”. Consequently, although it is counted as one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} shrines listed in the {{ill|Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, the publication “Historic Sites, Famous Landscapes, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture” asserts that the renaming to “Nomijinja”—by enshrining Nomitatsune to emulate a Shikinaisha—meant that its descendant shrine is the auxiliary shrine {{ill|Nomigami Shrine|ja|野身神社}} of {{ill|Kamigu Tenmangu|ja|上宮天満宮}} in Takatsuki City. In {{ill|1871|ja|1871st year }} (the 4th year of Meiji), it was classified as a prefectural shrine ({{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}) as a Shikinaisha; however, in November of the following year, {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year }} (the 5th year of Meiji), it was demoted to a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}) for the reasons mentioned above. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities – {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命}}, {{ill|Nomitatsune|ja|野見宿禰|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=野見宿禰命}} == Shrine Grounds == === Shrine Buildings === * Main Hall – Rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Tenwa|ja|天和 (日本)|lt=Tenwa}} ({{ill|1682|ja|1682nd year }}). * Haiden – Renovated in {{ill|2003|ja|2003rd year }} (the 15th year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}). * Noh Stage * Shrine Office === Auxiliary Shrines === * {{ill|Nagai Shrine|ja|永井神社}} – Enshrined deity: the first lord of {{ill|Takatsuki Domain|ja|高槻藩}}, {{ill|Nagai Naotoki|ja|永井直清}}. Founded in the 5th year of the {{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政}} era ({{ill|1793|ja|1793rd year }}) by the 9th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進}}. Initially called “Naotoki Shrine”, it was referred to as “Naotoki Gongen” during the late Edo period. In the first year of the {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} era ({{ill|1848|ja|1848th year }}), during the time of the 11th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naoteru|ja|永井直輝}}, the main hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Takatsuki City) was restored, and the haiden (also a designated tangible cultural property) along with the karamon (quadrangular gate, designated tangible cultural property) were established. The karamon was repaired in {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}} (the 22nd year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}). : Annual Festival: April 24{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e6%91%82%e6%9c%ab%e7%a4%be.html |title=野見神社 - 摂末社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} * {{ill|Kojima Shrine|ja|小島神社}} – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いちきしまひめのみこと}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=おほなむちのみこと}}, {{ill|Iwanagahime|ja|磐長姫命|lt=Iwanagahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いわながひめのみこと}}, and {{ill|Ameno Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=あめのこやねのみこと}}. Its founding date is unknown; the shrine building was newly constructed on October 1, {{ill|2021|ja|2021st year }} (the 3rd year of {{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和}}). : Annual Festival: Second Sunday of October (Nomijinja Autumn Evening Festival) * Shishamyojin – In the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正}} the separate shrines of Fukujinja, Inari Shrine, Iwajinsha, and Sorei Shrine were merged into one. : Annual Festival: April 11 :: Fukujinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Shinatsuhikokami|ja|志那都比古神|lt=Shinatsuhikokami|lt_ja=しなつひこのかみ}}, {{ill|Shinatsuhime|ja|志那都姫神|lt=Shinatsuhime|lt_ja=しなつひめのかみ}}, {{ill|Homusubi|ja|火産霊|lt=Homusubi|lt_ja=ほむすび}}, {{ill|Kanayamahikokami|ja|金山比古神|lt=Kanayamahikokami|lt_ja=かなやまひこのかみ}}, {{ill|Kanayamahime|ja|金山姫神|lt=Kanayamahime|lt_ja=かなやまひめのかみ}}, {{ill|Haniyamahikokami|ja|埴山比古神|lt=Haniyamahikokami|lt_ja=はにやまひこのかみ}} (linked as {{ill|Haniyamas|ja|ハニヤス}} with display 埴山比古神), and {{ill|Mizuhanomenokami|ja|弥都波能売神|lt=Mizuhanomenokami|lt_ja=みづはのめのかみ}}. Founded in August of the 9th year of {{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政}} ({{ill|1797|ja|1797th year }}) by {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進}}. :: Inari Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|宇迦之御魂神|lt=Ukano Mitama-no-Kami|lt_ja=うかのみたまのかみ}}. Founded in February of the 11th year of Kansei ({{ill|1799|ja|1799年}}) by Nagai Naotoshi. :: Iwajinsha – Enshrined deities: (i) Yachimata Hikonami, (ii) Yachimata Hime, and (iii) Kunado-no-Kami. Founded in May of the 4th year of {{ill|Genbun|ja|元文}} ({{ill|1739|ja|1739年}}) by {{ill|Nagai Ryunosuke|ja|永井流之助}}. :: Sorei Shrine – Enshrined are the successive chief priests, shrine priests, and parishioners who have devoted themselves to the shrine. Founded in {{ill|1878|ja|1878th year }} (the 11th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}}). * {{ill|Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine|ja|高槻えびす神社}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu|ja|蛭子大神}}. It was established in {{ill|1956|ja|1956年}} ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 31) as a branch from {{ill|Nishinomiya Shrine|ja|西宮神社}}, and the current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1966|ja|1966年}} ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 41). : Takatsuki Ebisu Festival – January 10 (subdivided into the 9th: Evening Ebisu, 10th: Main Ebisu, 11th: Remaining Fortune) * {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}} – Enshrined are In the late 10th century, when {{ill|Takatsuki Castle|ja|高槻城}} was constructed, the shrine was venerated as a guardian of the castle.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/ |title=高槻城内守護 野見神社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} However, in the 16th century when {{ill|Takayama Ukon|ja|高山右近}} became the lord of Takatsuki Castle, his adherence to {{ill|Christianity|ja|キリスト教}} led him to destroy the shrine building within the castle and confiscate its estate. Later, in the 5th year of the {{ill|Genna|ja|元和}} era ({{ill|1619|ja|1619th year }}), the lord of Takatsuki Castle, {{ill|Matsudaira Ienobu|ja|松平家信}}, rebuilt the shrine building.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e5%be%a1%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92-%e8%a1%8c%e4%ba%8b.html |title=野見神社 御由緒・年間行事 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} In {{ill|1868|ja|1868th year }} (the first year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}}), due to the {{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}, the name of the enshrined deity was changed to {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命}}, and furthermore, {{ill|Nomitatsune|ja|野見宿禰|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=野見宿禰命}} was enshrined alongside, prompting the shrine to be renamed “Nomijinja”. Consequently, although it is counted as one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} shrines listed in the {{ill|Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, the publication “Historic Sites, Famous Landscapes, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture” asserts that the renaming to “Nomijinja”—by enshrining Nomitatsune to emulate a Shikinaisha—meant that its descendant shrine is the auxiliary shrine {{ill|Nomigami Shrine|ja|野身神社}} of {{ill|Kamigu Tenmangu|ja|上宮天満宮}} in Takatsuki City. In {{ill|1871|ja|1871st year }} (the 4th year of Meiji), it was classified as a prefectural shrine ({{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}) as a Shikinaisha; however, in November of the following year, {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year }} (the 5th year of Meiji), it was demoted to a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}) for the reasons mentioned above. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities – {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命}}, {{ill|Nomitatsune|ja|野見宿禰|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=野見宿禰命}} == Shrine Grounds == === Shrine Buildings === * Main Hall – Rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Tenwa|ja|天和 (日本)|lt=Tenwa}} ({{ill|1682|ja|1682nd year }}). * Haiden – Renovated in {{ill|2003|ja|2003rd year }} (the 15th year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}). * Noh Stage * Shrine Office === Auxiliary Shrines === * {{ill|Nagai Shrine|ja|永井神社}} – Enshrined deity: the first lord of {{ill|Takatsuki Domain|ja|高槻藩}}, {{ill|Nagai Naotoki|ja|永井直清}}. Founded in the 5th year of the {{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政}} era ({{ill|1793|ja|1793rd year }}) by the 9th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進}}. Initially called “Naotoki Shrine”, it was referred to as “Naotoki Gongen” during the late Edo period. In the first year of the {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} era ({{ill|1848|ja|1848th year }}), during the time of the 11th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naoteru|ja|永井直輝}}, the main hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Takatsuki City) was restored, and the haiden (also a designated tangible cultural property) along with the karamon (quadrangular gate, designated tangible cultural property) were established. The karamon was repaired in {{ill|2010|ja|2010年}} (the 22nd year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}). : Annual Festival: April 24{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e6%91%82%e6%9c%ab%e7%a4%be.html |title=野見神社 - 摂末社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} * {{ill|Kojima Shrine|ja|小島神社}} – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いちきしまひめのみこと}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=おほなむちのみこと}}, {{ill|Iwanagahime|ja|磐長姫命|lt=Iwanagahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いわながひめのみこと}}, and {{ill|Ameno Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=あめのこやねのみこと}}. Its founding date is unknown; the shrine building was newly constructed on October 1, {{ill|2021|ja|2021st year }} (the 3rd year of {{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和}}). : Annual Festival: Second Sunday of October (Nomijinja Autumn Evening Festival) * Shishamyojin – In the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正}} the separate shrines of Fukujinja, Inari Shrine, Iwajinsha, and Sorei Shrine were merged into one. : Annual Festival: April 11 :: Fukujinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Shinatsuhikokami|ja|志那都比古神|lt=Shinatsuhikokami|lt_ja=しなつひこのかみ}}, {{ill|Shinatsuhime|ja|志那都姫神|lt=Shinatsuhime|lt_ja=しなつひめのかみ}}, {{ill|Homusubi|ja|火産霊|lt=Homusubi|lt_ja=ほむすび}}, {{ill|Kanayamahikokami|ja|金山比古神|lt=Kanayamahikokami|lt_ja=かなやまひこのかみ}}, {{ill|Kanayamahime|ja|金山姫神|lt=Kanayamahime|lt_ja=かなやまひめのかみ}}, {{ill|Haniyamahikokami|ja|埴山比古神|lt=Haniyamahikokami|lt_ja=はにやまひこのかみ}} (linked as {{ill|Haniyamas|ja|ハニヤス}} with display 埴山比古神), and {{ill|Mizuhanomenokami|ja|弥都波能売神|lt=Mizuhanomenokami|lt_ja=みづはのめのかみ}}. Founded in August of the 9th year of {{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政}} ({{ill|1797|ja|1797th year }}) by {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進}}. :: Inari Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|宇迦之御魂神|lt=Ukano Mitama-no-Kami|lt_ja=うかのみたまのかみ}}. Founded in February of the 11th year of Kansei ({{ill|1799|ja|1799th year }}) by Nagai Naotoshi. :: Iwajinsha – Enshrined deities: (i) Yachimata Hikonami, (ii) Yachimata Hime, and (iii) Kunado-no-Kami. Founded in May of the 4th year of {{ill|Genbun|ja|元文}} ({{ill|1739|ja|1739th year }}) by {{ill|Nagai Ryunosuke|ja|永井流之助}}. :: Sorei Shrine – Enshrined are the successive chief priests, shrine priests, and parishioners who have devoted themselves to the shrine. It was established in {{ill|1956|ja|1956年}} ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 31) as a branch from {{ill|Nishinomiya Shrine|ja|西宮神社}}, and the current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1966|ja|1966年}} ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 41). : Takatsuki Ebisu Festival – January 10 (subdivided into the 9th: Evening Ebisu, 10th: Main Ebisu, 11th: Remaining Fortune) * {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}} – Enshrined are Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=18|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|frame-height=300|coord={{coord|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Takatsuki City|ja|高槻市}} Nomimachi 6-6 |Coordinates = {{wikicoordinates|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命}}
{{ill|Nomitatsune|ja|野見宿禰|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=野見宿禰命}} |Founding = Late 9th century |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small), former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Annual Festival = Second Monday of October }} '''Nomijinja''' (のみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Nomimachi, {{ill|Takatsuki City|ja|高槻市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} shrine, and its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == History == In the late 9th century, during the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} of {{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}} ({{ill|887|ja|887th year }} – {{ill|897|ja|897th year }}), an epidemic spread throughout the region. Following a divine oracle, {{ill|Gozutennō|ja|牛頭天王|lt=Gozutennō|lt_ja=ごずてんのう}} (identified with {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命}}) was enshrined, and as the epidemic subsided, a shrine building was constructed, establishing the Gozutennō Shrine. In the late 10th century, when {{ill|Takatsuki Castle|ja|高槻城}} was constructed, the shrine was venerated as a guardian of the castle.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/ |title=高槻城内守護 野見神社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} However, in the 16th century when {{ill|Takayama Ukon|ja|高山右近}} became the lord of Takatsuki Castle, his adherence to {{ill|Christianity|ja|キリスト教}} led him to destroy the shrine building within the castle and confiscate its estate. {{Shinto shrine |Name = '''Nomijinja''' (野見神社) |Image = [[File:Nomijinja01.jpg|300px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=18|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|frame-height=300|coord={{coord|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Takatsuki City|ja|高槻市}} Nomimachi 6-6 |Coordinates = {{wikicoordinates|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命}}
{{ill|Nomitatsune|ja|野見宿禰|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=野見宿禰命}} |Founding = Late 9th century |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small), former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Annual Festival = Second Monday of October }} '''Nomijinja''' (のみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Nomimachi, {{ill|Takatsuki City|ja|高槻市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} shrine, and its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == History == In the late 9th century, during the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} of {{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}} ({{ill|887|ja|887th year }} – {{ill|897|ja|897th year }}), an epidemic spread throughout the region. Following a divine oracle, {{ill|Gozutennō|ja|牛頭天王|lt=Gozutennō|lt_ja=ごずてんのう}} (identified with {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命}}) was enshrined, and as the epidemic subsided, a shrine building was constructed, establishing the Gozutennō Shrine. In the late 10th century, when {{ill|Takatsuki Castle|ja|高槻城}} was constructed, the shrine was venerated as a guardian of the castle.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/ |title=高槻城内守護 野見神社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} However, in the 16th century when {{ill|Takayama Ukon|ja|高山右近}} became the lord of Takatsuki Castle, his adherence to {{ill|Christianity|ja|キリスト教}} led him to destroy the shrine building within the castle and confiscate its estate. Later, in the 5th year of the {{ill|Genna|ja|元和}} era ({{ill|1619|ja|1619th year }}), the lord of Takatsuki Castle, {{ill|Matsudaira Ienobu|ja|松平家信}}, rebuilt the shrine building.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e5%be%a1%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92-%e8%a1%8c%e4%ba%8b.html |title=野見神社 御由緒・年間行事 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} In {{ill|1868|ja|1868th year }} (the first year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}}), due to the {{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}, the name of the enshrined deity was changed to {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命}}, and furthermore, {{ill|Nomitatsune|ja|野見宿禰|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=野見宿禰命}} was enshrined alongside, prompting the shrine to be renamed “Nomijinja”. Consequently, although it is counted as one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} shrines listed in the {{ill|Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, the publication “Historic Sites, Famous Landscapes, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture” asserts that the renaming to “Nomijinja”—by enshrining Nomitatsune to emulate a Shikinaisha—meant that its descendant shrine is the auxiliary shrine {{ill|Nomigami Shrine|ja|野身神社}} of {{ill|Kamigu Tenmangu|ja|上宮天満宮}} in Takatsuki City. In {{ill|1871|ja|1871st year }} (the 4th year of Meiji), it was classified as a prefectural shrine ({{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}) as a Shikinaisha; however, in November of the following year, {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year }} (the 5th year of Meiji), it was demoted to a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}) for the reasons mentioned above. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities – {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命}}, {{ill|Nomitatsune|ja|野見宿禰|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=野見宿禰命}} == Shrine Grounds == === Shrine Buildings === * Main Hall – Rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Tenwa|ja|天和 (日本)|lt=Tenwa}} ({{ill|1682|ja|1682nd year }}). * Haiden – Renovated in {{ill|2003|ja|2003rd year }} (the 15th year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}). * Noh Stage * Shrine Office === Auxiliary Shrines === * {{ill|Nagai Shrine|ja|永井神社}} – Enshrined deity: the first lord of {{ill|Takatsuki Domain|ja|高槻藩}}, {{ill|Nagai Naotoki|ja|永井直清}}. Founded in the 5th year of the {{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政}} era ({{ill|1793|ja|1793rd year }}) by the 9th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進}}. Initially called “Naotoki Shrine”, it was referred to as “Naotoki Gongen” during the late Edo period. In the first year of the {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} era ({{ill|1848|ja|1848th year }}), during the time of the 11th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naoteru|ja|永井直輝}}, the main hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Takatsuki City) was restored, and the haiden (also a designated tangible cultural property) along with the karamon (quadrangular gate, designated tangible cultural property) were established. The karamon was repaired in {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year }} (the 22nd year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}). : Annual Festival: April 24{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e6%91%82%e6%9c%ab%e7%a4%be.html |title=野見神社 - 摂末社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} * {{ill|Kojima Shrine|ja|小島神社}} – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いちきしまひめのみこと}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=おほなむちのみこと}}, {{ill|Iwanagahime|ja|磐長姫命|lt=Iwanagahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いわながひめのみこと}}, and {{ill|Ameno Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=あめのこやねのみこと}}. Its founding date is unknown; the shrine building was newly constructed on October 1, {{ill|2021|ja|2021st year }} (the 3rd year of {{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和}}). : Annual Festival: Second Sunday of October (Nomijinja Autumn Evening Festival) * Shishamyojin – In the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正}} the separate shrines of Fukujinja, Inari Shrine, Iwajinsha, and Sorei Shrine were merged into one. : Annual Festival: April 11 :: Fukujinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Shinatsuhikokami|ja|志那都比古神|lt=Shinatsuhikokami|lt_ja=しなつひこのかみ}}, {{ill|Shinatsuhime|ja|志那都姫神|lt=Shinatsuhime|lt_ja=しなつひめのかみ}}, {{ill|Homusubi|ja|火産霊|lt=Homusubi|lt_ja=ほむすび}}, {{ill|Kanayamahikokami|ja|金山比古神|lt=Kanayamahikokami|lt_ja=かなやまひこのかみ}}, {{ill|Kanayamahime|ja|金山姫神|lt=Kanayamahime|lt_ja=かなやまひめのかみ}}, {{ill|Haniyamahikokami|ja|埴山比古神|lt=Haniyamahikokami|lt_ja=はにやまひこのかみ}} (linked as {{ill|Haniyamas|ja|ハニヤス}} with display 埴山比古神), and {{ill|Mizuhanomenokami|ja|弥都波能売神|lt=Mizuhanomenokami|lt_ja=みづはのめのかみ}}. Founded in August of the 9th year of {{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政}} ({{ill|1797|ja|1797th year }}) by {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進}}. :: Inari Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|宇迦之御魂神|lt=Ukano Mitama-no-Kami|lt_ja=うかのみたまのかみ}}. Founded in February of the 11th year of Kansei ({{ill|1799|ja|1799th year }}) by Nagai Naotoshi. :: Iwajinsha – Enshrined deities: (i) Yachimata Hikonami, (ii) Yachimata Hime, and (iii) Kunado-no-Kami. Founded in May of the 4th year of {{ill|Genbun|ja|元文}} ({{ill|1739|ja|1739th year }}) by {{ill|Nagai Ryunosuke|ja|永井流之助}}. :: Sorei Shrine – Enshrined are the successive chief priests, shrine priests, and parishioners who have devoted themselves to the shrine. Founded in {{ill|1878|ja|1878th year }} (the 11th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}}). * {{ill|Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine|ja|高槻えびす神社}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu|ja|蛭子大神}}. It was established in {{ill|1956|ja|1956th year }} ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 31) as a branch from {{ill|Nishinomiya Shrine|ja|西宮神社}}, and the current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1966|ja|1966th year }} ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 41). : Takatsuki Ebisu Festival – January 10 (subdivided into the 9th: Evening Ebisu, 10th: Main Ebisu, 11th: Remaining Fortune) * {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}} – Enshrined are the war dead of Takatsuki who perished in the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|ja|西南戦争}}, {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, {{ill|Second Sino-Japanese War|ja|日中戦争}}, and the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争}}. : Annual Festival: Third Sunday of May Nomijinja02.jpg|Auxiliary Shrine Nagai Shrine Karamon Tessen.png|Nagai Shrine {{ill|Family crest|ja|家紋}} ({{ill|Takatsuki Domain|ja|高槻藩}} lord, {{ill|Nagai family|ja|永井氏}}, {{ill|Family crest|ja|家紋}}) == Festivals == * New Year’s Festival – January 1 * {{ill|Tondo Festival|ja|とんど}} – January 15 * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭}} – February 3 * Dedication {{ill|Crying Sumo|ja|泣き相撲}} – April 29 * Great Purification of Summer Crossing (Chinowa Ritual) – June 30 * Autumn Grand Festival – Second Monday of October * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} – November 23 * Year-End Great Purification – December 31 == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Takatsuki City === * Nagai Shrine buildings (main hall, haiden) with attached items: 4 ridge plaques and 1 Takatsuki Castle ema – Designated on June 14, {{ill|2005|ja|2005th year }} (Heisei 17). * Nagai Shrine Karamon – Designated on June 14, 2005 (Heisei 17). * Seventeen items of materials related to the traditional Nagai Naotoki of Nagai Shrine – Deposited at {{ill|Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|ja|高槻市立しろあと歴史館}}. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Catholic Takatsuki Church|ja|カトリック高槻教会}} (adjacent) * Japan Kurarechian Association Headquarters Monastery * Maria Inmaculada Kindergarten * {{ill|Takatsuki Modern Theater|ja|高槻現代劇場}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|ja|高槻市立しろあと歴史館}} * The ruins of {{ill|Takatsuki Castle|ja|高槻城}}, {{ill|Castle Ruins Park|ja|城跡公園 (高槻市)}}, and the {{ill|Takatsuki City Historical Folk Museum|ja|高槻市立歴史民俗資料館}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.nomijinja.jp/ Nomijinja] (Official website) * {{Cite web|和書|year=2012 |url=http://www.city.takatsuki.osaka.jp/rekishi_kanko/rekishi/rekishikan/jidai/shaji/1327659158743.html |title=Nomijinja |work=インターネット歴史館 |publisher=Takatsuki City|accessdate=2014-12-13}} * {{Cite web|和書|year=2012 |url=http://www.city.takatsuki.osaka.jp/rekishi_kanko/rekishi/rekishikan/jidai/shaji/1327671298593.html |title=Revived "Nagai Shrine Karamon" |work=インターネット歴史館 |publisher=Takatsuki City|accessdate=2014-12-13}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Takatsuki City]] [[Category:History of Takatsuki City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha of Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Gion Shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Settsu Province]] {{shinto-stub}} [[category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Iwanaga Faith]]{{Iwanaga Faith}}[[Category:Iwanaga Faith]] [[ja:野見神社]]

({{ill|Takatsuki Domain|ja|高槻藩}} lord, {{ill|Nagai family|ja|永井氏}}, {{ill|Family crest|ja|家紋}}) == Festivals == * New Year’s Festival – January 1 * {{ill|Tondo Festival|ja|とんど}} – January 15 * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭}} – February 3 * Dedication {{ill|Crying Sumo|ja|泣き相撲}} – April 29 * Great Purification of Summer Crossing (Chinowa Ritual) – June 30 * Autumn Grand Festival – Second Monday of October * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} – November 23 * Year-End Great Purification – December 31 == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Takatsuki City === * Nagai Shrine buildings (main hall, haiden) with attached items: 4 ridge plaques and 1 Takatsuki Castle ema – Designated on June 14, {{ill|2005|ja|2005th year }} (Heisei 17). * Nagai Shrine Karamon – {{Shinto shrine |Name = '''Nomijinja''' (野見神社) |Image = [[File:Nomijinja01.jpg|300px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=18|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|frame-height=300|coord={{coord|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}{{ill|Takatsuki City|ja|高槻市}} Nomimachi 6-6 |Coordinates = {{wikicoordinates|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命}}
{{ill|Nomitatsune|ja|野見宿禰|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=野見宿禰命}} |Founding = Late 9th century |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (small), former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Annual Festival = Second Monday of October }} '''Nomijinja''' (のみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Nomimachi, {{ill|Takatsuki City|ja|高槻市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} shrine, and its former rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}. == History == In the late 9th century, during the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}} of {{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇}} ({{ill|887|ja|887th year }} – {{ill|897|ja|897th year }}), an epidemic spread throughout the region. Following a divine oracle, {{ill|Gozutennō|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|lt=Gozutennō|lt_ja=ごずてんのう}} (identified with {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命}}) was enshrined, and as the epidemic subsided, a shrine building was constructed, establishing the Gozutennō Shrine. In the late 10th century, when {{ill|Takatsuki Castle|ja|高槻城}} was constructed, the shrine was venerated as a guardian of the castle.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/ |title=高槻城内守護 野見神社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} However, in the 16th century when {{ill|Takayama Ukon|ja|高山右近}} became the lord of Takatsuki Castle, his adherence to {{ill|Christianity|ja|キリスト教}} led him to destroy the shrine building within the castle and confiscate its estate. Later, in the 5th year of the {{ill|Genna|ja|元和}} era ({{ill|1619|ja|1619th year }}), the lord of Takatsuki Castle, {{ill|Matsudaira Ienobu|ja|松平家信}}, rebuilt the shrine building.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e5%be%a1%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92-%e8%a1%8c%e4%ba%8b.html |title=野見神社 御由緒・年間行事 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} In {{ill|1868|ja|1868th year }} (the first year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}}), due to the {{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離}}, the name of the enshrined deity was changed to {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命}}, and furthermore, {{ill|Nomitatsune|ja|野見宿禰|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=野見宿禰命}} was enshrined alongside, prompting the shrine to be renamed “Nomijinja”. Consequently, although it is counted as one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} shrines listed in the {{ill|Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, the publication “Historic Sites, Famous Landscapes, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture” asserts that the renaming to “Nomijinja”—by enshrining Nomitatsune to emulate a Shikinaisha—meant that its descendant shrine is the auxiliary shrine {{ill|Nomigami Shrine|ja|野身神社}} of {{ill|Kamigu Tenmangu|ja|上宮天満宮}} in Takatsuki City. In {{ill|1871|ja|1871st year }} (the 4th year of Meiji), it was classified as a prefectural shrine ({{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}}) as a Shikinaisha; however, in November of the following year, {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year }} (the 5th year of Meiji), it was demoted to a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}}) for the reasons mentioned above. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities – {{ill|Susanoo|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命}}, {{ill|Nomitatsune|ja|野見宿禰|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=野見宿禰命}} == Shrine Grounds == === Shrine Buildings === * Main Hall – Rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Tenwa|ja|天和 (日本)|lt=Tenwa}} ({{ill|1682|ja|1682nd year }}). * Haiden – Renovated in {{ill|2003|ja|2003rd year }} (the 15th year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}). * Noh Stage * Shrine Office === Auxiliary Shrines === * {{ill|Nagai Shrine|ja|永井神社}} – Enshrined deity: the first lord of {{ill|Takatsuki Domain|ja|高槻藩}}, {{ill|Nagai Naotoki|ja|永井直清}}. Founded in the 5th year of the {{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政}} era ({{ill|1793|ja|1793rd year }}) by the 9th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進}}. Initially called “Naotoki Shrine”, it was referred to as “Naotoki Gongen” during the late Edo period. In the first year of the {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永}} era ({{ill|1848|ja|1848th year }}), during the time of the 11th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naoteru|ja|永井直輝}}, the main hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Takatsuki City) was restored, and the haiden (also a designated tangible cultural property) along with the karamon (quadrangular gate, designated tangible cultural property) were established. The karamon was repaired in {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year }} (the 22nd year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}}). : Annual Festival: April 24{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e6%91%82%e6%9c%ab%e7%a4%be.html |title=野見神社 - 摂末社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} * {{ill|Kojima Shrine|ja|小島神社}} – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市杵島姫命|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いちきしまひめのみこと}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=おほなむちのみこと}}, {{ill|Iwanagahime|ja|磐長姫命|lt=Iwanagahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いわながひめのみこと}}, and {{ill|Ameno Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=あめのこやねのみこと}}. Its founding date is unknown; the shrine building was newly constructed on October 1, {{ill|2021|ja|2021st year }} (the 3rd year of {{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和}}). : Annual Festival: Second Sunday of October (Nomijinja Autumn Evening Festival) * Shishamyojin – In the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正}} the separate shrines of Fukujinja, Inari Shrine, Iwajinsha, and Sorei Shrine were merged into one. : Annual Festival: April 11 :: Fukujinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Shinatsuhikokami|ja|志那都比古神|lt=Shinatsuhikokami|lt_ja=しなつひこのかみ}}, {{ill|Shinatsuhime|ja|志那都姫神|lt=Shinatsuhime|lt_ja=しなつひめのかみ}}, {{ill|Homusubi|ja|火産霊|lt=Homusubi|lt_ja=ほむすび}}, {{ill|Kanayamahikokami|ja|金山比古神|lt=Kanayamahikokami|lt_ja=かなやまひこのかみ}}, {{ill|Kanayamahime|ja|金山姫神|lt=Kanayamahime|lt_ja=かなやまひめのかみ}}, {{ill|Haniyamahikokami|ja|埴山比古神|lt=Haniyamahikokami|lt_ja=はにやまひこのかみ}} (linked as {{ill|Haniyamas|ja|ハニヤス}} with display 埴山比古神), and {{ill|Mizuhanomenokami|ja|弥都波能売神|lt=Mizuhanomenokami|lt_ja=みづはのめのかみ}}. Founded in August of the 9th year of {{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政}} ({{ill|1797|ja|1797th year }}) by {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進}}. :: Inari Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|宇迦之御魂神|lt=Ukano Mitama-no-Kami|lt_ja=うかのみたまのかみ}}. Founded in February of the 11th year of Kansei ({{ill|1799|ja|1799th year }}) by Nagai Naotoshi. :: Iwajinsha – Enshrined deities: (i) Yachimata Hikonami, (ii) Yachimata Hime, and (iii) Kunado-no-Kami. Founded in May of the 4th year of {{ill|Genbun|ja|元文}} ({{ill|1739|ja|1739th year }}) by {{ill|Nagai Ryunosuke|ja|永井流之助}}. :: Sorei Shrine – Enshrined are the successive chief priests, shrine priests, and parishioners who have devoted themselves to the shrine. Founded in {{ill|1878|ja|1878th year }} (the 11th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}}). * {{ill|Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine|ja|高槻えびす神社}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu|ja|蛭子大神}}. It was established in {{ill|1956|ja|1956th year }} ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 31) as a branch from {{ill|Nishinomiya Shrine|ja|西宮神社}}, and the current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1966|ja|1966th year }} ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 41). : Takatsuki Ebisu Festival – January 10 (subdivided into the 9th: Evening Ebisu, 10th: Main Ebisu, 11th: Remaining Fortune) * {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}} – Enshrined are the war dead of Takatsuki who perished in the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|ja|西南戦争}}, {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, {{ill|Second Sino-Japanese War|ja|日中戦争}}, and the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争}}. : Annual Festival: Third Sunday of May Nomijinja02.jpg|Auxiliary Shrine Nagai Shrine Karamon Tessen.png|Nagai Shrine {{ill|Family crest|ja|家紋}} ({{ill|Takatsuki Domain|ja|高槻藩}} lord, {{ill|Nagai family|ja|永井氏}}, {{ill|Family crest|ja|家紋}}) == Festivals == * New Year’s Festival – January 1 * {{ill|Tondo Festival|ja|とんど}} – January 15 * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭}} – February 3 * Dedication {{ill|Crying Sumo|ja|泣き相撲}} – April 29 * Great Purification of Summer Crossing (Chinowa Ritual) – June 30 * Autumn Grand Festival – Second Monday of October * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} – November 23 * Year-End Great Purification – December 31 == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Takatsuki City === * Nagai Shrine buildings (main hall, haiden) with attached items: 4 ridge plaques and 1 Takatsuki Castle ema – Designated on June 14, {{ill|2005|ja|2005th year }} (Heisei 17). * Nagai Shrine Karamon – Designated on June 14, 2005 (Heisei 17). * Seventeen items of materials related to the traditional Nagai Naotoki of Nagai Shrine – Deposited at {{ill|Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|ja|高槻市立しろあと歴史館}}. Founded in May of the 4th year of {{ill|Genbun|ja|元文}} ({{ill|1739|ja|1739th year }}) by {{ill|Nagai Ryunosuke|ja|永井流之助}}. :: Sorei Shrine – Enshrined are the successive chief priests, shrine priests, and parishioners who have devoted themselves to the shrine. Founded in {{ill|1878|ja|1878th year }} (the 11th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}}). * {{ill|Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine|ja|高槻えびす神社}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu|ja|蛭子大神}}. It was established in {{ill|1956|ja|1956th year }} ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 31) as a branch from {{ill|Nishinomiya Shrine|ja|西宮神社}}, and the current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1966|ja|1966th year }} ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 41). : Takatsuki Ebisu Festival – January 10 (subdivided into the 9th: Evening Ebisu, 10th: Main Ebisu, 11th: Remaining Fortune) * {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}} – Enshrined are the war dead of Takatsuki who perished in the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|ja|西南戦争}}, {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争}}, {{ill|Second Sino-Japanese War|ja|日中戦争}}, and the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争}}. : Annual Festival: Third Sunday of May Nomijinja02.jpg|Auxiliary Shrine Nagai Shrine Karamon Tessen.png|Nagai Shrine {{ill|Family crest|ja|家紋}} ({{ill|Takatsuki Domain|ja|高槻藩}} lord, {{ill|Nagai family|ja|永井氏}}, {{ill|Family crest|ja|家紋}}) == Festivals == * New Year’s Festival – January 1 * {{ill|Tondo Festival|ja|とんど}} – January 15 * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭}} – February 3 * Dedication {{ill|Crying Sumo|ja|泣き相撲}} – April 29 * Great Purification of Summer Crossing (Chinowa Ritual) – June 30 * Autumn Grand Festival – Second Monday of October * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} – November 23 * Year-End Great Purification – December 31 == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Takatsuki City === * Nagai Shrine buildings (main hall, haiden) with attached items: 4 ridge plaques and 1 Takatsuki Castle ema – Designated on June 14, {{ill|2005|ja|2005th year }} (Heisei 17). * Nagai Shrine Karamon – Designated on June 14, 2005 (Heisei 17). * Seventeen items of materials related to the traditional Nagai Naotoki of Nagai Shrine – Deposited at {{ill|Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|ja|高槻市立しろあと歴史館}}. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Catholic Takatsuki Church|ja|カトリック高槻教会}} (adjacent) * Japan Kurarechian Association Headquarters Monastery * Maria Inmaculada Kindergarten * {{ill|Takatsuki Modern Theater|ja|高槻現代劇場}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|ja|高槻市立しろあと歴史館}} * The ruins of {{ill|Takatsuki Castle|ja|高槻城}}, {{ill|Castle Ruins Park|ja|城跡公園 (高槻市)}}, and the {{ill|Takatsuki City Historical Folk Museum|ja|高槻市立歴史民俗資料館}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.nomijinja.jp/ Nomijinja] (Official website) * {{Cite web|和書|year=2012 |url=http://www.city.takatsuki.osaka.jp/rekishi_kanko/rekishi/rekishikan/jidai/shaji/1327659158743.html |title=Nomijinja |work=インターネット歴史館 |publisher=Takatsuki City|accessdate=2014-12-13}} * {{Cite web|和書|year=2012 |url=http://www.city.takatsuki.osaka.jp/rekishi_kanko/rekishi/rekishikan/jidai/shaji/1327671298593.html |title=Revived "Nagai Shrine Karamon" |work=インターネット歴史館 |publisher=Takatsuki City|accessdate=2014-12-13}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Takatsuki City]] [[Category:History of Takatsuki City]] [[Category:Former Gōsha of Osaka Prefecture]]
