{{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = '''Nomijinja''' (野見神社) |image = [[File:Nomijinja01.jpg|300px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=18|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|frame-height=300|coord={{coord|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft}}{{ill|Takatsuki City|ja|高槻市|qq=draft}} Nomimachi 6-6 |Coordinates = {{wikicoordinates|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Nomitatsune|ja|野見宿禰|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=野見宿禰命|qq=draft}} |Founding = Late 9th century |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft}} (small), former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft}} |Annual Festival = Second Monday of October }} '''Nomijinja''' (のみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft}} located in Nomimachi, {{ill|Takatsuki City|ja|高槻市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft}} shrine, and its former Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft}}. == History == In the late 9th century, during the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇|qq=draft}} ({{ill|887|ja|887th year|qq=draft}} – {{ill|897|ja|897th year|qq=draft}}), an epidemic spread throughout the region. Following a divine oracle, {{ill|Gozutennō|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|lt=Gozutennō|lt_ja=ごずてんのう|qq=draft}} (identified with {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命|qq=draft}}) was enshrined, and as the epidemic subsided, a shrine building was constructed, establishing the Gozutennō Shrine. In the late 10th century, when {{ill|Takatsuki Castle|ja|高槻城|qq=draft}} was constructed, the shrine was venerated as a guardian of the castle.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/ |title=高槻城内守護 野見神社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} However, in the 16th century when {{ill|Takayama Ukon|ja|高山右近|qq=draft}} became the lord of Takatsuki Castle, his adherence to {{ill|Christianity|ja|キリスト教|qq=draft}} led him to destroy the shrine building within the castle and confiscate its estate. Later, in the 5th year of the {{ill|Genna|ja|元和|qq=draft}} era ({{ill|1619|ja|1619th year|qq=draft}}), the lord of Takatsuki Castle, {{ill|Matsudaira Ienobu|ja|松平家信|qq=draft}}, rebuilt the shrine building.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e5%be%a1%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92-%e8%a1%8c%e4%ba%8b.html |title=野見神社 御由緒・年間行事 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} In {{ill|1868|ja|1868th year|qq=draft}} (the first year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft}}), due to the {{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft}}, the name of the enshrined deity was changed to {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命|qq=draft}}, and furthermore, {{ill|Nomitatsune|ja|野見宿禰|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=野見宿禰命|qq=draft}} was enshrined alongside, prompting the shrine to be renamed “Nomijinja”. Consequently, although it is counted as one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft}} shrines listed in the {{ill|Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft}}, the publication “Historic Sites, Famous Landscapes, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture” asserts that the renaming to “Nomijinja”—by enshrining Nomitatsune to emulate a Shikinaisha—meant that its descendant shrine is the auxiliary shrine {{ill|Nomigami Shrine|ja|野身神社|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Kamigu Tenmangu|ja|上宮天満宮|qq=draft}} in Takatsuki City. In {{ill|1871|ja|1871st year|qq=draft}} (the 4th year of Meiji), it was classified as a prefectural shrine ({{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft}}) as a Shikinaisha; however, in November of the following year, {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|qq=draft}} (the 5th year of Meiji), it was demoted to a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft}}) for the reasons mentioned above. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities – {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nomitatsune|ja|野見宿禰|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=野見宿禰命|qq=draft}} == Shrine Grounds == === Shrine Buildings === * Main Hall – Rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Tenwa|ja|天和 (日本)|lt=Tenwa|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1682|ja|1682nd year|qq=draft}}). * Haiden – Renovated in {{ill|2003|ja|2003rd year|qq=draft}} (the 15th year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft}}). * Noh Stage * Shrine Office === Auxiliary Shrines === * {{ill|Nagai Shrine|ja|永井神社|qq=draft}} – Enshrined deity: the first lord of {{ill|Takatsuki Domain|ja|高槻藩|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nagai Naotoki|ja|永井直清|qq=draft}}. Founded in the 5th year of the {{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政|qq=draft}} era ({{ill|1793|ja|1793rd year|qq=draft}}) by the 9th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進|qq=draft}}. Initially called “Naotoki Shrine”, it was referred to as “Naotoki Gongen” during the late Edo period. In the first year of the {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永|qq=draft}} era ({{ill|1848|ja|1848th year|qq=draft}}), during the time of the 11th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naoteru|ja|永井直輝|qq=draft}}, the main hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Takatsuki City) was restored, and the haiden (also a designated tangible cultural property) along with the karamon (quadrangular gate, designated tangible cultural property) were established. The karamon was repaired in {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year|qq=draft}} (the 22nd year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft}}). : Annual Festival: April 24{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e6%91%82%e6%9c%ab%e7%a4%be.html |title=野見神社 - 摂末社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} * {{ill|Kojima Shrine|ja|小島神社|qq=draft}} – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いちきしまひめのみこと|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=おほなむちのみこと|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Iwanagahime|ja|磐長姫命|lt=Iwanagahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いわながひめのみこと|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ameno Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=あめのこやねのみこと|qq=draft}}. Its founding date is unknown; the shrine building was newly constructed on October 1, {{ill|2021|ja|2021st year|qq=draft}} (the 3rd year of {{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和|qq=draft}}). : Annual Festival: Second Sunday of October (Nomijinja Autumn Evening Festival) * Shishamyojin – In the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正|qq=draft}} the separate shrines of Fukujinja, Inari Shrine, Iwajinsha, and Sorei Shrine were merged into one. : Annual Festival: April 11 :: Fukujinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Shinatsuhikokami|ja|志那都比古神|lt=Shinatsuhikokami|lt_ja=しなつひこのかみ|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shinatsuhime|ja|志那都姫神|lt=Shinatsuhime|lt_ja=しなつひめのかみ|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Homusubi|ja|火産霊|lt=Homusubi|lt_ja=ほむすび|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|ja|金山比古神|lt=Kanayamahikokami|lt_ja=かなやまひこのかみ|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|ja|金山姫神|lt=Kanayamahime|lt_ja=かなやまひめのかみ|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Haniyamahikokami|ja|埴山比古神|lt=Haniyamahikokami|lt_ja=はにやまひこのかみ|qq=draft}} (linked as {{ill|Haniyamas|ja|ハニヤス|qq=draft}} with display 埴山比古神), and {{ill|Mizuhanomenokami|ja|弥都波能売神|lt=Mizuhanomenokami|lt_ja=みづはのめのかみ|qq=draft}}. Founded in August of the 9th year of {{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1797|ja|1797th year|qq=draft}}) by {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進|qq=draft}}. :: Inari Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|宇迦之御魂神|1=Ukanomitama|2=ja|3=ウカノミタマ|lt=Ukano Mitama-no-Kami|lt_ja=うかのみたまのかみ|qq=draft}}. Founded in February of the 11th year of Kansei ({{ill|1799|ja|1799th year|qq=draft}}) by Nagai Naotoshi. :: Iwajinsha – Enshrined deities: (i) Yachimata Hikonami, (ii) Yachimata Hime, and (iii) Kunado-no-Kami. Founded in May of the 4th year of {{ill|Genbun|ja|元文|1=Genbun|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1739|ja|1739th year|qq=draft}}) by {{ill|Nagai Ryunosuke|ja|永井流之助|qq=draft}}. :: Sorei Shrine – Enshrined are the successive chief priests, shrine priests, and parishioners who have devoted themselves to the shrine. Founded in {{ill|1878|ja|1878th year|qq=draft}} (the 11th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft}}). * {{ill|Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine|ja|高槻えびす神社|qq=draft}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu|ja|蛭子大神|qq=draft}}. It was established in {{ill|1956|ja|1956th year|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft}} 31) as a branch from {{ill|Nishinomiya Shrine|ja|西宮神社|qq=draft}}, and the current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1966|ja|1966th year|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft}} 41). : Takatsuki Ebisu Festival – January 10 (subdivided into the 9th: Evening Ebisu, 10th: Main Ebisu, 11th: Remaining Fortune) * {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|qq=draft}} – Enshrined are the war dead of Takatsuki who perished in the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|ja|西南戦争|qq=draft}}, {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Second Sino-Japanese War|ja|日中戦争|qq=draft}}, and the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争|qq=draft}}. : Annual Festival: Third Sunday of May Nomijinja02.jpg|Auxiliary Shrine Nagai Shrine Karamon Tessen.png|Nagai Shrine {{ill|Family crest|ja|家紋|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Takatsuki Domain|ja|高槻藩|qq=draft}} lord, {{ill|Nagai family|ja|永井氏|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Family crest|ja|家紋|qq=draft}}) == Festivals == * New Year’s Festival – January 1 * {{ill|Tondo Festival|ja|とんど|qq=draft}} – January 15 * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|qq=draft}} – February 3 * Dedication {{ill|Crying Sumo|ja|泣き相撲|qq=draft}} – April 29 * Great Purification of Summer Crossing (Chinowa Ritual) – June 30 * Autumn Grand Festival – Second Monday of October * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft}} – November 23 * Year-End Great Purification – December 31 == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Takatsuki City === * Nagai Shrine buildings (main hall, haiden) with attached items: 4 ridge plaques and 1 Takatsuki Castle ema – Designated on June 14, {{ill|2005|ja|2005th year|qq=draft}} (Heisei 17). * Nagai Shrine Karamon – Designated on June 14, 2005 (Heisei 17). * Seventeen items of materials related to the traditional Nagai Naotoki of Nagai Shrine – Deposited at {{ill|Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|ja|高槻市立しろあと歴史館|qq=draft}}. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Catholic Takatsuki Church|ja|カトリック高槻教会|qq=draft}} (adjacent) * Japan Kurarechian Association Headquarters Monastery * Maria Inmaculada Kindergarten * {{ill|Takatsuki Modern Theater|ja|高槻現代劇場|qq=draft}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|ja|高槻市立しろあと歴史館|qq=draft}} * The ruins of {{ill|Takatsuki Castle|ja|高槻城|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Castle Ruins Park|ja|城跡公園 (高槻市)|qq=draft}}, and the {{ill|Takatsuki City Historical Folk Museum|ja|高槻市立歴史民俗資料館|qq=draft}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.nomijinja.jp/ Nomijinja] (Official website) * {{Cite web|和書|year=2012 |url=http://www.city.takatsuki.osaka.jp/rekishi_kanko/rekishi/rekishikan/jidai/shaji/1327659158743.html |title=Nomijinja |work=インターネット歴史館 |publisher=Takatsuki City|accessdate=2014-12-13}} * {{Cite web|和書|year=2012 |url=http://www.city.takatsuki.osaka.jp/rekishi_kanko/rekishi/rekishikan/jidai/shaji/1327671298593.html |title=Revived "Nagai Shrine Karamon" |work=インターネット歴史館 |publisher=Takatsuki City|accessdate=2014-12-13}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|野見神社|version=54014655|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Takatsuki City]] [[Category:History of Takatsuki City]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha of Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Gion Shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Iwanaga Faith]] }} [[ja:野見神社]]

Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=18|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|frame-height=300|coord={{coord|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft}}{{ill|Takatsuki City|ja|高槻市|qq=draft}} Nomimachi 6-6 |Coordinates = {{wikicoordinates|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命|qq=draft}}

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft}} shrine, and its former Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft}}. == History == In the late 9th century, during the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇|qq=draft}} ({{ill|887|ja|887th year|qq=draft}} – {{ill|897|ja|897th year|qq=draft}}), an epidemic spread throughout the region. Later, in the 5th year of the {{ill|Genna|ja|元和|qq=draft}} era ({{ill|1619|ja|1619th year|qq=draft}}), the lord of Takatsuki Castle, {{ill|Matsudaira Ienobu|ja|松平家信|qq=draft}}, rebuilt the shrine building.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e5%be%a1%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92-%e8%a1%8c%e4%ba%8b.html |title=野見神社 御由緒・年間行事 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} In {{ill|1868|ja|1868th year|qq=draft}} (the first year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft}}), due to the {{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft}}, the name of the enshrined deity was changed to {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命|qq=draft}}, and furthermore, {{ill|Nomitatsune|ja|野見宿禰|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=野見宿禰命|qq=draft}} was enshrined alongside, prompting the shrine to be renamed “Nomijinja”. Consequently, although it is counted as one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft}} shrines listed in the {{ill|Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft}}, the publication “Historic Sites, Famous Landscapes, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture” asserts that the renaming to “Nomijinja”—by enshrining Nomitatsune to emulate a Shikinaisha—meant that its descendant shrine is the auxiliary shrine {{ill|Nomigami Shrine|ja|野身神社|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Kamigu Tenmangu|ja|上宮天満宮|qq=draft}} in Takatsuki City. Founded in the 5th year of the {{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政|qq=draft}} era ({{ill|1793|ja|1793rd year|qq=draft}}) by the 9th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進|qq=draft}}. Initially called “Naotoki Shrine”, it was referred to as “Naotoki Gongen” during the late Edo period. In the first year of the {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永|qq=draft}} era ({{ill|1848|ja|1848th year|qq=draft}}), during the time of the 11th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naoteru|ja|永井直輝|qq=draft}}, the main hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Takatsuki City) was restored, and the haiden (also a designated tangible cultural property) along with the karamon (quadrangular gate, designated tangible cultural property) were established. The karamon was repaired in {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year|qq=draft}} (the 22nd year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft}}). : Annual Festival: April 24{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e6%91%82%e6%9c%ab%e7%a4%be.html |title=野見神社 - 摂末社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} * {{ill|Kojima Shrine|ja|小島神社|qq=draft}} – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いちきしまひめのみこと|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=おほなむちのみこと|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Iwanagahime|ja|磐長姫命|lt=Iwanagahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いわながひめのみこと|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ameno Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=あめのこやねのみこと|qq=draft}}. Its founding date is unknown; the shrine building was newly constructed on October 1, {{ill|2021|ja|2021st year|qq=draft}} (the 3rd year of {{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和|qq=draft}}). : Annual Festival: Second Sunday of October (Nomijinja Autumn Evening Festival) * Shishamyojin – In the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正|qq=draft}} the separate shrines of Fukujinja, Inari Shrine, Iwajinsha, and Sorei Shrine were merged into one. : Annual Festival: April 11 :: Fukujinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Shinatsuhikokami|ja|志那都比古神|lt=Shinatsuhikokami|lt_ja=しなつひこのかみ|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shinatsuhime|ja|志那都姫神|lt=Shinatsuhime|lt_ja=しなつひめのかみ|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Homusubi|ja|火産霊|lt=Homusubi|lt_ja=ほむすび|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|ja|金山比古神|lt=Kanayamahikokami|lt_ja=かなやまひこのかみ|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|ja|金山姫神|lt=Kanayamahime|lt_ja=かなやまひめのかみ|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Haniyamahikokami|ja|埴山比古神|lt=Haniyamahikokami|lt_ja=はにやまひこのかみ|qq=draft}} (linked as {{ill|Haniyamas|ja|ハニヤス|qq=draft}} with display 埴山比古神), and {{ill|Mizuhanomenokami|ja|弥都波能売神|lt=Mizuhanomenokami|lt_ja=みづはのめのかみ|qq=draft}}. Founded in August of the 9th year of {{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1797|ja|1797th year|qq=draft}}) by {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進|qq=draft}}. :: Inari Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|宇迦之御魂神|1=Ukanomitama|2=ja|3=ウカノミタマ|lt=Ukano Mitama-no-Kami|lt_ja=うかのみたまのかみ|qq=draft}}. Founded in February of the 11th year of Kansei ({{ill|1799|ja|1799th year|qq=draft}}) by Nagai Naotoshi. :: Iwajinsha – Enshrined deities: (i) Yachimata Hikonami, (ii) Yachimata Hime, and (iii) Kunado-no-Kami. Founded in May of the 4th year of {{ill|Genbun|ja|元文|1=Genbun|qq=draft}} ({{ill|1739|ja|1739th year|qq=draft}}) by {{ill|Nagai Ryunosuke|ja|永井流之助|qq=draft}}. :: Sorei Shrine – Enshrined are the successive chief priests, shrine priests, and parishioners who have devoted themselves to the shrine. Founded in {{ill|1878|ja|1878th year|qq=draft}} (the 11th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft}}). * {{ill|Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine|ja|高槻えびす神社|qq=draft}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu|ja|蛭子大神|qq=draft}}. It was established in {{ill|1956|ja|1956th year|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft}} 31) as a branch from {{ill|Nishinomiya Shrine|ja|西宮神社|qq=draft}}, and the current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1966|ja|1966th year|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft}} 41). : Takatsuki Ebisu Festival – January 10 (subdivided into the 9th: Evening Ebisu, 10th: Main Ebisu, 11th: Remaining Fortune) * {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|qq=draft}} – Enshrined are the war dead of Takatsuki who perished in the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|ja|西南戦争|qq=draft}}, {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Second Sino-Japanese War|ja|日中戦争|qq=draft}}, and the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争|qq=draft}}. : Annual Festival: Third Sunday of May Nomijinja02.jpg|Auxiliary Shrine Nagai Shrine Karamon Tessen.png|Nagai Shrine {{ill|Family crest|ja|家紋|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Takatsuki Domain|ja|高槻藩|qq=draft}} lord, {{ill|Nagai family|ja|永井氏|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Family crest|ja|家紋|qq=draft}}) == Festivals == * New Year’s Festival – January 1 * {{ill|Tondo Festival|ja|とんど|qq=draft}} – January 15 * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|qq=draft}} – February 3 * Dedication {{ill|Crying Sumo|ja|泣き相撲|qq=draft}} – April 29 * Great Purification of Summer Crossing (Chinowa Ritual) – June 30 * Autumn Grand Festival – Second Monday of October * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft}} – November 23 * Year-End Great Purification – December 31 == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Takatsuki City === * Nagai Shrine buildings (main hall, haiden) with attached items: 4 ridge plaques and 1 Takatsuki Castle ema – Designated on June 14, {{ill|2005|ja|2005th year|qq=draft}} (Heisei 17). * Nagai Shrine Karamon – Designated on June 14, 2005 (Heisei 17). * Seventeen items of materials related to the traditional Nagai Naotoki of Nagai Shrine – Deposited at {{ill|Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|ja|高槻市立しろあと歴史館|qq=draft}}. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Catholic Takatsuki Church|ja|カトリック高槻教会|qq=draft}} (adjacent) * Japan Kurarechian Association Headquarters Monastery * Maria Inmaculada Kindergarten * {{ill|Takatsuki Modern Theater|ja|高槻現代劇場|qq=draft}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|ja|高槻市立しろあと歴史館|qq=draft}} * The ruins of {{ill|Takatsuki Castle|ja|高槻城|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Castle Ruins Park|ja|城跡公園 (高槻市)|qq=draft}}, and the {{ill|Takatsuki City Historical Folk Museum|ja|高槻市立歴史民俗資料館|qq=draft}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.nomijinja.jp/ Nomijinja] (Official website) * {{Cite web|和書|year=2012 |url=http://www.city.takatsuki.osaka.jp/rekishi_kanko/rekishi/rekishikan/jidai/shaji/1327659158743.html |title=Nomijinja |work=インターネット歴史館 |publisher=Takatsuki City|accessdate=2014-12-13}} * {{Cite web|和書|year=2012 |url=http://www.city.takatsuki.osaka.jp/rekishi_kanko/rekishi/rekishikan/jidai/shaji/1327671298593.html |title=Revived "Nagai Shrine Karamon" |work=インターネット歴史館 |publisher=Takatsuki City|accessdate=2014-12-13}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|野見神社|version=54014655|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Takatsuki City]] [[Category:History of Takatsuki City]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Gion Shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Iwanaga Faith]] }} [[ja:野見神社]]

revision.text:
{{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = '''Nomijinja''' (野見神社) |image = [[File:Nomijinja01.jpg|300px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=18|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|frame-height=300|coord={{coord|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Takatsuki City|ja|高槻市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki City|qq=}} Nomimachi 6-6 |Coordinates = {{wikicoordinates|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Nomitatsune|ja|野見宿禰|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=野見宿禰命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomitatsune|qq=}} |Founding = Late 9th century |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (small), former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} |Annual Festival = Second Monday of October }} '''Nomijinja''' (のみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in Nomimachi, {{ill|Takatsuki City|ja|高槻市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} shrine, and its former Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. == History == In the late 9th century, during the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}} of {{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Uda|qq=}} ({{ill|887|ja|887th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/887|qq=}} – {{ill|897|ja|897th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/897|qq=}}), an epidemic spread throughout the region. Following a divine oracle, {{ill|Gozutennō|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|lt=Gozutennō|lt_ja=ごずてんのう|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozutennō|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozu Tennō|qq=}} (identified with {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}) was enshrined, and as the epidemic subsided, a shrine building was constructed, establishing the Gozutennō Shrine. In the late 10th century, when {{ill|Takatsuki Castle|ja|高槻城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Castle|qq=}} was constructed, the shrine was venerated as a guardian of the castle.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/ |title=高槻城内守護 野見神社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} However, in the 16th century when {{ill|Takayama Ukon|ja|高山右近|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama Ukon|qq=}} became the lord of Takatsuki Castle, his adherence to {{ill|Christianity|ja|キリスト教|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Christianity|qq=}} led him to destroy the shrine building within the castle and confiscate its estate. Later, in the 5th year of the {{ill|Genna|ja|元和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genna|qq=}} era ({{ill|1619|ja|1619th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1619|qq=}}), the lord of Takatsuki Castle, {{ill|Matsudaira Ienobu|ja|松平家信|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsudaira Ienobu|qq=}}, rebuilt the shrine building.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e5%be%a1%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92-%e8%a1%8c%e4%ba%8b.html |title=野見神社 御由緒・年間行事 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} In {{ill|1868|ja|1868th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1868|qq=}} (the first year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}), due to the {{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|qq=}}, the name of the enshrined deity was changed to {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, and furthermore, {{ill|Nomitatsune|ja|野見宿禰|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=野見宿禰命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomitatsune|qq=}} was enshrined alongside, prompting the shrine to be renamed “Nomijinja”. Consequently, although it is counted as one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} shrines listed in the {{ill|Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, the publication “Historic Sites, Famous Landscapes, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture” asserts that the renaming to “Nomijinja”—by enshrining Nomitatsune to emulate a Shikinaisha—meant that its descendant shrine is the auxiliary shrine {{ill|Nomigami Shrine|ja|野身神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomigami Shrine|qq=}} of {{ill|Kamigu Tenmangu|ja|上宮天満宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigu Tenmangu|qq=}} in Takatsuki City. In {{ill|1871|ja|1871st year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1871|qq=}} (the 4th year of Meiji), it was classified as a prefectural shrine ({{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}) as a Shikinaisha; however, in November of the following year, {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1872|qq=}} (the 5th year of Meiji), it was demoted to a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}) for the reasons mentioned above. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities – {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Nomitatsune|ja|野見宿禰|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=野見宿禰命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomitatsune|qq=}} == Shrine Grounds == === Shrine Buildings === * Main Hall – Rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Tenwa|ja|天和 (日本)|lt=Tenwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenwa|qq=}} ({{ill|1682|ja|1682nd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1682|qq=}}). * Haiden – Renovated in {{ill|2003|ja|2003rd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2003|qq=}} (the 15th year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei|qq=}}). * Noh Stage * Shrine Office === Auxiliary Shrines === * {{ill|Nagai Shrine|ja|永井神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Shrine|qq=}} – Enshrined deity: the first lord of {{ill|Takatsuki Domain|ja|高槻藩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Domain|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagai Naotoki|ja|永井直清|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naotoki|qq=}}. Founded in the 5th year of the {{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei|qq=}} era ({{ill|1793|ja|1793rd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1793|qq=}}) by the 9th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naotoshi|qq=}}. Initially called “Naotoki Shrine”, it was referred to as “Naotoki Gongen” during the late Edo period. In the first year of the {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaei|qq=}} era ({{ill|1848|ja|1848th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1848|qq=}}), during the time of the 11th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naoteru|ja|永井直輝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naoteru|qq=}}, the main hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Takatsuki City) was restored, and the haiden (also a designated tangible cultural property) along with the karamon (quadrangular gate, designated tangible cultural property) were established. The karamon was repaired in {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2010|qq=}} (the 22nd year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei|qq=}}). : Annual Festival: April 24{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e6%91%82%e6%9c%ab%e7%a4%be.html |title=野見神社 - 摂末社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} * {{ill|Kojima Shrine|ja|小島神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Shrine|qq=}} – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いちきしまひめのみこと|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=おほなむちのみこと|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Iwanagahime|ja|磐長姫命|lt=Iwanagahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いわながひめのみこと|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanagahime|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ameno Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=あめのこやねのみこと|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno Koyane|qq=}}. Its founding date is unknown; the shrine building was newly constructed on October 1, {{ill|2021|ja|2021st year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2021|qq=}} (the 3rd year of {{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiwa|qq=}}). : Annual Festival: Second Sunday of October (Nomijinja Autumn Evening Festival) * Shishamyojin – In the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō period|qq=}} the separate shrines of Fukujinja, Inari Shrine, Iwajinsha, and Sorei Shrine were merged into one. : Annual Festival: April 11 :: Fukujinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Shinatsuhikokami|ja|志那都比古神|lt=Shinatsuhikokami|lt_ja=しなつひこのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhikokami|qq=}}, {{ill|Shinatsuhime|ja|志那都姫神|lt=Shinatsuhime|lt_ja=しなつひめのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Homusubi|ja|火産霊|lt=Homusubi|lt_ja=ほむすび|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homusubi|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|ja|金山比古神|lt=Kanayamahikokami|lt_ja=かなやまひこのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|ja|金山姫神|lt=Kanayamahime|lt_ja=かなやまひめのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Haniyamahikokami|ja|埴山比古神|lt=Haniyamahikokami|lt_ja=はにやまひこのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyamahikokami|qq=}} (linked as {{ill|Haniyamas|ja|ハニヤス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyamas|qq=}} with display 埴山比古神), and {{ill|Mizuhanomenokami|ja|弥都波能売神|lt=Mizuhanomenokami|lt_ja=みづはのめのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanomenokami|qq=}}. Founded in August of the 9th year of {{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei|qq=}} ({{ill|1797|ja|1797th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1797|qq=}}) by {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naotoshi|qq=}}. :: Inari Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|宇迦之御魂神|1=Ukanomitama|2=ja|3=ウカノミタマ|lt=Ukano Mitama-no-Kami|lt_ja=うかのみたまのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}. Founded in February of the 11th year of Kansei ({{ill|1799|ja|1799th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1799|qq=}}) by Nagai Naotoshi. :: Iwajinsha – Enshrined deities: (i) Yachimata Hikonami, (ii) Yachimata Hime, and (iii) Kunado-no-Kami. Founded in May of the 4th year of {{ill|Genbun|ja|元文|1=Genbun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genbun|13=User:Immanuelle/Genbun|qq=}} ({{ill|1739|ja|1739th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1739|qq=}}) by {{ill|Nagai Ryunosuke|ja|永井流之助|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Ryunosuke|qq=}}. :: Sorei Shrine – Enshrined are the successive chief priests, shrine priests, and parishioners who have devoted themselves to the shrine. Founded in {{ill|1878|ja|1878th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1878|qq=}} (the 11th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}). * {{ill|Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine|ja|高槻えびす神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine|qq=}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu|ja|蛭子大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=}}. It was established in {{ill|1956|ja|1956th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1956|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 31) as a branch from {{ill|Nishinomiya Shrine|ja|西宮神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya Shrine|qq=}}, and the current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1966|ja|1966th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1966|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 41). : Takatsuki Ebisu Festival – January 10 (subdivided into the 9th: Evening Ebisu, 10th: Main Ebisu, 11th: Remaining Fortune) * {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} – Enshrined are the war dead of Takatsuki who perished in the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|ja|西南戦争|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satsuma Rebellion|qq=}}, {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/First Sino-Japanese War|qq=}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Russo-Japanese War|qq=}}, {{ill|Second Sino-Japanese War|ja|日中戦争|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Sino-Japanese War|qq=}}, and the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacific War|qq=}}. : Annual Festival: Third Sunday of May Nomijinja02.jpg|Auxiliary Shrine Nagai Shrine Karamon Tessen.png|Nagai Shrine {{ill|Family crest|ja|家紋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Family crest|qq=}} ({{ill|Takatsuki Domain|ja|高槻藩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Domain|qq=}} lord, {{ill|Nagai family|ja|永井氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai family|qq=}}, {{ill|Family crest|ja|家紋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Family crest|qq=}}) == Festivals == * New Year’s Festival – January 1 * {{ill|Tondo Festival|ja|とんど|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tondo Festival|qq=}} – January 15 * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|qq=}} – February 3 * Dedication {{ill|Crying Sumo|ja|泣き相撲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Crying Sumo|qq=}} – April 29 * Great Purification of Summer Crossing (Chinowa Ritual) – June 30 * Autumn Grand Festival – Second Monday of October * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiname-sai|qq=}} – November 23 * Year-End Great Purification – December 31 == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Takatsuki City === * Nagai Shrine buildings (main hall, haiden) with attached items: 4 ridge plaques and 1 Takatsuki Castle ema – Designated on June 14, {{ill|2005|ja|2005th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2005|qq=}} (Heisei 17). * Nagai Shrine Karamon – Designated on June 14, 2005 (Heisei 17). * Seventeen items of materials related to the traditional Nagai Naotoki of Nagai Shrine – Deposited at {{ill|Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|ja|高槻市立しろあと歴史館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|qq=}}. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Catholic Takatsuki Church|ja|カトリック高槻教会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Catholic Takatsuki Church|qq=}} (adjacent) * Japan Kurarechian Association Headquarters Monastery * Maria Inmaculada Kindergarten * {{ill|Takatsuki Modern Theater|ja|高槻現代劇場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Modern Theater|qq=}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|ja|高槻市立しろあと歴史館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|qq=}} * The ruins of {{ill|Takatsuki Castle|ja|高槻城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Castle|qq=}}, {{ill|Castle Ruins Park|ja|城跡公園 (高槻市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Castle Ruins Park|qq=}}, and the {{ill|Takatsuki City Historical Folk Museum|ja|高槻市立歴史民俗資料館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki City Historical Folk Museum|qq=}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.nomijinja.jp/ Nomijinja] |publisher=Takatsuki City|accessdate=2014-12-13}} * {{Cite web|和書|year=2012 |url=http://www.city.takatsuki.osaka.jp/rekishi_kanko/rekishi/rekishikan/jidai/shaji/1327671298593.html |title=Revived {{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = '''Nomijinja''' (野見神社) |image = [[File:Nomijinja01.jpg|300px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=18|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|frame-height=300|coord={{coord|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Takatsuki City|ja|高槻市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki City|qq=}} Nomimachi 6-6 |Coordinates = {{wikicoordinates|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Nomitatsune|ja|野見宿禰|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=野見宿禰命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomitatsune|qq=}} |Founding = Late 9th century |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (small), former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} |Annual Festival = Second Monday of October }} '''Nomijinja''' (のみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in Nomimachi, {{ill|Takatsuki City|ja|高槻市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} shrine, and its former Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. == History == In the late 9th century, during the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}} of {{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Uda|qq=}} ({{ill|887|ja|887th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/887|qq=}} – {{ill|897|ja|897th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/897|qq=}}), an epidemic spread throughout the region. Following a divine oracle, {{ill|Gozutennō|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|lt=Gozutennō|lt_ja=ごずてんのう|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozutennō|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozu Tennō|qq=}} (identified with {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}) was enshrined, and as the epidemic subsided, a shrine building was constructed, establishing the Gozutennō Shrine. In the late 10th century, when {{ill|Takatsuki Castle|ja|高槻城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Castle|qq=}} was constructed, the shrine was venerated as a guardian of the castle.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/ |title=高槻城内守護 野見神社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} However, in the 16th century when {{ill|Takayama Ukon|ja|高山右近|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama Ukon|qq=}} became the lord of Takatsuki Castle, his adherence to {{ill|Christianity|ja|キリスト教|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Christianity|qq=}} led him to destroy the shrine building within the castle and confiscate its estate. Later, in the 5th year of the {{ill|Genna|ja|元和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genna|qq=}} era ({{ill|1619|ja|1619th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1619|qq=}}), the lord of Takatsuki Castle, {{ill|Matsudaira Ienobu|ja|松平家信|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsudaira Ienobu|qq=}}, rebuilt the shrine building.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e5%be%a1%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92-%e8%a1%8c%e4%ba%8b.html |title=野見神社 御由緒・年間行事 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} In {{ill|1868|ja|1868th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1868|qq=}} (the first year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}), due to the {{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|qq=}}, the name of the enshrined deity was changed to {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, and furthermore, {{ill|Nomitatsune|ja|野見宿禰|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=野見宿禰命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomitatsune|qq=}} was enshrined alongside, prompting the shrine to be renamed “Nomijinja”. Consequently, although it is counted as one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} shrines listed in the {{ill|Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, the publication “Historic Sites, Famous Landscapes, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture” asserts that the renaming to “Nomijinja”—by enshrining Nomitatsune to emulate a Shikinaisha—meant that its descendant shrine is the auxiliary shrine {{ill|Nomigami Shrine|ja|野身神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomigami Shrine|qq=}} of {{ill|Kamigu Tenmangu|ja|上宮天満宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigu Tenmangu|qq=}} in Takatsuki City. In {{ill|1871|ja|1871st year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1871|qq=}} (the 4th year of Meiji), it was classified as a prefectural shrine ({{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}) as a Shikinaisha; however, in November of the following year, {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1872|qq=}} (the 5th year of Meiji), it was demoted to a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}) for the reasons mentioned above. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities – {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Nomitatsune|ja|野見宿禰|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=野見宿禰命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomitatsune|qq=}} == Shrine Grounds == === Shrine Buildings === * Main Hall – Rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Tenwa|ja|天和 (日本)|lt=Tenwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenwa|qq=}} ({{ill|1682|ja|1682nd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1682|qq=}}). * Haiden – Renovated in {{ill|2003|ja|2003rd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2003|qq=}} (the 15th year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei|qq=}}). * Noh Stage * Shrine Office === Auxiliary Shrines === * {{ill|Nagai Shrine|ja|永井神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Shrine|qq=}} – Enshrined deity: the first lord of {{ill|Takatsuki Domain|ja|高槻藩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Domain|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagai Naotoki|ja|永井直清|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naotoki|qq=}}. Founded in the 5th year of the {{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei|qq=}} era ({{ill|1793|ja|1793rd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1793|qq=}}) by the 9th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naotoshi|qq=}}. Initially called “Naotoki Shrine”, it was referred to as “Naotoki Gongen” during the late Edo period. In the first year of the {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaei|qq=}} era ({{ill|1848|ja|1848th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1848|qq=}}), during the time of the 11th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naoteru|ja|永井直輝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naoteru|qq=}}, the main hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Takatsuki City) was restored, and the haiden (also a designated tangible cultural property) along with the karamon (quadrangular gate, designated tangible cultural property) were established. The karamon was repaired in {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2010|qq=}} (the 22nd year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei|qq=}}). : Annual Festival: April 24{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e6%91%82%e6%9c%ab%e7%a4%be.html |title=野見神社 - 摂末社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} * {{ill|Kojima Shrine|ja|小島神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Shrine|qq=}} – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いちきしまひめのみこと|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=おほなむちのみこと|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Iwanagahime|ja|磐長姫命|lt=Iwanagahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いわながひめのみこと|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanagahime|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ameno Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=あめのこやねのみこと|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno Koyane|qq=}}. Its founding date is unknown; the shrine building was newly constructed on October 1, {{ill|2021|ja|2021st year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2021|qq=}} (the 3rd year of {{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiwa|qq=}}). : Annual Festival: Second Sunday of October (Nomijinja Autumn Evening Festival) * Shishamyojin – In the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō period|qq=}} the separate shrines of Fukujinja, Inari Shrine, Iwajinsha, and Sorei Shrine were merged into one. : Annual Festival: April 11 :: Fukujinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Shinatsuhikokami|ja|志那都比古神|lt=Shinatsuhikokami|lt_ja=しなつひこのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhikokami|qq=}}, {{ill|Shinatsuhime|ja|志那都姫神|lt=Shinatsuhime|lt_ja=しなつひめのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Homusubi|ja|火産霊|lt=Homusubi|lt_ja=ほむすび|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homusubi|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|ja|金山比古神|lt=Kanayamahikokami|lt_ja=かなやまひこのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|ja|金山姫神|lt=Kanayamahime|lt_ja=かなやまひめのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Haniyamahikokami|ja|埴山比古神|lt=Haniyamahikokami|lt_ja=はにやまひこのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyamahikokami|qq=}} (linked as {{ill|Haniyamas|ja|ハニヤス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyamas|qq=}} with display 埴山比古神), and {{ill|Mizuhanomenokami|ja|弥都波能売神|lt=Mizuhanomenokami|lt_ja=みづはのめのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanomenokami|qq=}}. Founded in August of the 9th year of {{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei|qq=}} ({{ill|1797|ja|1797th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1797|qq=}}) by {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naotoshi|qq=}}. :: Inari Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|宇迦之御魂神|1=Ukanomitama|2=ja|3=ウカノミタマ|lt=Ukano Mitama-no-Kami|lt_ja=うかのみたまのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}. Founded in February of the 11th year of Kansei ({{ill|1799|ja|1799th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1799|qq=}}) by Nagai Naotoshi. :: Iwajinsha – Enshrined deities: (i) Yachimata Hikonami, (ii) Yachimata Hime, and (iii) Kunado-no-Kami. Founded in May of the 4th year of {{ill|Genbun|ja|元文|1=Genbun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genbun|13=User:Immanuelle/Genbun|qq=}} ({{ill|1739|ja|1739th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1739|qq=}}) by {{ill|Nagai Ryunosuke|ja|永井流之助|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Ryunosuke|qq=}}. :: Sorei Shrine – Enshrined are the successive chief priests, shrine priests, and parishioners who have devoted themselves to the shrine. Founded in {{ill|1878|ja|1878th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1878|qq=}} (the 11th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}). * {{ill|Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine|ja|高槻えびす神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine|qq=}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu|ja|蛭子大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=}}. It was established in {{ill|1956|ja|1956th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1956|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 31) as a branch from {{ill|Nishinomiya Shrine|ja|西宮神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya Shrine|qq=}}, and the current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1966|ja|1966th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1966|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 41). : Takatsuki Ebisu Festival – January 10 (subdivided into the 9th: Evening Ebisu, 10th: Main Ebisu, 11th: Remaining Fortune) * {{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} – Enshrined are the war dead of Takatsuki who perished in the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|ja|西南戦争|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satsuma Rebellion|qq=}}, {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/First Sino-Japanese War|qq=}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Russo-Japanese War|qq=}}, {{ill|Second Sino-Japanese War|ja|日中戦争|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Sino-Japanese War|qq=}}, and the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacific War|qq=}}. : Annual Festival: Third Sunday of May Nomijinja02.jpg|Auxiliary Shrine Nagai Shrine Karamon Tessen.png|Nagai Shrine {{ill|Family crest|ja|家紋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Family crest|qq=}} ({{ill|Takatsuki Domain|ja|高槻藩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Domain|qq=}} lord, {{ill|Nagai family|ja|永井氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai family|qq=}}, {{ill|Family crest|ja|家紋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Family crest|qq=}}) == Festivals == * New Year’s Festival – January 1 * {{ill|Tondo Festival|ja|とんど|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tondo Festival|qq=}} – January 15 * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|qq=}} – February 3 * Dedication {{ill|Crying Sumo|ja|泣き相撲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Crying Sumo|qq=}} – April 29 * Great Purification of Summer Crossing (Chinowa Ritual) – June 30 * Autumn Grand Festival – Second Monday of October * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiname-sai|qq=}} – November 23 * Year-End Great Purification – December 31 == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Takatsuki City === * Nagai Shrine buildings (main hall, haiden) with attached items: 4 ridge plaques and 1 Takatsuki Castle ema – Designated on June 14, {{ill|2005|ja|2005th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2005|qq=}} (Heisei 17). * Nagai Shrine Karamon – Designated on June 14, 2005 (Heisei 17). * Seventeen items of materials related to the traditional Nagai Naotoki of Nagai Shrine – Deposited at {{ill|Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|ja|高槻市立しろあと歴史館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|qq=}}. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Catholic Takatsuki {{ill|Takatsuki Modern Theater|ja|高槻現代劇場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Modern Theater|qq=}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|ja|高槻市立しろあと歴史館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|qq=}} * The ruins of {{ill|Takatsuki Castle|ja|高槻城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Museum|ja|高槻市立歴史民俗資料館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki City Historical Folk Museum|qq=}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.nomijinja.jp/ Nomijinja] {{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = '''Nomijinja''' (野見神社) |image = [[File:Nomijinja01.jpg|300px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=18|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|frame-height=300|coord={{coord|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Takatsuki City|ja|高槻市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki City|qq=}} Nomimachi 6-6 |Coordinates = {{wikicoordinates|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}
{{ill|Nomitatsune|ja|野見宿禰|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=野見宿禰命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomitatsune|qq=}} |Founding = Late 9th century |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (small), former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} |Annual Festival = Second Monday of October }} '''Nomijinja''' (のみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in Nomimachi, {{ill|Takatsuki City|ja|高槻市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka Prefecture|qq=}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} shrine, and its former Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}. == History == In the late 9th century, during the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}} of {{ill|Emperor Uda|ja|宇多天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Uda|qq=}} ({{ill|887|ja|887th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/887|qq=}} – {{ill|897|ja|897th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/897|qq=}}), an epidemic spread throughout the region. Following a divine oracle, {{ill|Gozutennō|ja|牛頭天王|1=Gozu Tennō|lt=Gozutennō|lt_ja=ごずてんのう|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozutennō|13=User:Immanuelle/Gozu Tennō|qq=}} (identified with {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}) was enshrined, and as the epidemic subsided, a shrine building was constructed, establishing the Gozutennō Shrine. In the late 10th century, when {{ill|Takatsuki Castle|ja|高槻城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Castle|qq=}} was constructed, the shrine was venerated as a guardian of the castle.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/ |title=高槻城内守護 野見神社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} However, in the 16th century when {{ill|Takayama Ukon|ja|高山右近|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama Ukon|qq=}} became the lord of Takatsuki Castle, his adherence to {{ill|Christianity|ja|キリスト教|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Christianity|qq=}} led him to destroy the shrine building within the castle and confiscate its estate. Later, in the 5th year of the {{ill|Genna|ja|元和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genna|qq=}} era ({{ill|1619|ja|1619th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1619|qq=}}), the lord of Takatsuki Castle, {{ill|Matsudaira Ienobu|ja|松平家信|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsudaira Ienobu|qq=}}, rebuilt the shrine building.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e5%be%a1%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92-%e8%a1%8c%e4%ba%8b.html |title=野見神社 御由緒・年間行事 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} In {{ill|1868|ja|1868th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1868|qq=}} (the first year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}), due to the {{ill|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|ja|神仏分離|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Separation of Shinto and Buddhism|qq=}}, the name of the enshrined deity was changed to {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, and furthermore, {{ill|Nomitatsune|ja|野見宿禰|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=野見宿禰命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomitatsune|qq=}} was enshrined alongside, prompting the shrine to be renamed “Nomijinja”. Consequently, although it is counted as one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} shrines listed in the {{ill|Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, the publication “Historic Sites, Famous Landscapes, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture” asserts that the renaming to “Nomijinja”—by enshrining Nomitatsune to emulate a Shikinaisha—meant that its descendant shrine is the auxiliary shrine {{ill|Nomigami Shrine|ja|野身神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomigami Shrine|qq=}} of {{ill|Kamigu Tenmangu|ja|上宮天満宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigu Tenmangu|qq=}} in Takatsuki City. In {{ill|1871|ja|1871st year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1871|qq=}} (the 4th year of Meiji), it was classified as a prefectural shrine ({{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}) as a Shikinaisha; however, in November of the following year, {{ill|1872|ja|1872nd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1872|qq=}} (the 5th year of Meiji), it was demoted to a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}}) for the reasons mentioned above. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities – {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオ|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=須佐之男命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Nomitatsune|ja|野見宿禰|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=野見宿禰命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomitatsune|qq=}} == Shrine Grounds == === Shrine Buildings === * Main Hall – Rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Tenwa|ja|天和 (日本)|lt=Tenwa|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenwa|qq=}} ({{ill|1682|ja|1682nd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1682|qq=}}). * Haiden – Renovated in {{ill|2003|ja|2003rd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2003|qq=}} (the 15th year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei|qq=}}). * Noh Stage * Shrine Office === Auxiliary Shrines === * {{ill|Nagai Shrine|ja|永井神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Shrine|qq=}} – Enshrined deity: the first lord of {{ill|Takatsuki Domain|ja|高槻藩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Domain|qq=}}, {{ill|Nagai Naotoki|ja|永井直清|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naotoki|qq=}}. Founded in the 5th year of the {{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei|qq=}} era ({{ill|1793|ja|1793rd year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1793|qq=}}) by the 9th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naotoshi|qq=}}. Initially called “Naotoki Shrine”, it was referred to as “Naotoki Gongen” during the late Edo period. In the first year of the {{ill|Kaei|ja|嘉永|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaei|qq=}} era ({{ill|1848|ja|1848th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1848|qq=}}), during the time of the 11th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naoteru|ja|永井直輝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naoteru|qq=}}, the main hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Takatsuki City) was restored, and the haiden (also a designated tangible cultural property) along with the karamon (quadrangular gate, designated tangible cultural property) were established. The karamon was repaired in {{ill|2010|ja|2010th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2010|qq=}} (the 22nd year of {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei|qq=}}). : Annual Festival: April 24{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e6%91%82%e6%9c%ab%e7%a4%be.html |title=野見神社 - 摂末社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} * {{ill|Kojima Shrine|ja|小島神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Shrine|qq=}} – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いちきしまひめのみこと|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=おほなむちのみこと|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Iwanagahime|ja|磐長姫命|lt=Iwanagahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いわながひめのみこと|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanagahime|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ameno Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=あめのこやねのみこと|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno Koyane|qq=}}. Its founding date is unknown; the shrine building was newly constructed on October 1, {{ill|2021|ja|2021st year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2021|qq=}} (the 3rd year of {{ill|Reiwa|ja|令和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reiwa|qq=}}). : Annual Festival: Second Sunday of October (Nomijinja Autumn Evening Festival) * Shishamyojin – In the {{ill|Taishō period|ja|大正|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taishō period|qq=}} the separate shrines of Fukujinja, Inari Shrine, Iwajinsha, and Sorei Shrine were merged into one. : Annual Festival: April 11 :: Fukujinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Shinatsuhikokami|ja|志那都比古神|lt=Shinatsuhikokami|lt_ja=しなつひこのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhikokami|qq=}}, {{ill|Shinatsuhime|ja|志那都姫神|lt=Shinatsuhime|lt_ja=しなつひめのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhime|qq=}}, {{ill|Homusubi|ja|火産霊|lt=Homusubi|lt_ja=ほむすび|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homusubi|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|ja|金山比古神|lt=Kanayamahikokami|lt_ja=かなやまひこのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|ja|金山姫神|lt=Kanayamahime|lt_ja=かなやまひめのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Haniyamahikokami|ja|埴山比古神|lt=Haniyamahikokami|lt_ja=はにやまひこのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyamahikokami|qq=}} (linked as {{ill|Haniyamas|ja|ハニヤス|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyamas|qq=}} with display 埴山比古神), and {{ill|Mizuhanomenokami|ja|弥都波能売神|lt=Mizuhanomenokami|lt_ja=みづはのめのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanomenokami|qq=}}. Founded in August of the 9th year of {{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kansei|qq=}} ({{ill|1797|ja|1797th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1797|qq=}}) by {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naotoshi|qq=}}. :: Inari Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ukano Mitama|ja|宇迦之御魂神|1=Ukanomitama|2=ja|3=ウカノミタマ|lt=Ukano Mitama-no-Kami|lt_ja=うかのみたまのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukano Mitama|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}. Founded in February of the 11th year of Kansei ({{ill|1799|ja|1799th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1799|qq=}}) by Nagai Naotoshi. :: Iwajinsha – Enshrined deities: (i) Yachimata Hikonami, (ii) Yachimata Hime, and (iii) Kunado-no-Kami. Founded in May of the 4th year of {{ill|Genbun|ja|元文|1=Genbun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Genbun|13=User:Immanuelle/Genbun|qq=}} ({{ill|1739|ja|1739th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1739|qq=}}) by {{ill|Nagai Ryunosuke|ja|永井流之助|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Ryunosuke|qq=}}. :: Sorei Shrine – Enshrined are the successive chief priests, shrine priests, and parishioners who have devoted themselves to the shrine. Founded in {{ill|1878|ja|1878th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1878|qq=}} (the 11th year of {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}}). * {{ill|Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine|ja|高槻えびす神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine|qq=}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu|ja|蛭子大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=}}. It was established in {{ill|1956|ja|1956th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1956|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 31) as a branch from {{ill|Nishinomiya Shrine|ja|西宮神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinomiya Shrine|qq=}}, and the current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1966|ja|1966th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1966|qq=}} ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 41). : Takatsuki Ebisu Festival – January 10 (subdivided into the 9th: Evening Ebisu, 10th: Main Ebisu, 11th: Remaining Fortune) * {{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokoku Shrine|qq=}} – Enshrined are the war dead of Takatsuki who perished in the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|ja|西南戦争|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satsuma Rebellion|qq=}}, {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|ja|日清戦争|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/First Sino-Japanese War|qq=}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|ja|日露戦争|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Russo-Japanese War|qq=}}, {{ill|Second Sino-Japanese War|ja|日中戦争|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Sino-Japanese War|qq=}}, and the {{ill|Pacific War|ja|太平洋戦争|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pacific War|qq=}}. : Annual Festival: Third Sunday of May Nomijinja02.jpg|Auxiliary Shrine Nagai Shrine Karamon Tessen.png|Nagai Shrine {{ill|Family crest|ja|家紋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Family crest|qq=}} ({{ill|Takatsuki Domain|ja|高槻藩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Domain|qq=}} lord, {{ill|Nagai family|ja|永井氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai family|qq=}}, {{ill|Family crest|ja|家紋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Family crest|qq=}}) == Festivals == * New Year’s Festival – January 1 * {{ill|Tondo Festival|ja|とんど|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tondo Festival|qq=}} – January 15 * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|qq=}} – February 3 * Dedication {{ill|Crying Sumo|ja|泣き相撲|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Crying Sumo|qq=}} – April 29 * Great Purification of Summer Crossing (Chinowa Ritual) – June 30 * Autumn Grand Festival – Second Monday of October * {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiname-sai|qq=}} – November 23 * Year-End Great Purification – December 31 == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Takatsuki City === * Nagai Shrine buildings (main hall, haiden) with attached items: 4 ridge plaques and 1 Takatsuki Castle ema – Designated on June 14, {{ill|2005|ja|2005th year|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2005|qq=}} (Heisei 17). * Nagai Shrine Karamon – Designated on June 14, 2005 (Heisei 17). * Seventeen items of materials related to the traditional Nagai Naotoki of Nagai Shrine – Deposited at {{ill|Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|ja|高槻市立しろあと歴史館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|qq=}}. == Surroundings == * Kindergarten * {{ill|Takatsuki Modern Theater|ja|高槻現代劇場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Modern Theater|qq=}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|ja|高槻市立しろあと歴史館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|qq=}} * The ruins of {{ill|Takatsuki Castle|ja|高槻城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki
