{{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11646127]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = '''Nomijinja''' (野見神社) |image = [[File:Nomijinja01.jpg|300px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=18|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|frame-height=300|coord={{coord|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E}}}} |Location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:Takatsuki|Takatsuki City]] Nomimachi 6-6 |Coordinates = {{wikicoordinates|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[:en:Nomi no Sukune|Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto]] |Founding = Late 9th century |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small), former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Annual Festival = Second Monday of October }} '''Nomijinja''' (のみじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Nomimachi, [[:en:Takatsuki|Takatsuki City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} shrine, and its former Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == History == In the late 9th century, during the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] of [[:en:Emperor Uda|Emperor Uda]] ([[:en:887|887]] – [[:en:897|897]]), an epidemic spread throughout the region. Following a divine oracle, [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozutennō]] (identified with [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) was enshrined, and as the epidemic subsided, a shrine building was constructed, establishing the Gozutennō Shrine. In the late 10th century, when {{ill|Takatsuki Castle|ja|高槻城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Castle|qq=|1=Takatsuki Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670758}} was constructed, the shrine was venerated as a guardian of the castle.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/ |title=高槻城内守護 野見神社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} However, in the 16th century when [[:en:Justo Takayama|Takayama Ukon]] became the lord of Takatsuki Castle, his adherence to [[:en:Christianity|Christianity]] led him to destroy the shrine building within the castle and confiscate its estate. Later, in the 5th year of the [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna]] era ([[:en:1619|1619]]), the lord of Takatsuki Castle, [[:en:Matsudaira Ienobu|Matsudaira Ienobu]], rebuilt the shrine building.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e5%be%a1%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92-%e8%a1%8c%e4%ba%8b.html |title=野見神社 御由緒・年間行事 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} In [[:en:1868|1868]] (the first year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), due to the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], the name of the enshrined deity was changed to [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], and furthermore, [[:en:Nomi no Sukune|Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto]] was enshrined alongside, prompting the shrine to be renamed “Nomijinja”. Consequently, although it is counted as one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} shrines listed in the {{ill|Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, the publication “Historic Sites, Famous Landscapes, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture” asserts that the renaming to “Nomijinja”—by enshrining Nomitatsune to emulate a Shikinaisha—meant that its descendant shrine is the auxiliary shrine {{ill|Nomigami Shrine|ja|野身神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomigami Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Kamigu Tenmangu|ja|上宮天満宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigu Tenmangu|qq=|1=Jōgū Tenmangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358789}} in Takatsuki City. In [[:en:1871|1871]] (the 4th year of Meiji), it was classified as a prefectural shrine ({{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) as a Shikinaisha; however, in November of the following year, [[:en:1872|1872]] (the 5th year of Meiji), it was demoted to a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) for the reasons mentioned above. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities – [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Nomi no Sukune|Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto]] == Shrine Grounds == === Shrine Buildings === * Main Hall – Rebuilt in the 2nd year of [[:en:Tenna|Tenwa]] ([[:en:1682|1682]]). * Haiden – Renovated in [[:en:2003|2003]] (the 15th year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]). * Noh Stage * Shrine Office === Auxiliary Shrines === * {{ill|Nagai Shrine|ja|永井神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 野見神社 (高槻市)|1=Nomi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646130}} – Enshrined deity: the first lord of [[:en:Takatsuki Domain|Takatsuki Domain]], {{ill|Nagai Naotoki|ja|永井直清|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naotoki|qq=|1=Nagai Naokiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549807}}. Founded in the 5th year of the [[:en:Kansei|Kansei]] era ([[:en:1793|1793]]) by the 9th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naotoshi|qq=|1=Nagai Naonobu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549817}}. Initially called “Naotoki Shrine”, it was referred to as “Naotoki Gongen” during the late Edo period. In the first year of the [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] era ([[:en:1848|1848]]), during the time of the 11th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naoteru|ja|永井直輝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naoteru|qq=|1=Nagai Naoteru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549816}}, the main hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Takatsuki City) was restored, and the haiden (also a designated tangible cultural property) along with the karamon (quadrangular gate, designated tangible cultural property) were established. The karamon was repaired in [[:en:2010|2010]] (the 22nd year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]). : Annual Festival: April 24{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e6%91%82%e6%9c%ab%e7%a4%be.html |title=野見神社 - 摂末社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} * {{ill|Kojima Shrine|ja|小島神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いちきしまひめのみこと|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=おほなむちのみこと|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Iwanagahime|ja|磐長姫命|lt=Iwanagahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いわながひめのみこと|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanagahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191705}}, and {{ill|Ameno Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=あめのこやねのみこと|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2564508}}. Its founding date is unknown; the shrine building was newly constructed on October 1, [[:en:2021|2021]] (the 3rd year of [[:en:Reiwa era|Reiwa]]). : Annual Festival: Second Sunday of October (Nomijinja Autumn Evening Festival) * Shishamyojin – In the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō period]] the separate shrines of Fukujinja, Inari Shrine, Iwajinsha, and Sorei Shrine were merged into one. : Annual Festival: April 11 :: Fukujinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Shinatsuhikokami|ja|志那都比古神|lt=Shinatsuhikokami|lt_ja=しなつひこのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhikokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}, {{ill|Shinatsuhime|ja|志那都姫神|lt=Shinatsuhime|lt_ja=しなつひめのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Homusubi|ja|火産霊|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Homusubi|lt_ja=ほむすび|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}, {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|ja|金山比古神|lt=Kanayamahikokami|lt_ja=かなやまひこのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金山彦神}}, {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|ja|金山姫神|lt=Kanayamahime|lt_ja=かなやまひめのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金山彦神}}, {{ill|Haniyamahikokami|ja|埴山比古神|lt=Haniyamahikokami|lt_ja=はにやまひこのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyamahikokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (linked as [[:en:Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|Haniyamas]] with display 埴山比古神), and {{ill|Mizuhanomenokami|ja|弥都波能売神|lt=Mizuhanomenokami|lt_ja=みづはのめのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanomenokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}. Founded in August of the 9th year of [[:en:Kansei|Kansei]] ([[:en:1797|1797]]) by {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naotoshi|qq=|1=Nagai Naonobu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549817}}. :: Inari Shrine – Enshrined deity: [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukano Mitama-no-Kami]]. Founded in February of the 11th year of Kansei ([[:en:1799|1799]]) by Nagai Naotoshi. :: Iwajinsha – Enshrined deities: (i) Yachimata Hikonami, (ii) Yachimata Hime, and (iii) Kunado-no-Kami. Founded in May of the 4th year of [[:en:Genbun|Genbun]] ([[:en:1739|1739]]) by {{ill|Nagai Ryunosuke|ja|永井流之助|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Ryunosuke|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. :: Sorei Shrine – Enshrined are the successive chief priests, shrine priests, and parishioners who have devoted themselves to the shrine. Founded in [[:en:1878|1878]] (the 11th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]). * {{ill|Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine|ja|高槻えびす神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu|ja|蛭子大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす}}. It was established in [[:en:1956|1956]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 31) as a branch from [[:en:Nishinomiya Shrine|Nishinomiya Shrine]], and the current shrine building was constructed in [[:en:1966|1966]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 41). : Takatsuki Ebisu Festival – January 10 (subdivided into the 9th: Evening Ebisu, 10th: Main Ebisu, 11th: Remaining Fortune) * [[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku Shrine]] – Enshrined are the war dead of Takatsuki who perished in the [[:en:Satsuma Rebellion|Satsuma Rebellion]], [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]], [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], [[:en:Second Sino-Japanese War|Second Sino-Japanese War]], and the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]]. : Annual Festival: Third Sunday of May {{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11646127]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = '''Nomijinja''' (野見神社) |image = [[File:Nomijinja01.jpg|300px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=18|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|frame-height=300|coord={{coord|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E}}}} |Location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:Takatsuki|Takatsuki City]] Nomimachi 6-6 |Coordinates = {{wikicoordinates|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[:en:Nomi no Sukune|Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto]] |Founding = Late 9th century |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small), former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Annual Festival = Second Monday of October }} '''Nomijinja''' (のみじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Nomimachi, [[:en:Takatsuki|Takatsuki City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} shrine, and its former Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == History == In the late 9th century, during the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] of [[:en:Emperor Uda|Emperor Uda]] ([[:en:887|887]] – [[:en:897|897]]), an epidemic spread throughout the region. Following a divine oracle, [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozutennō]] (identified with [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) was enshrined, and as the epidemic subsided, a shrine building was constructed, establishing the Gozutennō Shrine. In the late 10th century, when {{ill|Takatsuki Castle|ja|高槻城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Castle|qq=|1=Takatsuki Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670758}} was constructed, the shrine was venerated as a guardian of the castle.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/ |title=高槻城内守護 野見神社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} However, in the 16th century when [[:en:Justo Takayama|Takayama Ukon]] became the lord of Takatsuki Castle, his adherence to [[:en:Christianity|Christianity]] led him to destroy the shrine building within the castle and confiscate its estate. Later, in the 5th year of the [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna]] era ([[:en:1619|1619]]), the lord of Takatsuki Castle, [[:en:Matsudaira Ienobu|Matsudaira Ienobu]], rebuilt the shrine building.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e5%be%a1%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92-%e8%a1%8c%e4%ba%8b.html |title=野見神社 御由緒・年間行事 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} In [[:en:1868|1868]] (the first year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), due to the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], the name of the enshrined deity was changed to [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], and furthermore, [[:en:Nomi no Sukune|Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto]] was enshrined alongside, prompting the shrine to be renamed “Nomijinja”. Consequently, although it is counted as one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} shrines listed in the {{ill|Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, the publication “Historic Sites, Famous Landscapes, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture” asserts that the renaming to “Nomijinja”—by enshrining Nomitatsune to emulate a Shikinaisha—meant that its descendant shrine is the auxiliary shrine {{ill|Nomigami Shrine|ja|野身神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomigami Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Kamigu Tenmangu|ja|上宮天満宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigu Tenmangu|qq=|1=Jōgū Tenmangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358789}} in Takatsuki City. In [[:en:1871|1871]] (the 4th year of Meiji), it was classified as a prefectural shrine ({{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) as a Shikinaisha; however, in November of the following year, [[:en:1872|1872]] (the 5th year of Meiji), it was demoted to a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) for the reasons mentioned above. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities – [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Nomi no Sukune|Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto]] == Shrine Grounds == === Shrine Buildings === * Main Hall – Rebuilt in the 2nd year of [[:en:Tenna|Tenwa]] ([[:en:1682|1682]]). * Haiden – Renovated in [[:en:2003|2003]] (the 15th year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]). * Noh Stage * Shrine Office === Auxiliary Shrines === * {{ill|Nagai Shrine|ja|永井神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 野見神社 (高槻市)|1=Nomi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646130}} – Enshrined deity: the first lord of [[:en:Takatsuki Domain|Takatsuki Domain]], {{ill|Nagai Naotoki|ja|永井直清|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naotoki|qq=|1=Nagai Naokiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549807}}. Founded in the 5th year of the [[:en:Kansei|Kansei]] era ([[:en:1793|1793]]) by the 9th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naotoshi|qq=|1=Nagai Naonobu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549817}}. Initially called “Naotoki Shrine”, it was referred to as “Naotoki Gongen” during the late Edo period. In the first year of the [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] era ([[:en:1848|1848]]), during the time of the 11th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naoteru|ja|永井直輝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naoteru|qq=|1=Nagai Naoteru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549816}}, the main hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Takatsuki City) was restored, and the haiden (also a designated tangible cultural property) along with the karamon (quadrangular gate, designated tangible cultural property) were established. The karamon was repaired in [[:en:2010|2010]] (the 22nd year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]). : Annual Festival: April 24{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e6%91%82%e6%9c%ab%e7%a4%be.html |title=野見神社 - 摂末社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} * {{ill|Kojima Shrine|ja|小島神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いちきしまひめのみこと|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=おほなむちのみこと|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Iwanagahime|ja|磐長姫命|lt=Iwanagahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いわながひめのみこと|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanagahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191705}}, and {{ill|Ameno Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=あめのこやねのみこと|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2564508}}. Its founding date is unknown; the shrine building was newly constructed on October 1, [[:en:2021|2021]] (the 3rd year of [[:en:Reiwa era|Reiwa]]). : Annual Festival: Second Sunday of October (Nomijinja Autumn Evening Festival) * Shishamyojin – In the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō period]] the separate shrines of Fukujinja, Inari Shrine, Iwajinsha, and Sorei Shrine were merged into one. : Annual Festival: April 11 :: Fukujinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Shinatsuhikokami|ja|志那都比古神|lt=Shinatsuhikokami|lt_ja=しなつひこのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhikokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}, {{ill|Shinatsuhime|ja|志那都姫神|lt=Shinatsuhime|lt_ja=しなつひめのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Homusubi|ja|火産霊|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Homusubi|lt_ja=ほむすび|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}, {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|ja|金山比古神|lt=Kanayamahikokami|lt_ja=かなやまひこのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金山彦神}}, {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|ja|金山姫神|lt=Kanayamahime|lt_ja=かなやまひめのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金山彦神}}, {{ill|Haniyamahikokami|ja|埴山比古神|lt=Haniyamahikokami|lt_ja=はにやまひこのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyamahikokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (linked as [[:en:Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|Haniyamas]] with display 埴山比古神), and {{ill|Mizuhanomenokami|ja|弥都波能売神|lt=Mizuhanomenokami|lt_ja=みづはのめのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanomenokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}. Founded in August of the 9th year of [[:en:Kansei|Kansei]] ([[:en:1797|1797]]) by {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naotoshi|qq=|1=Nagai Naonobu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549817}}. :: Inari Shrine – Enshrined deity: [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukano Mitama-no-Kami]]. Founded in February of the 11th year of Kansei ([[:en:1799|1799]]) by Nagai Naotoshi. :: Iwajinsha – Enshrined deities: (i) Yachimata Hikonami, (ii) Yachimata Hime, and (iii) Kunado-no-Kami. Founded in May of the 4th year of [[:en:Genbun|Genbun]] ([[:en:1739|1739]]) by {{ill|Nagai Ryunosuke|ja|永井流之助|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Ryunosuke|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. :: Sorei Shrine – Enshrined are the successive chief priests, shrine priests, and parishioners who have devoted themselves to the shrine. Founded in [[:en:1878|1878]] (the 11th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]). * {{ill|Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine|ja|高槻えびす神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu|ja|蛭子大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす}}. It was established in [[:en:1956|1956]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 31) as a branch from [[:en:Nishinomiya Shrine|Nishinomiya Shrine]], and the current shrine building was constructed in [[:en:1966|1966]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 41). : Takatsuki Ebisu Festival – January 10 (subdivided into the 9th: Evening Ebisu, 10th: Main Ebisu, 11th: Remaining Fortune) * [[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku Shrine]] – Enshrined are the war dead of Takatsuki who perished in the [[:en:Satsuma Rebellion|Satsuma Rebellion]], [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]], [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], [[:en:Second Sino-Japanese War|Second Sino-Japanese War]], and the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]]. : Annual Festival: Third Sunday of May Nomijinja02.jpg|Auxiliary Shrine Nagai Shrine Karamon Tessen.png|Nagai Shrine [[:en:Mon (emblem)|Family crest]] ([[:en:Takatsuki Domain|Takatsuki Domain]] lord, {{ill|Nagai family|ja|永井氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai family|qq=}}, [[:en:Mon (emblem)|Family crest]]) == Festivals == * New Year’s Festival – January 1 * {{ill|Tondo Festival|ja|とんど|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tondo Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左義長|1=Sagichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9332740}} – January 15 * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分|1=Setsubun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q746798}} – February 3 * Dedication [[:en:Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival|Crying Sumo]] – April 29 * Great Purification of Summer Crossing (Chinowa Ritual) – June 30 * Autumn Grand Festival – Second Monday of October * [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] – November 23 * Year-End Great Purification – December 31 == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Takatsuki City === * Nagai Shrine buildings (main hall, haiden) with attached items: 4 ridge plaques and 1 Takatsuki Castle ema – Designated on June 14, [[:en:2005|2005]] (Heisei 17). * Nagai Shrine Karamon – Designated on June 14, 2005 (Heisei 17). * Seventeen items of materials related to the traditional Nagai Naotoki of Nagai Shrine – Deposited at {{ill|Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|ja|高槻市立しろあと歴史館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|qq=|1=Shiroato History Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670762}}. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Catholic Takatsuki Church|ja|カトリック高槻教会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Catholic Takatsuki Church|qq=|1=Takatsuki Catholic Church|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96718046}} (adjacent) * Japan Kurarechian Association Headquarters Monastery * Maria Inmaculada Kindergarten * {{ill|Takatsuki Modern Theater|ja|高槻現代劇場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Modern Theater|qq=|1=Takatsuki Arts Theatre|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670836}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|ja|高槻市立しろあと歴史館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|qq=|1=Shiroato History Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670762}} * The ruins of {{ill|Takatsuki Castle|ja|高槻城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Castle|qq=|1=Takatsuki Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670758}}, {{ill|Castle Ruins Park|ja|城跡公園 (高槻市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Castle Ruins Park|qq=|1=Takatsuki Castle Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q111830073}}, and the {{ill|Takatsuki City Historical Folk Museum|ja|高槻市立歴史民俗資料館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki City Historical Folk Museum|qq=|1=Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670799}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.nomijinja.jp/ Nomijinja] (Official website) * {{Cite web|和書|year=2012 |url=http://www.city.takatsuki.osaka.jp/rekishi_kanko/rekishi/rekishikan/jidai/shaji/1327659158743.html |title=Nomijinja |work=インターネット歴史館 |publisher=Takatsuki City|accessdate=2014-12-13}} * {{Cite web|和書|year=2012 |url=http://www.city.takatsuki.osaka.jp/rekishi_kanko/rekishi/rekishikan/jidai/shaji/1327671298593.html |title=Revived "Nagai Shrine Karamon" |work=インターネット歴史館 |publisher=Takatsuki City|accessdate=2014-12-13}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|野見神社|version=54014655|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka {{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11646127]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = '''Nomijinja''' (野見神社) |image = [[File:Nomijinja01.jpg|300px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=18|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|frame-height=300|coord={{coord|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E}}}} |Location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:Takatsuki|Takatsuki City]] Nomimachi 6-6 |Coordinates = {{wikicoordinates|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[:en:Nomi no Sukune|Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto]] |Founding = Late 9th century |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small), former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Annual Festival = Second Monday of October }} '''Nomijinja''' (のみじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Nomimachi, [[:en:Takatsuki|Takatsuki City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} shrine, and its former Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == History == In the late 9th century, during the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] of [[:en:Emperor Uda|Emperor Uda]] ([[:en:887|887]] – [[:en:897|897]]), an epidemic spread throughout the region. Following a divine oracle, [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozutennō]] (identified with [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) was enshrined, and as the epidemic subsided, a shrine building was constructed, establishing the Gozutennō Shrine. In the late 10th century, when {{ill|Takatsuki Castle|ja|高槻城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Castle|qq=|1=Takatsuki Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670758}} was constructed, the shrine was venerated as a guardian of the castle.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/ |title=高槻城内守護 野見神社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} However, in the 16th century when [[:en:Justo Takayama|Takayama Ukon]] became the lord of Takatsuki Castle, his adherence to [[:en:Christianity|Christianity]] led him to destroy the shrine building within the castle and confiscate its estate. Later, in the 5th year of the [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna]] era ([[:en:1619|1619]]), the lord of Takatsuki Castle, [[:en:Matsudaira Ienobu|Matsudaira Ienobu]], rebuilt the shrine building.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e5%be%a1%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92-%e8%a1%8c%e4%ba%8b.html |title=野見神社 御由緒・年間行事 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} In [[:en:1868|1868]] (the first year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), due to the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], the name of the enshrined deity was changed to [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], and furthermore, [[:en:Nomi no Sukune|Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto]] was enshrined alongside, prompting the shrine to be renamed “Nomijinja”. Consequently, although it is counted as one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} shrines listed in the {{ill|Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, the publication “Historic Sites, Famous Landscapes, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture” asserts that the renaming to “Nomijinja”—by enshrining Nomitatsune to emulate a Shikinaisha—meant that its descendant shrine is the auxiliary shrine {{ill|Nomigami Shrine|ja|野身神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomigami Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Kamigu Tenmangu|ja|上宮天満宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigu Tenmangu|qq=|1=Jōgū Tenmangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358789}} in Takatsuki City. In [[:en:1871|1871]] (the 4th year of Meiji), it was classified as a prefectural shrine ({{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) as a Shikinaisha; however, in November of the following year, [[:en:1872|1872]] (the 5th year of Meiji), it was demoted to a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) for the reasons mentioned above. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities – [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Nomi no Sukune|Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto]] == Shrine Grounds == === Shrine Buildings === * Main Hall – Rebuilt in the 2nd year of [[:en:Tenna|Tenwa]] ([[:en:1682|1682]]). * Haiden – Renovated in [[:en:2003|2003]] (the 15th year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]). * Noh Stage * Shrine Office === Auxiliary Shrines === * {{ill|Nagai Shrine|ja|永井神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 野見神社 (高槻市)|1=Nomi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646130}} – Enshrined deity: the first lord of [[:en:Takatsuki Domain|Takatsuki Domain]], {{ill|Nagai Naotoki|ja|永井直清|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naotoki|qq=|1=Nagai Naokiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549807}}. Founded in the 5th year of the [[:en:Kansei|Kansei]] era ([[:en:1793|1793]]) by the 9th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naotoshi|qq=|1=Nagai Naonobu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549817}}. Initially called “Naotoki Shrine”, it was referred to as “Naotoki Gongen” during the late Edo period. In the first year of the [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] era ([[:en:1848|1848]]), during the time of the 11th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naoteru|ja|永井直輝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naoteru|qq=|1=Nagai Naoteru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549816}}, the main hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Takatsuki City) was restored, and the haiden (also a designated tangible cultural property) along with the karamon (quadrangular gate, designated tangible cultural property) were established. The karamon was repaired in [[:en:2010|2010]] (the 22nd year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]). : Annual Festival: April 24{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e6%91%82%e6%9c%ab%e7%a4%be.html |title=野見神社 - 摂末社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} * {{ill|Kojima Shrine|ja|小島神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いちきしまひめのみこと|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=おほなむちのみこと|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Iwanagahime|ja|磐長姫命|lt=Iwanagahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いわながひめのみこと|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanagahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191705}}, and {{ill|Ameno Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=あめのこやねのみこと|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2564508}}. Its founding date is unknown; the shrine building was newly constructed on October 1, [[:en:2021|2021]] (the 3rd year of [[:en:Reiwa era|Reiwa]]). : Annual Festival: Second Sunday of October (Nomijinja Autumn Evening Festival) * Shishamyojin – In the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō period]] the separate shrines of Fukujinja, Inari Shrine, Iwajinsha, and Sorei Shrine were merged into one. : Annual Festival: April 11 :: Fukujinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Shinatsuhikokami|ja|志那都比古神|lt=Shinatsuhikokami|lt_ja=しなつひこのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhikokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}, {{ill|Shinatsuhime|ja|志那都姫神|lt=Shinatsuhime|lt_ja=しなつひめのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Homusubi|ja|火産霊|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Homusubi|lt_ja=ほむすび|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}, {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|ja|金山比古神|lt=Kanayamahikokami|lt_ja=かなやまひこのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金山彦神}}, {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|ja|金山姫神|lt=Kanayamahime|lt_ja=かなやまひめのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金山彦神}}, {{ill|Haniyamahikokami|ja|埴山比古神|lt=Haniyamahikokami|lt_ja=はにやまひこのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyamahikokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (linked as [[:en:Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|Haniyamas]] with display 埴山比古神), and {{ill|Mizuhanomenokami|ja|弥都波能売神|lt=Mizuhanomenokami|lt_ja=みづはのめのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanomenokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}. Founded in August of the 9th year of [[:en:Kansei|Kansei]] ([[:en:1797|1797]]) by {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naotoshi|qq=|1=Nagai Naonobu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549817}}. :: Inari Shrine – Enshrined deity: [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukano Mitama-no-Kami]]. Founded in February of the 11th year of Kansei ([[:en:1799|1799]]) by Nagai Naotoshi. :: Iwajinsha – Enshrined deities: (i) Yachimata Hikonami, (ii) Yachimata Hime, and (iii) Kunado-no-Kami. Founded in May of the 4th year of [[:en:Genbun|Genbun]] ([[:en:1739|1739]]) by {{ill|Nagai Ryunosuke|ja|永井流之助|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Ryunosuke|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. :: Sorei Shrine – Enshrined are the successive chief priests, shrine priests, and parishioners who have devoted themselves to the shrine. Founded in [[:en:1878|1878]] (the 11th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]). * {{ill|Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine|ja|高槻えびす神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu|ja|蛭子大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす}}. It was established in [[:en:1956|1956]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 31) as a branch from [[:en:Nishinomiya Shrine|Nishinomiya Shrine]], and the current shrine building was constructed in [[:en:1966|1966]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 41). : Takatsuki Ebisu Festival – January 10 (subdivided into the 9th: Evening Ebisu, 10th: Main Ebisu, 11th: Remaining Fortune) * [[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku Shrine]] – Enshrined are the war dead of Takatsuki who perished in the [[:en:Satsuma Rebellion|Satsuma Rebellion]], [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]], [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], [[:en:Second Sino-Japanese War|Second Sino-Japanese War]], and the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]]. : Annual Festival: Third Sunday of May Nomijinja02.jpg|Auxiliary Shrine Nagai Shrine Karamon Tessen.png|Nagai Shrine [[:en:Mon (emblem)|Family crest]] ([[:en:Takatsuki Domain|Takatsuki Domain]] lord, {{ill|Nagai family|ja|永井氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai family|qq=}}, [[:en:Mon (emblem)|Family crest]]) == Festivals == * New Year’s Festival – January 1 * {{ill|Tondo Festival|ja|とんど|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tondo Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左義長|1=Sagichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9332740}} – January 15 * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分|1=Setsubun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q746798}} – February 3 * Dedication [[:en:Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival|Crying Sumo]] – April 29 * Great Purification of Summer Crossing (Chinowa Ritual) – June 30 * Autumn Grand Festival – Second Monday of October * [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] – November 23 * Year-End Great Purification – December 31 == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Takatsuki City === * Nagai Shrine buildings (main hall, haiden) with attached items: 4 ridge plaques and 1 Takatsuki Castle ema – Designated on June 14, [[:en:2005|2005]] (Heisei 17). * Nagai Shrine Karamon – Designated on June 14, 2005 (Heisei 17). * Seventeen items of materials related to the traditional Nagai Naotoki of Nagai Shrine – Deposited at {{ill|Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|ja|高槻市立しろあと歴史館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|qq=|1=Shiroato History Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670762}}. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Catholic Takatsuki Church|ja|カトリック高槻教会|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Catholic Takatsuki Church|qq=|1=Takatsuki Catholic Church|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96718046}} (adjacent) * Japan Kurarechian Association Headquarters Monastery * Maria Inmaculada Kindergarten * {{ill|Takatsuki Modern Theater|ja|高槻現代劇場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Modern Theater|qq=|1=Takatsuki Arts Theatre|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670836}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|ja|高槻市立しろあと歴史館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|qq=|1=Shiroato History Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670762}} * The ruins of {{ill|Takatsuki Castle|ja|高槻城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Castle|qq=|1=Takatsuki Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670758}}, {{ill|Castle Ruins Park|ja|城跡公園 (高槻市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Castle Ruins Park|qq=|1=Takatsuki Castle Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q111830073}}, and the {{ill|Takatsuki City Historical Folk Museum|ja|高槻市立歴史民俗資料館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki City Historical Folk Museum|qq=|1=Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670799}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.nomijinja.jp/ Nomijinja] (Official website) * {{Cite web|和書|year=2012 |url=http://www.city.takatsuki.osaka.jp/rekishi_kanko/rekishi/rekishikan/jidai/shaji/1327659158743.html |title=Nomijinja |work=インターネット歴史館 |publisher=Takatsuki City|accessdate=2014-12-13}} * {{Cite web|和書|year=2012 |url=http://www.city.takatsuki.osaka.jp/rekishi_kanko/rekishi/rekishikan/jidai/shaji/1327671298593.html |title=Revived "Nagai Shrine Karamon" |work=インターネット歴史館 |publisher=Takatsuki City|accessdate=2014-12-13}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|野見神社|version=54014655|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka {{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11646127]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = '''Nomijinja''' (野見神社) |image = [[File:Nomijinja01.jpg|300px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=18|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|frame-height=300|coord={{coord|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E}}}} |Location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:Takatsuki|Takatsuki City]] Nomimachi 6-6 |Coordinates = {{wikicoordinates|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[:en:Nomi no Sukune|Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto]] |Founding = Late 9th century |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small), former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Annual Festival = Second Monday of October }} '''Nomijinja''' (のみじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Nomimachi, [[:en:Takatsuki|Takatsuki City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} shrine, and its former Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == History == In the late 9th century, during the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] of [[:en:Emperor Uda|Emperor Uda]] ([[:en:887|887]] – [[:en:897|897]]), an epidemic spread throughout the region. Following a divine oracle, [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozutennō]] (identified with [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) was enshrined, and as the epidemic subsided, a shrine building was constructed, establishing the Gozutennō Shrine. In the late 10th century, when {{ill|Takatsuki Castle|ja|高槻城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Castle|qq=|1=Takatsuki Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670758}} was constructed, the shrine was venerated as a guardian of the castle.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/ |title=高槻城内守護 野見神社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} However, in the 16th century when [[:en:Justo Takayama|Takayama Ukon]] became the lord of Takatsuki Castle, his adherence to [[:en:Christianity|Christianity]] led him to destroy the shrine building within the castle and confiscate its estate. Later, in the 5th year of the [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna]] era ([[:en:1619|1619]]), the lord of Takatsuki Castle, [[:en:Matsudaira Ienobu|Matsudaira Ienobu]], rebuilt the shrine building.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e5%be%a1%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92-%e8%a1%8c%e4%ba%8b.html |title=野見神社 御由緒・年間行事 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} In [[:en:1868|1868]] (the first year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), due to the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], the name of the enshrined deity was changed to [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], and furthermore, [[:en:Nomi no Sukune|Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto]] was enshrined alongside, prompting the shrine to be renamed “Nomijinja”. Consequently, although it is counted as one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} shrines listed in the {{ill|Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, the publication “Historic Sites, Famous Landscapes, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture” asserts that the renaming to “Nomijinja”—by enshrining Nomitatsune to emulate a Shikinaisha—meant that its descendant shrine is the auxiliary shrine {{ill|Nomigami Shrine|ja|野身神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomigami Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Kamigu Tenmangu|ja|上宮天満宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigu Tenmangu|qq=|1=Jōgū Tenmangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358789}} in Takatsuki City. In [[:en:1871|1871]] (the 4th year of Meiji), it was classified as a prefectural shrine ({{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) as a Shikinaisha; however, in November of the following year, [[:en:1872|1872]] (the 5th year of Meiji), it was demoted to a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) for the reasons mentioned above. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities – [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Nomi no Sukune|Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto]] == Shrine Grounds == === Shrine Buildings === * Main Hall – Rebuilt in the 2nd year of [[:en:Tenna|Tenwa]] ([[:en:1682|1682]]). * Haiden – Renovated in [[:en:2003|2003]] (the 15th year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]). * Noh Stage * Shrine Office === Auxiliary Shrines === * {{ill|Nagai Shrine|ja|永井神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 野見神社 (高槻市)|1=Nomi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646130}} – Enshrined deity: the first lord of [[:en:Takatsuki Domain|Takatsuki Domain]], {{ill|Nagai Naotoki|ja|永井直清|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naotoki|qq=|1=Nagai Naokiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549807}}. Founded in the 5th year of the [[:en:Kansei|Kansei]] era ([[:en:1793|1793]]) by the 9th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naotoshi|qq=|1=Nagai Naonobu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549817}}. Initially called “Naotoki Shrine”, it was referred to as “Naotoki Gongen” during the late Edo period. In the first year of the [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] era ([[:en:1848|1848]]), during the time of the 11th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naoteru|ja|永井直輝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naoteru|qq=|1=Nagai Naoteru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549816}}, the main hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Takatsuki City) was restored, and the haiden (also a designated tangible cultural property) along with the karamon (quadrangular gate, designated tangible cultural property) were established. The karamon was repaired in [[:en:2010|2010]] (the 22nd year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]). : Annual Festival: April 24{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e6%91%82%e6%9c%ab%e7%a4%be.html |title=野見神社 - 摂末社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} * {{ill|Kojima Shrine|ja|小島神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いちきしまひめのみこと|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=おほなむちのみこと|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Iwanagahime|ja|磐長姫命|lt=Iwanagahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いわながひめのみこと|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanagahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191705}}, and {{ill|Ameno Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=あめのこやねのみこと|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2564508}}. Its founding date is unknown; the shrine building was newly constructed on October 1, [[:en:2021|2021]] (the 3rd year of [[:en:Reiwa era|Reiwa]]). : Annual Festival: Second Sunday of October (Nomijinja Autumn Evening Festival) * Shishamyojin – In the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō period]] the separate shrines of Fukujinja, Inari Shrine, Iwajinsha, and Sorei Shrine were merged into one. : Annual Festival: April 11 :: Fukujinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Shinatsuhikokami|ja|志那都比古神|lt=Shinatsuhikokami|lt_ja=しなつひこのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhikokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}, {{ill|Shinatsuhime|ja|志那都姫神|lt=Shinatsuhime|lt_ja=しなつひめのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Homusubi|ja|火産霊|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Homusubi|lt_ja=ほむすび|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}, {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|ja|金山比古神|lt=Kanayamahikokami|lt_ja=かなやまひこのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金山彦神}}, {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|ja|金山姫神|lt=Kanayamahime|lt_ja=かなやまひめのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金山彦神}}, {{ill|Haniyamahikokami|ja|埴山比古神|lt=Haniyamahikokami|lt_ja=はにやまひこのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyamahikokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (linked as [[:en:Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|Haniyamas]] with display 埴山比古神), and {{ill|Mizuhanomenokami|ja|弥都波能売神|lt=Mizuhanomenokami|lt_ja=みづはのめのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanomenokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}. Founded in August of the 9th year of [[:en:Kansei|Kansei]] ([[:en:1797|1797]]) by {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naotoshi|qq=|1=Nagai Naonobu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549817}}. :: Inari Shrine – Enshrined deity: [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukano Mitama-no-Kami]]. Founded in February of the 11th year of Kansei ([[:en:1799|1799]]) by Nagai Naotoshi. :: Iwajinsha – Enshrined deities: (i) Yachimata Hikonami, (ii) Yachimata Hime, and (iii) Kunado-no-Kami. Founded in May of the 4th year of [[:en:Genbun|Genbun]] ([[:en:1739|1739]]) by {{ill|Nagai Ryunosuke|ja|永井流之助|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Ryunosuke|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. :: Sorei Shrine – Enshrined are the successive chief priests, shrine priests, and parishioners who have devoted themselves to the shrine. Founded in [[:en:1878|1878]] (the 11th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]). * {{ill|Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine|ja|高槻えびす神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu|ja|蛭子大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす}}. It was established in [[:en:1956|1956]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 31) as a branch from [[:en:Nishinomiya Shrine|Nishinomiya Shrine]], and the current shrine building was constructed in [[:en:1966|1966]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 41). : Takatsuki Ebisu Festival – January 10 (subdivided into the 9th: Evening Ebisu, 10th: Main Ebisu, 11th: Remaining Fortune) * [[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku Shrine]] – Enshrined are the war dead of Takatsuki who perished in the [[:en:Satsuma Rebellion|Satsuma Rebellion]], [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]], [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], [[:en:Second Sino-Japanese War|Second Sino-Japanese War]], and the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]]. : Annual Festival: Third Sunday of May Festival|ja|とんど|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tondo Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左義長|1=Sagichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9332740}} – January 15 * {{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11646127]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = '''Nomijinja''' (野見神社) |image = [[File:Nomijinja01.jpg|300px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=18|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|frame-height=300|coord={{coord|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E}}}} |Location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[:en:Takatsuki|Takatsuki City]] Nomimachi 6-6 |Coordinates = {{wikicoordinates|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[:en:Nomi no Sukune|Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto]] |Founding = Late 9th century |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small), former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Annual Festival = Second Monday of October }} '''Nomijinja''' (のみじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Nomimachi, [[:en:Takatsuki|Takatsuki City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} shrine, and its former Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == History == In the late 9th century, during the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] of [[:en:Emperor Uda|Emperor Uda]] ([[:en:887|887]] – [[:en:897|897]]), an epidemic spread throughout the region. Following a divine oracle, [[:en:Gozu Tennō|Gozutennō]] (identified with [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]) was enshrined, and as the epidemic subsided, a shrine building was constructed, establishing the Gozutennō Shrine. In the late 10th century, when {{ill|Takatsuki Castle|ja|高槻城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Castle|qq=|1=Takatsuki Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670758}} was constructed, the shrine was venerated as a guardian of the castle.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/ |title=高槻城内守護 野見神社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} However, in the 16th century when [[:en:Justo Takayama|Takayama Ukon]] became the lord of Takatsuki Castle, his adherence to [[:en:Christianity|Christianity]] led him to destroy the shrine building within the castle and confiscate its estate. Later, in the 5th year of the [[:en:Yuanhe|Genna]] era ([[:en:1619|1619]]), the lord of Takatsuki Castle, [[:en:Matsudaira Ienobu|Matsudaira Ienobu]], rebuilt the shrine building.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e5%be%a1%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92-%e8%a1%8c%e4%ba%8b.html |title=野見神社 御由緒・年間行事 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} In [[:en:1868|1868]] (the first year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]), due to the [[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Separation of Shinto and Buddhism]], the name of the enshrined deity was changed to [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], and furthermore, [[:en:Nomi no Sukune|Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto]] was enshrined alongside, prompting the shrine to be renamed “Nomijinja”. Consequently, although it is counted as one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} shrines listed in the {{ill|Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, the publication “Historic Sites, Famous Landscapes, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture” asserts that the renaming to “Nomijinja”—by enshrining Nomitatsune to emulate a Shikinaisha—meant that its descendant shrine is the auxiliary shrine {{ill|Nomigami Shrine|ja|野身神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomigami Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Kamigu Tenmangu|ja|上宮天満宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigu Tenmangu|qq=|1=Jōgū Tenmangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358789}} in Takatsuki City. In [[:en:1871|1871]] (the 4th year of Meiji), it was classified as a prefectural shrine ({{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) as a Shikinaisha; however, in November of the following year, [[:en:1872|1872]] (the 5th year of Meiji), it was demoted to a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) for the reasons mentioned above. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities – [[:en:Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Nomi no Sukune|Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto]] == Shrine Grounds == === Shrine Buildings === * Main Hall – Rebuilt in the 2nd year of [[:en:Tenna|Tenwa]] ([[:en:1682|1682]]). * Haiden – Renovated in [[:en:2003|2003]] (the 15th year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]). * Noh Stage * Shrine Office === Auxiliary Shrines === * {{ill|Nagai Shrine|ja|永井神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 野見神社 (高槻市)|1=Nomi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646130}} – Enshrined deity: the first lord of [[:en:Takatsuki Domain|Takatsuki Domain]], {{ill|Nagai Naotoki|ja|永井直清|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naotoki|qq=|1=Nagai Naokiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549807}}. Founded in the 5th year of the [[:en:Kansei|Kansei]] era ([[:en:1793|1793]]) by the 9th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naotoshi|qq=|1=Nagai Naonobu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549817}}. Initially called “Naotoki Shrine”, it was referred to as “Naotoki Gongen” during the late Edo period. In the first year of the [[:en:Kaei|Kaei]] era ([[:en:1848|1848]]), during the time of the 11th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naoteru|ja|永井直輝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naoteru|qq=|1=Nagai Naoteru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549816}}, the main hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Takatsuki City) was restored, and the haiden (also a designated tangible cultural property) along with the karamon (quadrangular gate, designated tangible cultural property) were established. The karamon was repaired in [[:en:2010|2010]] (the 22nd year of [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]]). : Annual Festival: April 24{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e6%91%82%e6%9c%ab%e7%a4%be.html |title=野見神社 - 摂末社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} * {{ill|Kojima Shrine|ja|小島神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いちきしまひめのみこと|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=おほなむちのみこと|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Iwanagahime|ja|磐長姫命|lt=Iwanagahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いわながひめのみこと|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanagahime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191705}}, and {{ill|Ameno Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=あめのこやねのみこと|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2564508}}. Its founding date is unknown; the shrine building was newly constructed on October 1, [[:en:2021|2021]] (the 3rd year of [[:en:Reiwa era|Reiwa]]). : Annual Festival: Second Sunday of October (Nomijinja Autumn Evening Festival) * Shishamyojin – In the [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō period]] the separate shrines of Fukujinja, Inari Shrine, Iwajinsha, and Sorei Shrine were merged into one. : Annual Festival: April 11 :: Fukujinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Shinatsuhikokami|ja|志那都比古神|lt=Shinatsuhikokami|lt_ja=しなつひこのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhikokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}, {{ill|Shinatsuhime|ja|志那都姫神|lt=Shinatsuhime|lt_ja=しなつひめのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Homusubi|ja|火産霊|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Homusubi|lt_ja=ほむすび|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homusubi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}, {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|ja|金山比古神|lt=Kanayamahikokami|lt_ja=かなやまひこのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金山彦神}}, {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|ja|金山姫神|lt=Kanayamahime|lt_ja=かなやまひめのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金山彦神}}, {{ill|Haniyamahikokami|ja|埴山比古神|lt=Haniyamahikokami|lt_ja=はにやまひこのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyamahikokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (linked as [[:en:Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|Haniyamas]] with display 埴山比古神), and {{ill|Mizuhanomenokami|ja|弥都波能売神|lt=Mizuhanomenokami|lt_ja=みづはのめのかみ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanomenokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}. Founded in August of the 9th year of [[:en:Kansei|Kansei]] ([[:en:1797|1797]]) by {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naotoshi|qq=|1=Nagai Naonobu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549817}}. :: Inari Shrine – Enshrined deity: [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukano Mitama-no-Kami]]. Founded in February of the 11th year of Kansei ([[:en:1799|1799]]) by Nagai Naotoshi. :: Iwajinsha – Enshrined deities: (i) Yachimata Hikonami, (ii) Yachimata Hime, and (iii) Kunado-no-Kami. Founded in May of the 4th year of [[:en:Genbun|Genbun]] ([[:en:1739|1739]]) by {{ill|Nagai Ryunosuke|ja|永井流之助|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Ryunosuke|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. :: Sorei Shrine – Enshrined are the successive chief priests, shrine priests, and parishioners who have devoted themselves to the shrine. Founded in [[:en:1878|1878]] (the 11th year of [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]]). * {{ill|Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine|ja|高槻えびす神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu|ja|蛭子大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす}}. It was established in [[:en:1956|1956]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 31) as a branch from [[:en:Nishinomiya Shrine|Nishinomiya Shrine]], and the current shrine building was constructed in [[:en:1966|1966]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 41). : Takatsuki Ebisu Festival – January 10 (subdivided into the 9th: Evening Ebisu, 10th: Main Ebisu, 11th: Remaining Fortune) * [[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku Shrine]] – Enshrined are the war dead of Takatsuki who perished in the [[:en:Satsuma Rebellion|Satsuma Rebellion]], [[:en:First Sino-Japanese War|First Sino-Japanese War]], [[:en:Russo-Japanese War|Russo-Japanese War]], [[:en:Second Sino-Japanese War|Second Sino-Japanese War]], and the [[:en:Pacific War|Pacific War]]. : Annual Festival: Third Sunday of May
