[[ja:野見神社]] [[ja:野見神社]] [[ja:野見神社]] { {{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = '''Nomijinja''' (野見神社) |image = [[File:Nomijinja01.jpg|300px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=18|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|frame-height=300|coord={{coord|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|Takatsuki|en|Takatsuki|WD=Q335423|lt=Takatsuki City}} Nomimachi 6-6 |Coordinates = {{wikicoordinates|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|Nomi no Sukune|en|Nomi no Sukune|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto}} |Founding = Late 9th century |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small), former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Annual Festival = Second Monday of October }} '''Nomijinja''' (のみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Nomimachi, {{ill|Takatsuki|en|Takatsuki|WD=Q335423|lt=Takatsuki City}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} shrine, and its former Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == History == In the late 9th century, during the mid-{{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} of {{ill|Emperor Uda|en|Emperor Uda|WD=Q349267|lt=Emperor Uda}} ({{ill|887|en|887|WD=Q25010|lt=887}} – {{ill|897|en|897|WD=Q30468|lt=897}}), an epidemic spread throughout the region. Following a divine oracle, {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozutennō}} (identified with {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}) was enshrined, and as the epidemic subsided, a shrine building was constructed, establishing the Gozutennō Shrine. In the late 10th century, when {{ill|Takatsuki Castle|ja|高槻城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Castle|1=Takatsuki Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670758}} was constructed, the shrine was venerated as a guardian of the castle.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/ |title=高槻城内守護 野見神社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} However, in the 16th century when {{ill|Justo Takayama|en|Justo Takayama|WD=Q749873|lt=Takayama Ukon}} became the lord of Takatsuki Castle, his adherence to {{ill|Christianity|en|Christianity|WD=Q5043|lt=Christianity}} led him to destroy the shrine building within the castle and confiscate its estate. Later, in the 5th year of the {{ill|Yuanhe|en|Yuanhe|WD=Q10889567|lt=Genna}} era ({{ill|1619|en|1619|WD=Q6804|lt=1619}}), the lord of Takatsuki Castle, {{ill|Matsudaira Ienobu|en|Matsudaira Ienobu|WD=Q5368670|lt=Matsudaira Ienobu}}, rebuilt the shrine building.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e5%be%a1%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92-%e8%a1%8c%e4%ba%8b.html |title=野見神社 御由緒・年間行事 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} In {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868}} (the first year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}), due to the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism}}, the name of the enshrined deity was changed to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and furthermore, {{ill|Nomi no Sukune|en|Nomi no Sukune|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto}} was enshrined alongside, prompting the shrine to be renamed “Nomijinja”. Consequently, although it is counted as one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} shrines listed in the {{ill|Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, the publication “Historic Sites, Famous Landscapes, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture” asserts that the renaming to “Nomijinja”—by enshrining Nomitatsune to emulate a Shikinaisha—meant that its descendant shrine is the auxiliary shrine {{ill|Nomigami Shrine|ja|野身神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomigami Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Kamigu Tenmangu|ja|上宮天満宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigu Tenmangu|1=Jōgū Tenmangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358789}} in Takatsuki City. In {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871}} (the 4th year of Meiji), it was classified as a prefectural shrine ({{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) as a Shikinaisha; however, in November of the following year, {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872}} (the 5th year of Meiji), it was demoted to a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) for the reasons mentioned above. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities – {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Nomi no Sukune|en|Nomi no Sukune|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto}} == Shrine Grounds == === Shrine Buildings === * Main Hall – Rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Tenna|en|Tenna|WD=Q1145289|lt=Tenwa}} ({{ill|1682|en|1682|WD=Q7648|lt=1682}}). * Haiden – Renovated in {{ill|2003|en|2003|WD=Q1986|lt=2003}} (the 15th year of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}}). * Noh Stage * Shrine Office === Auxiliary Shrines === * {{ill|Nagai Shrine|ja|永井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 野見神社 (高槻市)|1=Nomi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646130}} – Enshrined deity: the first lord of {{ill|Takatsuki Domain|en|Takatsuki Domain|WD=Q10863415|lt=Takatsuki Domain}}, {{ill|Nagai Naotoki|ja|永井直清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naotoki|1=Nagai Naokiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549807}}. Founded in the 5th year of the {{ill|Kansei|en|Kansei|WD=Q1195131|lt=Kansei}} era ({{ill|1793|en|1793|WD=Q7807|lt=1793}}) by the 9th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naotoshi|1=Nagai Naonobu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549817}}. Initially called “Naotoki Shrine”, it was referred to as “Naotoki Gongen” during the late Edo period. In the first year of the {{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei}} era ({{ill|1848|en|1848|WD=Q7647|lt=1848}}), during the time of the 11th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naoteru|ja|永井直輝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naoteru|1=Nagai Naoteru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549816}}, the main hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Takatsuki City) was restored, and the haiden (also a designated tangible cultural property) along with the karamon (quadrangular gate, designated tangible cultural property) were established. The karamon was repaired in {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=2010}} (the 22nd year of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}}). : Annual Festival: April 24{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e6%91%82%e6%9c%ab%e7%a4%be.html |title=野見神社 - 摂末社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} * {{ill|Kojima Shrine|ja|小島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いちきしまひめのみこと|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=おほなむちのみこと|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Iwanagahime|ja|磐長姫命|lt=Iwanagahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いわながひめのみこと|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanagahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191705}}, and {{ill|Ameno Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=あめのこやねのみこと|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2564508}}. Its founding date is unknown; the shrine building was newly constructed on October 1, {{ill|2021|en|2021|WD=Q49628|lt=2021}} (the 3rd year of {{ill|Reiwa era|en|Reiwa era|WD=Q62609017|lt=Reiwa}}). : Annual Festival: Second Sunday of October (Nomijinja Autumn Evening Festival) * Shishamyojin – In the {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō period}} the separate shrines of Fukujinja, Inari Shrine, Iwajinsha, and Sorei Shrine were merged into one. : Annual Festival: April 11 :: Fukujinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Shinatsuhikokami|ja|志那都比古神|lt=Shinatsuhikokami|lt_ja=しなつひこのかみ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhikokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}, {{ill|Shinatsuhime|ja|志那都姫神|lt=Shinatsuhime|lt_ja=しなつひめのかみ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Homusubi|ja|火産霊|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Homusubi|lt_ja=ほむすび|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}, {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|ja|金山比古神|lt=Kanayamahikokami|lt_ja=かなやまひこのかみ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金山彦神}}, {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|ja|金山姫神|lt=Kanayamahime|lt_ja=かなやまひめのかみ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金山彦神}}, {{ill|Haniyamahikokami|ja|埴山比古神|lt=Haniyamahikokami|lt_ja=はにやまひこのかみ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyamahikokami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (linked as {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|en|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|WD=Q9285919|lt=Haniyamas}} with display 埴山比古神), and {{ill|Mizuhanomenokami|ja|弥都波能売神|lt=Mizuhanomenokami|lt_ja=みづはのめのかみ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanomenokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}. Founded in August of the 9th year of {{ill|Kansei|en|Kansei|WD=Q1195131|lt=Kansei}} ({{ill|1797|en|1797|WD=Q7820|lt=1797}}) by {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naotoshi|1=Nagai Naonobu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549817}}. :: Inari Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukano Mitama-no-Kami}}. Founded in February of the 11th year of Kansei ({{ill|1799|en|1799|WD=Q7827|lt=1799}}) by Nagai Naotoshi. :: Iwajinsha – Enshrined deities: (i) Yachimata Hikonami, (ii) Yachimata Hime, and (iii) Kunado-no-Kami. Founded in May of the 4th year of {{ill|Genbun|en|Genbun|WD=Q1145754|lt=Genbun}} ({{ill|1739|en|1739|WD=Q7002|lt=1739}}) by {{ill|Nagai Ryunosuke|ja|永井流之助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Ryunosuke|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. :: Sorei Shrine – Enshrined are the successive chief priests, shrine priests, and parishioners who have devoted themselves to the shrine. Founded in {{ill|1878|en|1878|WD=Q7776|lt=1878}} (the 11th year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}). * {{ill|Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine|ja|高槻えびす神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu|ja|蛭子大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす}}. It was established in {{ill|1956|en|1956|WD=Q5221|lt=1956}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa}} 31) as a branch from {{ill|Nishinomiya Shrine|en|Nishinomiya Shrine|WD=Q705297|lt=Nishinomiya Shrine}}, and the current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1966|en|1966|WD=Q2649|lt=1966}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa}} 41). : Takatsuki Ebisu Festival – January 10 (subdivided into the 9th: Evening Ebisu, 10th: Main Ebisu, 11th: Remaining Fortune) * {{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine}} – Enshrined are the war dead of Takatsuki who perished in the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|en|Satsuma Rebellion|WD=Q714820|lt=Satsuma Rebellion}}, {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|en|First Sino-Japanese War|WD=Q178687|lt=First Sino-Japanese War}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|en|Russo-Japanese War|WD=Q159950|lt=Russo-Japanese War}}, {{ill|Second Sino-Japanese War|en|Second Sino-Japanese War|WD=Q170314|lt=Second Sino-Japanese War}}, and the {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}. : Annual Festival: Third Sunday of May Nomijinja02.jpg|Auxiliary Shrine Nagai Shrine Karamon Tessen.png|Nagai Shrine {{ill|Mon (emblem)|en|Mon (emblem)|WD=Q600964|lt=Family crest}} ({{ill|Takatsuki Domain|en|Takatsuki Domain|WD=Q10863415|lt=Takatsuki Domain}} lord, {{ill|Nagai family|ja|永井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai family|qq=}}, {{ill|Mon (emblem)|en|Mon (emblem)|WD=Q600964|lt=Family crest}}) == Festivals == * New Year’s Festival – January 1 * {{ill|Tondo Festival|ja|とんど|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tondo Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左義長|1=Sagichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9332740}} – January 15 * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分|1=Setsubun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q746798}} – February 3 * Dedication {{ill|Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival|en|Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival|WD=Q1461738|lt=Crying Sumo}} – April 29 * Great Purification of Summer Crossing (Chinowa Ritual) – June 30 * Autumn Grand Festival – Second Monday of October * {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai}} – November 23 * Year-End Great Purification – December 31 == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Takatsuki City === * Nagai Shrine buildings (main hall, haiden) with attached items: 4 ridge plaques and 1 Takatsuki Castle ema – Designated on June 14, {{ill|2005|en|2005|WD=Q2019|lt=2005}} (Heisei 17). * Nagai Shrine Karamon – Designated on June 14, 2005 (Heisei 17). * Seventeen items of materials related to the traditional Nagai Naotoki of Nagai Shrine – Deposited at {{ill|Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|ja|高槻市立しろあと歴史館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|1=Shiroato History Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670762}}. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Catholic Takatsuki Church|ja|カトリック高槻教会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Catholic Takatsuki Church|1=Takatsuki Catholic Church|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96718046}} (adjacent) * Japan Kurarechian Association Headquarters Monastery * Maria Inmaculada Kindergarten * {{ill|Takatsuki Modern Theater|ja|高槻現代劇場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Modern Theater|1=Takatsuki Arts Theatre|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670836}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|ja|高槻市立しろあと歴史館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|1=Shiroato History Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670762}} * The ruins of {{ill|Takatsuki Castle|ja|高槻城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Castle|1=Takatsuki Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670758}}, {{ill|Castle Ruins Park|ja|城跡公園 (高槻市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Castle Ruins Park|1=Takatsuki Castle Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q111830073}}, and the {{ill|Takatsuki City Historical Folk Museum|ja|高槻市立歴史民俗資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki City Historical Folk Museum|1=Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670799}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.nomijinja.jp/ Nomijinja] (Official website) * {{Cite web|和書|year=2012 |url=http://www.city.takatsuki.osaka.jp/rekishi_kanko/rekishi/rekishikan/jidai/shaji/1327659158743.html |title=Nomijinja |work=インターネット歴史館 |publisher=Takatsuki City|accessdate=2014-12-13}} * {{Cite web|和書|year=2012 |url=http://www.city.takatsuki.osaka.jp/rekishi_kanko/rekishi/rekishikan/jidai/shaji/1327671298593.html |title=Revived "Nagai Shrine Karamon" |work=インターネット歴史館 |publisher=Takatsuki City|accessdate=2014-12-13}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|野見神社|version=54014655|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:野見神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11646127}} revision.text:
[[ja:野見神社]] [[ja:野見神社]] [[ja:野見神社]] { {{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = '''Nomijinja''' (野見神社) |image = [[File:Nomijinja01.jpg|300px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=18|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|frame-height=300|coord={{coord|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|Takatsuki|en|Takatsuki|WD=Q335423|lt=Takatsuki City}} Nomimachi 6-6 |Coordinates = {{wikicoordinates|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|Nomi no Sukune|en|Nomi no Sukune|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto}} |Founding = Late 9th century |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small), former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Annual Festival = Second Monday of October }} '''Nomijinja''' (のみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Nomimachi, {{ill|Takatsuki|en|Takatsuki|WD=Q335423|lt=Takatsuki City}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} shrine, and its former Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == History == In the late 9th century, during the mid-{{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} of {{ill|Emperor Uda|en|Emperor Uda|WD=Q349267|lt=Emperor Uda}} ({{ill|887|en|887|WD=Q25010|lt=887}} – {{ill|897|en|897|WD=Q30468|lt=897}}), an epidemic spread throughout the region. Following a divine oracle, {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozutennō}} (identified with {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}) was enshrined, and as the epidemic subsided, a shrine building was constructed, establishing the Gozutennō Shrine. In the late 10th century, when {{ill|Takatsuki Castle|ja|高槻城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Castle|1=Takatsuki Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670758}} was constructed, the shrine was venerated as a guardian of the castle.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/ |title=高槻城内守護 野見神社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} However, in the 16th century when {{ill|Justo Takayama|en|Justo Takayama|WD=Q749873|lt=Takayama Ukon}} became the lord of Takatsuki Castle, his adherence to {{ill|Christianity|en|Christianity|WD=Q5043|lt=Christianity}} led him to destroy the shrine building within the castle and confiscate its estate. Later, in the 5th year of the {{ill|Yuanhe|en|Yuanhe|WD=Q10889567|lt=Genna}} era ({{ill|1619|en|1619|WD=Q6804|lt=1619}}), the lord of Takatsuki Castle, {{ill|Matsudaira Ienobu|en|Matsudaira Ienobu|WD=Q5368670|lt=Matsudaira Ienobu}}, rebuilt the shrine building.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e5%be%a1%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92-%e8%a1%8c%e4%ba%8b.html |title=野見神社 御由緒・年間行事 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} In {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868}} (the first year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}), due to the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism}}, the name of the enshrined deity was changed to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and furthermore, {{ill|Nomi no Sukune|en|Nomi no Sukune|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto}} was enshrined alongside, prompting the shrine to be renamed “Nomijinja”. Consequently, although it is counted as one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} shrines listed in the {{ill|Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, the publication “Historic Sites, Famous Landscapes, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture” asserts that the renaming to “Nomijinja”—by enshrining Nomitatsune to emulate a Shikinaisha—meant that its descendant shrine is the auxiliary shrine {{ill|Nomigami Shrine|ja|野身神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomigami Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Kamigu Tenmangu|ja|上宮天満宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigu Tenmangu|1=Jōgū Tenmangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358789}} in Takatsuki City. In {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871}} (the 4th year of Meiji), it was classified as a prefectural shrine ({{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) as a Shikinaisha; however, in November of the following year, {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872}} (the 5th year of Meiji), it was demoted to a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) for the reasons mentioned above. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities – {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Nomi no Sukune|en|Nomi no Sukune|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto}} == Shrine Grounds == === Shrine Buildings === * Main Hall – Rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Tenna|en|Tenna|WD=Q1145289|lt=Tenwa}} ({{ill|1682|en|1682|WD=Q7648|lt=1682}}). * Haiden – Renovated in {{ill|2003|en|2003|WD=Q1986|lt=2003}} (the 15th year of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}}). * Noh Stage * Shrine Office === Auxiliary Shrines === * {{ill|Nagai Shrine|ja|永井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 野見神社 (高槻市)|1=Nomi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646130}} – Enshrined deity: the first lord of {{ill|Takatsuki Domain|en|Takatsuki Domain|WD=Q10863415|lt=Takatsuki Domain}}, {{ill|Nagai Naotoki|ja|永井直清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naotoki|1=Nagai Naokiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549807}}. Founded in the 5th year of the {{ill|Kansei|en|Kansei|WD=Q1195131|lt=Kansei}} era ({{ill|1793|en|1793|WD=Q7807|lt=1793}}) by the 9th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naotoshi|1=Nagai Naonobu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549817}}. Initially called “Naotoki Shrine”, it was referred to as “Naotoki Gongen” during the late Edo period. In the first year of the {{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei}} era ({{ill|1848|en|1848|WD=Q7647|lt=1848}}), during the time of the 11th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naoteru|ja|永井直輝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naoteru|1=Nagai Naoteru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549816}}, the main hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Takatsuki City) was restored, and the haiden (also a designated tangible cultural property) along with the karamon (quadrangular gate, designated tangible cultural property) were established. The karamon was repaired in {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=2010}} (the 22nd year of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}}). : Annual Festival: April 24{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e6%91%82%e6%9c%ab%e7%a4%be.html |title=野見神社 - 摂末社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} * {{ill|Kojima Shrine|ja|小島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いちきしまひめのみこと|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=おほなむちのみこと|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Iwanagahime|ja|磐長姫命|lt=Iwanagahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いわながひめのみこと|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanagahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191705}}, and {{ill|Ameno Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=あめのこやねのみこと|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2564508}}. Its founding date is unknown; the shrine building was newly constructed on October 1, {{ill|2021|en|2021|WD=Q49628|lt=2021}} (the 3rd year of {{ill|Reiwa era|en|Reiwa era|WD=Q62609017|lt=Reiwa}}). : Annual Festival: Second Sunday of October (Nomijinja Autumn Evening Festival) * Shishamyojin – In the {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō period}} the separate shrines of Fukujinja, Inari Shrine, Iwajinsha, and Sorei Shrine were merged into one. : Annual Festival: April 11 :: Fukujinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Shinatsuhikokami|ja|志那都比古神|lt=Shinatsuhikokami|lt_ja=しなつひこのかみ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhikokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}, {{ill|Shinatsuhime|ja|志那都姫神|lt=Shinatsuhime|lt_ja=しなつひめのかみ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Homusubi|ja|火産霊|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Homusubi|lt_ja=ほむすび|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}, {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|ja|金山比古神|lt=Kanayamahikokami|lt_ja=かなやまひこのかみ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金山彦神}}, {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|ja|金山姫神|lt=Kanayamahime|lt_ja=かなやまひめのかみ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金山彦神}}, {{ill|Haniyamahikokami|ja|埴山比古神|lt=Haniyamahikokami|lt_ja=はにやまひこのかみ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyamahikokami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (linked as {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|en|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|WD=Q9285919|lt=Haniyamas}} with display 埴山比古神), and {{ill|Mizuhanomenokami|ja|弥都波能売神|lt=Mizuhanomenokami|lt_ja=みづはのめのかみ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanomenokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}. Founded in August of the 9th year of {{ill|Kansei|en|Kansei|WD=Q1195131|lt=Kansei}} ({{ill|1797|en|1797|WD=Q7820|lt=1797}}) by {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naotoshi|1=Nagai Naonobu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549817}}. :: Inari Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukano Mitama-no-Kami}}. Founded in February of the 11th year of Kansei ({{ill|1799|en|1799|WD=Q7827|lt=1799}}) by Nagai Naotoshi. :: Iwajinsha – Enshrined deities: (i) Yachimata Hikonami, (ii) Yachimata Hime, and (iii) Kunado-no-Kami. Founded in May of the 4th year of {{ill|Genbun|en|Genbun|WD=Q1145754|lt=Genbun}} ({{ill|1739|en|1739|WD=Q7002|lt=1739}}) by {{ill|Nagai Ryunosuke|ja|永井流之助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Ryunosuke|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. :: Sorei Shrine – Enshrined are the successive chief priests, shrine priests, and parishioners who have devoted themselves to the shrine. Founded in {{ill|1878|en|1878|WD=Q7776|lt=1878}} (the 11th year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}). * {{ill|Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine|ja|高槻えびす神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu|ja|蛭子大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす}}. It was established in {{ill|1956|en|1956|WD=Q5221|lt=1956}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa}} 31) as a branch from {{ill|Nishinomiya Shrine|en|Nishinomiya Shrine|WD=Q705297|lt=Nishinomiya Shrine}}, and the current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1966|en|1966|WD=Q2649|lt=1966}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa}} 41). : Takatsuki Ebisu Festival – January 10 (subdivided into the 9th: Evening Ebisu, 10th: Main Ebisu, 11th: Remaining Fortune) * {{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine}} – Enshrined are the war dead of Takatsuki who perished in the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|en|Satsuma Rebellion|WD=Q714820|lt=Satsuma Rebellion}}, {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|en|First Sino-Japanese War|WD=Q178687|lt=First Sino-Japanese War}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|en|Russo-Japanese War|WD=Q159950|lt=Russo-Japanese War}}, {{ill|Second Sino-Japanese War|en|Second Sino-Japanese War|WD=Q170314|lt=Second Sino-Japanese War}}, and the {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}. : Annual Festival: Third Sunday of May Nomijinja02.jpg|Auxiliary Shrine Nagai Shrine Karamon Tessen.png|Nagai Shrine {{ill|Mon (emblem)|en|Mon (emblem)|WD=Q600964|lt=Family crest}} ({{ill|Takatsuki Domain|en|Takatsuki Domain|WD=Q10863415|lt=Takatsuki Domain}} lord, {{ill|Nagai family|ja|永井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai family|qq=}}, {{ill|Mon (emblem)|en|Mon (emblem)|WD=Q600964|lt=Family crest}}) == Festivals == * New Year’s Festival – January 1 * {{ill|Tondo Festival|ja|とんど|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tondo Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左義長|1=Sagichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9332740}} – January 15 * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分|1=Setsubun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q746798}} – February 3 * Dedication {{ill|Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival|en|Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival|WD=Q1461738|lt=Crying Sumo}} – April 29 * Great Purification of Summer Crossing (Chinowa Ritual) – June 30 * Autumn Grand Festival – Second Monday of October * {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai}} – November 23 * Year-End Great Purification – December 31 == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Takatsuki City === * Nagai Shrine buildings (main hall, haiden) with attached items: 4 ridge plaques and 1 Takatsuki Castle ema – Designated on June 14, {{ill|2005|en|2005|WD=Q2019|lt=2005}} (Heisei 17). * Nagai Shrine Karamon – Designated on June 14, 2005 (Heisei 17). * Seventeen items of materials related to the traditional Nagai Naotoki of Nagai Shrine – Deposited at {{ill|Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|ja|高槻市立しろあと歴史館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|1=Shiroato History Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670762}}. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Catholic Takatsuki Church|ja|カトリック高槻教会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Catholic Takatsuki Church|1=Takatsuki Catholic Church|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96718046}} (adjacent) * Japan Kurarechian Association Headquarters Monastery * Maria Inmaculada Kindergarten * {{ill|Takatsuki Modern Theater|ja|高槻現代劇場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Modern Theater|1=Takatsuki Arts Theatre|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670836}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|ja|高槻市立しろあと歴史館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|1=Shiroato History Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670762}} * The ruins of {{ill|Takatsuki Castle|ja|高槻城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Castle|1=Takatsuki Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670758}}, {{ill|Castle Ruins Park|ja|城跡公園 (高槻市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Castle Ruins Park|1=Takatsuki Castle Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q111830073}}, and the {{ill|Takatsuki City Historical Folk Museum|ja|高槻市立歴史民俗資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki City Historical Folk Museum|1=Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670799}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.nomijinja.jp/ Nomijinja] (Official website) * {{Cite web|和書|year=2012 |url=http://www.city.takatsuki.osaka.jp/rekishi_kanko/rekishi/rekishikan/jidai/shaji/1327659158743.html |title=Nomijinja |work=インターネット歴史館 |publisher=Takatsuki City|accessdate=2014-12-13}} * {{Cite web|和書|year=2012 |url=http://www.city.takatsuki.osaka.jp/rekishi_kanko/rekishi/rekishikan/jidai/shaji/1327671298593.html |title=Revived "Nagai Shrine Karamon" |work=インターネット歴史館 |publisher=Takatsuki City|accessdate=2014-12-13}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|野見神社|version=54014655|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:野見神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11646127}} revision.text:
[[ja:野見神社]] [[ja:野見神社]] [[ja:野見神社]] { {{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = '''Nomijinja''' (野見神社) |image = [[File:Nomijinja01.jpg|300px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=18|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|frame-height=300|coord={{coord|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|Takatsuki|en|Takatsuki|WD=Q335423|lt=Takatsuki City}} Nomimachi 6-6 |Coordinates = {{wikicoordinates|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|Nomi no Sukune|en|Nomi no Sukune|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto}} |Founding = Late 9th century |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small), former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Annual Festival = Second Monday of October }} '''Nomijinja''' (のみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Nomimachi, {{ill|Takatsuki|en|Takatsuki|WD=Q335423|lt=Takatsuki City}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} shrine, and its former Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == History == In the late 9th century, during the mid-{{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} of {{ill|Emperor Uda|en|Emperor Uda|WD=Q349267|lt=Emperor Uda}} ({{ill|887|en|887|WD=Q25010|lt=887}} – {{ill|897|en|897|WD=Q30468|lt=897}}), an epidemic spread throughout the region. Following a divine oracle, {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozutennō}} (identified with {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}) was enshrined, and as the epidemic subsided, a shrine building was constructed, establishing the Gozutennō Shrine. In the late 10th century, when {{ill|Takatsuki Castle|ja|高槻城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Castle|1=Takatsuki Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670758}} was constructed, the shrine was venerated as a guardian of the castle.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/ |title=高槻城内守護 野見神社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} However, in the 16th century when {{ill|Justo Takayama|en|Justo Takayama|WD=Q749873|lt=Takayama Ukon}} became the lord of Takatsuki Castle, his adherence to {{ill|Christianity|en|Christianity|WD=Q5043|lt=Christianity}} led him to destroy the shrine building within the castle and confiscate its estate. Later, in the 5th year of the {{ill|Yuanhe|en|Yuanhe|WD=Q10889567|lt=Genna}} era ({{ill|1619|en|1619|WD=Q6804|lt=1619}}), the lord of Takatsuki Castle, {{ill|Matsudaira Ienobu|en|Matsudaira Ienobu|WD=Q5368670|lt=Matsudaira Ienobu}}, rebuilt the shrine building.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e5%be%a1%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92-%e8%a1%8c%e4%ba%8b.html |title=野見神社 御由緒・年間行事 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} In {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868}} (the first year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}), due to the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism}}, the name of the enshrined deity was changed to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and furthermore, {{ill|Nomi no Sukune|en|Nomi no Sukune|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto}} was enshrined alongside, prompting the shrine to be renamed “Nomijinja”. Consequently, although it is counted as one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} shrines listed in the {{ill|Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, the publication “Historic Sites, Famous Landscapes, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture” asserts that the renaming to “Nomijinja”—by enshrining Nomitatsune to emulate a Shikinaisha—meant that its descendant shrine is the auxiliary shrine {{ill|Nomigami Shrine|ja|野身神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomigami Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Kamigu Tenmangu|ja|上宮天満宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigu Tenmangu|1=Jōgū Tenmangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358789}} in Takatsuki City. In {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871}} (the 4th year of Meiji), it was classified as a prefectural shrine ({{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) as a Shikinaisha; however, in November of the following year, {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872}} (the 5th year of Meiji), it was demoted to a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) for the reasons mentioned above. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities – {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Nomi no Sukune|en|Nomi no Sukune|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto}} == Shrine Grounds == === Shrine Buildings === * Main Hall – Rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Tenna|en|Tenna|WD=Q1145289|lt=Tenwa}} ({{ill|1682|en|1682|WD=Q7648|lt=1682}}). * Haiden – Renovated in {{ill|2003|en|2003|WD=Q1986|lt=2003}} (the 15th year of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}}). * Noh Stage * Shrine Office === Auxiliary Shrines === * {{ill|Nagai Shrine|ja|永井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 野見神社 (高槻市)|1=Nomi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646130}} – Enshrined deity: the first lord of {{ill|Takatsuki Domain|en|Takatsuki Domain|WD=Q10863415|lt=Takatsuki Domain}}, {{ill|Nagai Naotoki|ja|永井直清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naotoki|1=Nagai Naokiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549807}}. Founded in the 5th year of the {{ill|Kansei|en|Kansei|WD=Q1195131|lt=Kansei}} era ({{ill|1793|en|1793|WD=Q7807|lt=1793}}) by the 9th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naotoshi|1=Nagai Naonobu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549817}}. Initially called “Naotoki Shrine”, it was referred to as “Naotoki Gongen” during the late Edo period. In the first year of the {{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei}} era ({{ill|1848|en|1848|WD=Q7647|lt=1848}}), during the time of the 11th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naoteru|ja|永井直輝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naoteru|1=Nagai Naoteru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549816}}, the main hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Takatsuki City) was restored, and the haiden (also a designated tangible cultural property) along with the karamon (quadrangular gate, designated tangible cultural property) were established. The karamon was repaired in {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=2010}} (the 22nd year of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}}). : Annual Festival: April 24{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e6%91%82%e6%9c%ab%e7%a4%be.html |title=野見神社 - 摂末社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} * {{ill|Kojima Shrine|ja|小島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いちきしまひめのみこと|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=おほなむちのみこと|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Iwanagahime|ja|磐長姫命|lt=Iwanagahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いわながひめのみこと|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanagahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191705}}, and {{ill|Ameno Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=あめのこやねのみこと|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2564508}}. Its founding date is unknown; the shrine building was newly constructed on October 1, {{ill|2021|en|2021|WD=Q49628|lt=2021}} (the 3rd year of {{ill|Reiwa era|en|Reiwa era|WD=Q62609017|lt=Reiwa}}). : Annual Festival: Second Sunday of October (Nomijinja Autumn Evening Festival) * Shishamyojin – In the {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō period}} the separate shrines of Fukujinja, Inari Shrine, Iwajinsha, and Sorei Shrine were merged into one. : Annual Festival: April 11 :: Fukujinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Shinatsuhikokami|ja|志那都比古神|lt=Shinatsuhikokami|lt_ja=しなつひこのかみ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhikokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}, {{ill|Shinatsuhime|ja|志那都姫神|lt=Shinatsuhime|lt_ja=しなつひめのかみ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Homusubi|ja|火産霊|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Homusubi|lt_ja=ほむすび|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}, {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|ja|金山比古神|lt=Kanayamahikokami|lt_ja=かなやまひこのかみ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金山彦神}}, {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|ja|金山姫神|lt=Kanayamahime|lt_ja=かなやまひめのかみ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金山彦神}}, {{ill|Haniyamahikokami|ja|埴山比古神|lt=Haniyamahikokami|lt_ja=はにやまひこのかみ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyamahikokami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (linked as {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|en|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|WD=Q9285919|lt=Haniyamas}} with display 埴山比古神), and {{ill|Mizuhanomenokami|ja|弥都波能売神|lt=Mizuhanomenokami|lt_ja=みづはのめのかみ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanomenokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}. Founded in August of the 9th year of {{ill|Kansei|en|Kansei|WD=Q1195131|lt=Kansei}} ({{ill|1797|en|1797|WD=Q7820|lt=1797}}) by {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naotoshi|1=Nagai Naonobu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549817}}. :: Inari Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukano Mitama-no-Kami}}. Founded in February of the 11th year of Kansei ({{ill|1799|en|1799|WD=Q7827|lt=1799}}) by Nagai Naotoshi. :: Iwajinsha – Enshrined deities: (i) Yachimata Hikonami, (ii) Yachimata Hime, and (iii) Kunado-no-Kami. Founded in May of the 4th year of {{ill|Genbun|en|Genbun|WD=Q1145754|lt=Genbun}} ({{ill|1739|en|1739|WD=Q7002|lt=1739}}) by {{ill|Nagai Ryunosuke|ja|永井流之助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Ryunosuke|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. :: Sorei Shrine – Enshrined are the successive chief priests, shrine priests, and parishioners who have devoted themselves to the shrine. Founded in {{ill|1878|en|1878|WD=Q7776|lt=1878}} (the 11th year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}). * {{ill|Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine|ja|高槻えびす神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu|ja|蛭子大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす}}. It was established in {{ill|1956|en|1956|WD=Q5221|lt=1956}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa}} 31) as a branch from {{ill|Nishinomiya Shrine|en|Nishinomiya Shrine|WD=Q705297|lt=Nishinomiya Shrine}}, and the current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1966|en|1966|WD=Q2649|lt=1966}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa}} 41). : Takatsuki Ebisu Festival – January 10 (subdivided into the 9th: Evening Ebisu, 10th: Main Ebisu, 11th: Remaining Fortune) * {{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine}} – Enshrined are the war dead of Takatsuki who perished in the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|en|Satsuma Rebellion|WD=Q714820|lt=Satsuma Rebellion}}, {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|en|First Sino-Japanese War|WD=Q178687|lt=First Sino-Japanese War}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|en|Russo-Japanese War|WD=Q159950|lt=Russo-Japanese War}}, {{ill|Second Sino-Japanese War|en|Second Sino-Japanese War|WD=Q170314|lt=Second Sino-Japanese War}}, and the {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}. : Annual Festival: Third Sunday of May Nomijinja02.jpg|Auxiliary Shrine Nagai Shrine [[ja:野見神社]] [[ja:野見神社]] [[ja:野見神社]] { {{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = '''Nomijinja''' (野見神社) |image = [[File:Nomijinja01.jpg|300px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=18|frame-align=center|frame-width=300|frame-height=300|coord={{coord|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E}}}} |Location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|Takatsuki|en|Takatsuki|WD=Q335423|lt=Takatsuki City}} Nomimachi 6-6 |Coordinates = {{wikicoordinates|34|50|43|N|135|37|16.5|E|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deities = {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|Nomi no Sukune|en|Nomi no Sukune|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto}} |Founding = Late 9th century |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small), former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |Annual Festival = Second Monday of October }} '''Nomijinja''' (のみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Nomimachi, {{ill|Takatsuki|en|Takatsuki|WD=Q335423|lt=Takatsuki City}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} shrine, and its former Rank was {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == History == In the late 9th century, during the mid-{{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} of {{ill|Emperor Uda|en|Emperor Uda|WD=Q349267|lt=Emperor Uda}} ({{ill|887|en|887|WD=Q25010|lt=887}} – {{ill|897|en|897|WD=Q30468|lt=897}}), an epidemic spread throughout the region. Following a divine oracle, {{ill|Gozu Tennō|en|Gozu Tennō|WD=Q11570247|lt=Gozutennō}} (identified with {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}) was enshrined, and as the epidemic subsided, a shrine building was constructed, establishing the Gozutennō Shrine. In the late 10th century, when {{ill|Takatsuki Castle|ja|高槻城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Castle|1=Takatsuki Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670758}} was constructed, the shrine was venerated as a guardian of the castle.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/ |title=高槻城内守護 野見神社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} However, in the 16th century when {{ill|Justo Takayama|en|Justo Takayama|WD=Q749873|lt=Takayama Ukon}} became the lord of Takatsuki Castle, his adherence to {{ill|Christianity|en|Christianity|WD=Q5043|lt=Christianity}} led him to destroy the shrine building within the castle and confiscate its estate. Later, in the 5th year of the {{ill|Yuanhe|en|Yuanhe|WD=Q10889567|lt=Genna}} era ({{ill|1619|en|1619|WD=Q6804|lt=1619}}), the lord of Takatsuki Castle, {{ill|Matsudaira Ienobu|en|Matsudaira Ienobu|WD=Q5368670|lt=Matsudaira Ienobu}}, rebuilt the shrine building.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e5%be%a1%e7%94%b1%e7%b7%92-%e8%a1%8c%e4%ba%8b.html |title=野見神社 御由緒・年間行事 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} In {{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868}} (the first year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}), due to the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|WD=Q1970535|lt=Separation of Shinto and Buddhism}}, the name of the enshrined deity was changed to {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, and furthermore, {{ill|Nomi no Sukune|en|Nomi no Sukune|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto}} was enshrined alongside, prompting the shrine to be renamed “Nomijinja”. Consequently, although it is counted as one of the {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} shrines listed in the {{ill|Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engi Shiki Shinmei-chō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, the publication “Historic Sites, Famous Landscapes, and Natural Monuments of Osaka Prefecture” asserts that the renaming to “Nomijinja”—by enshrining Nomitatsune to emulate a Shikinaisha—meant that its descendant shrine is the auxiliary shrine {{ill|Nomigami Shrine|ja|野身神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nomigami Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Kamigu Tenmangu|ja|上宮天満宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigu Tenmangu|1=Jōgū Tenmangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11358789}} in Takatsuki City. In {{ill|1871|en|1871|WD=Q7746|lt=1871}} (the 4th year of Meiji), it was classified as a prefectural shrine ({{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) as a Shikinaisha; however, in November of the following year, {{ill|1872|en|1872|WD=Q7753|lt=1872}} (the 5th year of Meiji), it was demoted to a village shrine ({{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}) for the reasons mentioned above. == Enshrined Deities == * Main Deities – {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}, {{ill|Nomi no Sukune|en|Nomi no Sukune|WD=Q965742|lt=Nomitatsune-no-Mikoto}} == Shrine Grounds == === Shrine Buildings === * Main Hall – Rebuilt in the 2nd year of {{ill|Tenna|en|Tenna|WD=Q1145289|lt=Tenwa}} ({{ill|1682|en|1682|WD=Q7648|lt=1682}}). * Haiden – Renovated in {{ill|2003|en|2003|WD=Q1986|lt=2003}} (the 15th year of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}}). * Noh Stage * Shrine Office === Auxiliary Shrines === * {{ill|Nagai Shrine|ja|永井神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 野見神社 (高槻市)|1=Nomi Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11646130}} – Enshrined deity: the first lord of {{ill|Takatsuki Domain|en|Takatsuki Domain|WD=Q10863415|lt=Takatsuki Domain}}, {{ill|Nagai Naotoki|ja|永井直清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naotoki|1=Nagai Naokiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549807}}. Founded in the 5th year of the {{ill|Kansei|en|Kansei|WD=Q1195131|lt=Kansei}} era ({{ill|1793|en|1793|WD=Q7807|lt=1793}}) by the 9th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naotoshi|1=Nagai Naonobu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549817}}. Initially called “Naotoki Shrine”, it was referred to as “Naotoki Gongen” during the late Edo period. In the first year of the {{ill|Kaei|en|Kaei|WD=Q1146125|lt=Kaei}} era ({{ill|1848|en|1848|WD=Q7647|lt=1848}}), during the time of the 11th lord, {{ill|Nagai Naoteru|ja|永井直輝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naoteru|1=Nagai Naoteru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549816}}, the main hall (a designated tangible cultural property of Takatsuki City) was restored, and the haiden (also a designated tangible cultural property) along with the karamon (quadrangular gate, designated tangible cultural property) were established. The karamon was repaired in {{ill|2010|en|2010|WD=Q1995|lt=2010}} (the 22nd year of {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}}). : Annual Festival: April 24{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nomijinja.jp/%e6%91%82%e6%9c%ab%e7%a4%be.html |title=野見神社 - 摂末社 |publisher=野見神社 |accessdate=2014-12-13}} * {{ill|Kojima Shrine|ja|小島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojima Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市杵島姫命|lt=Ichikishima-hime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いちきしまひめのみこと|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|lt=Ōkuninushi-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=おほなむちのみこと|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Iwanagahime|ja|磐長姫命|lt=Iwanagahime-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=いわながひめのみこと|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanagahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191705}}, and {{ill|Ameno Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|lt=Ameno Koyane-no-Mikoto|lt_ja=あめのこやねのみこと|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ameno Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2564508}}. Its founding date is unknown; the shrine building was newly constructed on October 1, {{ill|2021|en|2021|WD=Q49628|lt=2021}} (the 3rd year of {{ill|Reiwa era|en|Reiwa era|WD=Q62609017|lt=Reiwa}}). : Annual Festival: Second Sunday of October (Nomijinja Autumn Evening Festival) * Shishamyojin – In the {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō period}} the separate shrines of Fukujinja, Inari Shrine, Iwajinsha, and Sorei Shrine were merged into one. : Annual Festival: April 11 :: Fukujinja – Enshrined deities: {{ill|Shinatsuhikokami|ja|志那都比古神|lt=Shinatsuhikokami|lt_ja=しなつひこのかみ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhikokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}, {{ill|Shinatsuhime|ja|志那都姫神|lt=Shinatsuhime|lt_ja=しなつひめのかみ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinatsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Homusubi|ja|火産霊|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Homusubi|lt_ja=ほむすび|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Homusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}}, {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|ja|金山比古神|lt=Kanayamahikokami|lt_ja=かなやまひこのかみ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金山彦神}}, {{ill|Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|ja|金山姫神|lt=Kanayamahime|lt_ja=かなやまひめのかみ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金山彦神}}, {{ill|Haniyamahikokami|ja|埴山比古神|lt=Haniyamahikokami|lt_ja=はにやまひこのかみ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyamahikokami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (linked as {{ill|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|en|Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|WD=Q9285919|lt=Haniyamas}} with display 埴山比古神), and {{ill|Mizuhanomenokami|ja|弥都波能売神|lt=Mizuhanomenokami|lt_ja=みづはのめのかみ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mizuhanomenokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ミヅハノメ|1=Mizuhanome|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8191796}}. Founded in August of the 9th year of {{ill|Kansei|en|Kansei|WD=Q1195131|lt=Kansei}} ({{ill|1797|en|1797|WD=Q7820|lt=1797}}) by {{ill|Nagai Naotoshi|ja|永井直進|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Naotoshi|1=Nagai Naonobu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11549817}}. :: Inari Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ukanomitama|en|Ukanomitama|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukano Mitama-no-Kami}}. Founded in February of the 11th year of Kansei ({{ill|1799|en|1799|WD=Q7827|lt=1799}}) by Nagai Naotoshi. :: Iwajinsha – Enshrined deities: (i) Yachimata Hikonami, (ii) Yachimata Hime, and (iii) Kunado-no-Kami. Founded in May of the 4th year of {{ill|Genbun|en|Genbun|WD=Q1145754|lt=Genbun}} ({{ill|1739|en|1739|WD=Q7002|lt=1739}}) by {{ill|Nagai Ryunosuke|ja|永井流之助|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai Ryunosuke|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. :: Sorei Shrine – Enshrined are the successive chief priests, shrine priests, and parishioners who have devoted themselves to the shrine. Founded in {{ill|1878|en|1878|WD=Q7776|lt=1878}} (the 11th year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}). * {{ill|Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine|ja|高槻えびす神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Ebisu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Enshrined deity: {{ill|Ebisu|ja|蛭子大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebisu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to えびす}}. It was established in {{ill|1956|en|1956|WD=Q5221|lt=1956}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa}} 31) as a branch from {{ill|Nishinomiya Shrine|en|Nishinomiya Shrine|WD=Q705297|lt=Nishinomiya Shrine}}, and the current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1966|en|1966|WD=Q2649|lt=1966}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa}} 41). : Takatsuki Ebisu Festival – January 10 (subdivided into the 9th: Evening Ebisu, 10th: Main Ebisu, 11th: Remaining Fortune) * {{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine}} – Enshrined are the war dead of Takatsuki who perished in the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|en|Satsuma Rebellion|WD=Q714820|lt=Satsuma Rebellion}}, {{ill|First Sino-Japanese War|en|First Sino-Japanese War|WD=Q178687|lt=First Sino-Japanese War}}, {{ill|Russo-Japanese War|en|Russo-Japanese War|WD=Q159950|lt=Russo-Japanese War}}, {{ill|Second Sino-Japanese War|en|Second Sino-Japanese War|WD=Q170314|lt=Second Sino-Japanese War}}, and the {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|WD=Q184425|lt=Pacific War}}. : Annual Festival: Third Sunday of May Nomijinja02.jpg|Auxiliary Shrine Nagai Shrine Karamon Tessen.png|Nagai Shrine {{ill|Mon (emblem)|en|Mon (emblem)|WD=Q600964|lt=Family crest}} ({{ill|Takatsuki Domain|en|Takatsuki Domain|WD=Q10863415|lt=Takatsuki Domain}} lord, {{ill|Nagai family|ja|永井氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagai family|qq=}}, {{ill|Mon (emblem)|en|Mon (emblem)|WD=Q600964|lt=Family crest}}) == Festivals == * New Year’s Festival – January 1 * {{ill|Tondo Festival|ja|とんど|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tondo Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 左義長|1=Sagichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9332740}} – January 15 * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分|1=Setsubun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q746798}} – February 3 * Dedication {{ill|Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival|en|Naki Sumo Crying Baby Festival|WD=Q1461738|lt=Crying Sumo}} – April 29 * Great Purification of Summer Crossing (Chinowa Ritual) – June 30 * Autumn Grand Festival – Second Monday of October * {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai}} – November 23 * Year-End Great Purification – December 31 == Cultural Properties == === Tangible Cultural Properties Designated by Takatsuki City === * Nagai Shrine buildings (main hall, haiden) with attached items: 4 ridge plaques and 1 Takatsuki Castle ema – Designated on June 14, {{ill|2005|en|2005|WD=Q2019|lt=2005}} (Heisei 17). * Nagai Shrine Karamon – Designated on June 14, 2005 (Heisei 17). * Seventeen items of materials related to the traditional Nagai Naotoki of Nagai Shrine – Deposited at {{ill|Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|ja|高槻市立しろあと歴史館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|1=Shiroato History Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670762}}. == Surroundings == * {{ill|Catholic Takatsuki Church|ja|カトリック高槻教会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Catholic Takatsuki Church|1=Takatsuki Catholic Church|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q96718046}} (adjacent) * Japan Kurarechian Association Headquarters Monastery * Maria Inmaculada Kindergarten * {{ill|Takatsuki Modern Theater|ja|高槻現代劇場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Modern Theater|1=Takatsuki Arts Theatre|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670836}} (adjacent) * {{ill|Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|ja|高槻市立しろあと歴史館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki City Shiroato Museum|1=Shiroato History Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670762}} * The ruins of {{ill|Takatsuki Castle|ja|高槻城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki Castle|1=Takatsuki Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670758}}, {{ill|Castle Ruins Park|ja|城跡公園 (高槻市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Castle Ruins Park|1=Takatsuki Castle Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q111830073}}, and the {{ill|Takatsuki City Historical Folk Museum|ja|高槻市立歴史民俗資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuki City Historical Folk Museum|1=Museum of History and Folklore|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11670799}}. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.nomijinja.jp/ Nomijinja] (Official website) * {{Cite web|和書|year=2012 |url=http://www.city.takatsuki.osaka.jp/rekishi_kanko/rekishi/rekishikan/jidai/shaji/1327659158743.html |title=Nomijinja |work=インターネット歴史館 |publisher=Takatsuki City|accessdate=2014-12-13}} * {{Cite web|和書|year=2012 |url=http://www.city.takatsuki.osaka.jp/rekishi_kanko/rekishi/rekishikan/jidai/shaji/1327671298593.html |title=Revived "Nagai Shrine Karamon" |work=インターネット歴史館 |publisher=Takatsuki City|accessdate=2014-12-13}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated page|ja|野見神社|version=54014655|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:野見神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11646127}}
